Command: MEASUrement:MEAS<x>:RESUlts:CURRentacq:PK2PK?
Description: This query-only command returns the peak-to-peak value for the specified measurement for the current acquisition. The measurement number is specified by x.
Category: Measurement

title: Population
body:
Command: MEASUrement:MEAS<x>:RESUlts:CURRentacq:POPUlation?
Description: This query-only command returns the population for the specified measurement for the current acquisition. The measurement number is specified by x.
Category: Measurement

title: Stddev
body:
Command: MEASUrement:MEAS<x>:RESUlts:CURRentacq:STDDev?
Description: This query-only command returns the standard deviation for the specified measurement for all acquisitions accumulated since statistics were last reset. The measurement number is specified by x.
Category: Measurement

title: Maximum
body:
Command: MEASUrement:MEAS<x>:RESUlts:HISTory:MAXimum?
Description: This query-only command returns the maximum value for the specified measurement for each acquisition in the history. The measurement number is specified by x.
Category: Measurement

title: Mean
body:
Command: MEASUrement:MEAS<x>:RESUlts:HISTory:MEAN?
Description: This query-only command returns the mean value for the specified measurement for each acquisition in the history. The measurement number is specified by x.
Category: Measurement

title: Minimum
body:
Command: MEASUrement:MEAS<x>:RESUlts:HISTory:MINimum?
Description: This query-only command returns the minimum value for the specified measurement for each acquisition in the history. The measurement number is specified by x.
Category: Measurement

title: Pk2pk
body:
Command: MEASUrement:MEAS<x>:RESUlts:HISTory:PK2PK?

Category: Measurement

title: Population
body:
Command: MEASUrement:MEAS<x>:RESUlts:HISTory:POPUlation?

title: Stddev
body:
Command: MEASUrement:MEAS<x>:RESUlts:HISTory:STDDev?

title: Rfrequency

title: Rrange

title: Sequence

title: Signalfrequency

title: Signaltype

title: Smoothingfilter

title: Source

title: Srate

title: Srho

title: Status

title: Stype

title: Sunits

title: Vlevel

title: Width

title: Windowlength

Description: This command sets or queries how often reference levels are calculated on Jitter measurements. If the mode is set to Latch, ref levels are calculated only on the first acquisition after a statistics reset. If it is set to Continuous, reference levels are calculated on every acquisition.

title: Start
body:

Description: This command sets or queries the starting acquisition number for transferring measurement history results.
Category: Measurement

title: Stop
body:
Command: MEASUrement:RESUlts:HISTory:STOP
Description: This command sets or queries the last acquisition number that will be transferred for measurement history results.
Category: Measurement

title: Cyclemode
body:
Command: MEASUrement:STATIstics:CYCLEMode
Description: This command turns on and off cycle to cycle measurement statistics tracking and affects computation and display of cycle-cycle statistics in the Measurement Result table. It affects measurement statistics after being enabled and after new data is acquired and measured.
Category: Measurement

title: Ntimes
body:
Command: DIAg:LOOP:OPTion:NTIMes
Description: This command sets or queries how many loops to run, if N-times is being used.
Category: Self Test

title: Option
body:
Command: DIAg:LOOP:OPTion
Description: This command sets or queries the type of looping desired.
Category: Self Test

title: Stop
body:
Command: DIAg:LOOP:STOP
Description: Request that diagnostics stop looping.
Category: Self Test

title: Mode
body:
Command: DIAg:MODe
Description: This command sets or queries the diagnostics mode.
Category: Self Test

title: Result
body:
Command: DIAg:RESUlt?
Description: This query returns both the overall diagnostics test results and the results of each individual test area.
Category: Self Test

title: Flag
body:
Command: DIAg:RESUlt:FLAg?
Description: This query returns the status of the diagnostic test area that has been selected.
Category: Self Test

title: Log
body:
Command: DIAg:RESUlt:LOG?
Description: This query returns the test Pass/Fail status of each diagnostic area. It does not return the overall status.
Category: Self Test

title: Select
body:
Command: DIAg:SELect
Description: This command selects an available diagnostic area.
Category: Self Test

title: State
body:
Command: DIAg:STATE
Description: This command starts or aborts Self Test. Abort happens after group under test completes.
Category: Self Test

title: Calibrate
body:
Command: TOUCHSCReen:CALibrate
Description: This command launches the touch screen calibration procedure. This command is equivalent to tapping the Calibrate touch screen control in the Utility->Self Test menu.
Category: Self Test

title: Display
body:
Command: DISplay?
Description: This query-only command returns the current Display settings.
Category: Display

title: Invertcolor
body:
Command: DISplay:{CH<x>|MATH<x>|REF<x>}:INVERTColor
Description: This command sets or queries the Inverted mode color of the specified input source to the specified color. You can assign one of 48 unique colors to any channel, math, or reference waveform. These colors replace the default Inverted colors and remain in effect until you reset the colors.
Category: Display

title: Normalcolor
body:
Command: DISplay:{CH<x>|MATH<x>|REF<x>}:NORMALColor
Description: This command sets or queries the normal mode color of the specified input source to the specified color. You can assign one of 48 unique colors to any channel, math, or reference waveform. These colors replace the default normal colors and remain in effect until you reset the colors.
Category: Display

title: Colors
body:
Command: DISplay:COLors
Description: Sets or queries the color mode for the graticule and waveform display.
Category: Display

title: State
body:
Command: DISplay:GLObal:B<x>:STATE
Description: This command sets or queries the global state (display mode On or Off) of the specified bus. Setting this value true (On or NR1 ≠ 0 ) turns on the source in the waveform view. Setting this value false (Off or NR1 = 0 ) turns off the source in the waveform view. This command only works if the specified bus is added already.
Category: Display

title: State
body:
Command: DISplay:GLObal:CH<x>:STATE
Description: This command sets or queries the global state (display mode On or Off) of the specified channel. Setting this value true (On or NR1 ≠ 0 ) turns on the source in the waveform view. Setting this value false (Off or NR1 = 0 ) turns off the source in the waveform view. This command only works if the specified channel is added already.
Category: Display

title: State
body:
Command: DISplay:GLObal:MATH<x>:STATE
Description: This command sets or queries the global state (display mode On or Off) of the specified math. Setting this value true (On or NR1 ≠ 0) turns on the source in the waveform view. Setting this value false (Off or NR1 = 0) turns off the source in the waveform view. This command only works if the specified math waveform is added already.
Category: Display

title: State
body:
Command: DISplay:GLObal:PLOT<x>:STATE
Description: This command sets or queries the global state (display mode On or Off) of the specified time trend plot. Setting this value true (On or NR1
≠ 0) turns on the source in the waveform view. Setting this value false (Off or NR1 = 0) turns off the source in the waveform view. This command only works if the specified plot is added already.
Category: Display

title: State
body:
Command: DISplay:GLObal:REF<x>:STATE
Description: This command sets or queries the global state (display mode On or Off) of the specified reference waveform. Setting this value true (On or NR1 ≠ 0) turns on the source in the waveform view. Setting this value false (Off or NR1 = 0) turns off the source in the waveform view. This command only works if the specified reference waveform is added already.
Category: Display

title: Intensity
body:
Command: DISplay:INTENSITy?
Description: This query-only command returns the waveform saturation level and screen saver settings.
Category: Display

title: Backlight
body:
Command: DISplay:INTENSITy:BACKLight
Description: This command sets or queries the display backlight intensity setting.
Category: Display

title: Enable
body:
Command: DISplay:INTENSITy:BACKLight:AUTODim:ENAble
Description: Sets or queries the state of the display auto-dim feature. By default it is enabled. Once the backlight has dimmed, any button push, knob turn or mouse movement returns the backlight value to the value set by :DISplay:INTENSITy:BACKLight.
Category: Display

title: Time
body:
Command: DISplay:INTENSITy:BACKLight:AUTODim:TIMe
Description: Sets or queries the amount of time, in minutes, to wait for no user interface activity before automatically dimming the display. The time can range from a minimum of 10 minutes to a maximum of 1440 minutes (24 hours). The default is 10 minutes.
Category: Display

title: Autoscale
body:
Command: DISplay:MATHFFTView<x>:AUTOScale
Description: This command sets or returns the enabled state of autoscale for Math/FFT waveforms.
Category: Display

title: Asource
body:
Command: DISplay:MATHFFTView<x>:CURSor:ASOUrce?
Description: This command queries the Math-FFT waveform view source for cursor A.
Category: Display

title: Bsource
body:
Command: DISplay:MATHFFTView<x>:CURSor:BSOUrce?
Description: This command queries the Math-FFT waveform view source for cursor B.
Category: Display

title: Ddt
body:
Command: DISplay:MATHFFTView<x>:CURSor:DDT?
Description: This command queries the delta Y over delta X (ΔY/Δ X) cursor readout value of the specified cursor in the specified Math-FFT view.
Category: Display

title: Function
body:
Command: DISplay:MATHFFTView<x>:CURSor:FUNCtion
Description: This command sets or queries the cursor type for the specified Math-FFT view.
Category: Display

title: Aposition
body:
Command: DISplay:MATHFFTView<x>:CURSor:HBArs:APOSition
Description: This command sets or returns the position of horizontal cursor A for the specified Math-FFT view.
Category: Display

title: Aunits
body:
Command: DISplay:MATHFFTView<x>:CURSor:HBArs:AUNIts?
Description: This command queries the vertical units of horizontal cursor A for the specified Math-FFT view.
Category: Display

title: Bposition
body:
Command: DISplay:MATHFFTView<x>:CURSor:HBArs:BPOSition
Description: This command sets or returns the position of horizontal cursor B for the specified Math-FFT view.
Category: Display

title: Bunits
body:
Command: DISplay:MATHFFTView<x>:CURSor:HBArs:BUNIts?
Description: This command queries the vertical units of horizontal cursor B for the specified Math-FFT view.
Category: Display

title: Position
body:
Command: DISplay:WAVEView<x>:ZOOM:ZOOM<x>:HORizontal:POSition
Description: Sets or queries the horizontal zoom position (of the specified zoom in the specified Waveform View) of the zoomed waveform or zoom waveform in the display, around which the zoom waveform displays. It is freely movable around the acquisition settings (horizontal span). An acquired waveform or reference could extend off screen. The valid zoom area does not care about the waveform itself, only the user setting for acquisition.
For example, if horizontal scale is set to 1 second, position to 50, then the acquisition area will go from -5 s to +5 s. Zoom window 0 will focus on -5 s and zoom area 100 will focus on +5 s. If the instrument is stopped and the scale
changed to 0.5 s, there will be data off the ends of the display. However, 0% zoom will put the user focus on -2.5 s, the lower bound of the acquisition span.
Category: Zoom

title: Scale

title: Position
body:
Command: DISplay:WAVEView<x>:ZOOM:ZOOM<x>:VERTical:POSition
Description: This command sets or queries the vertical position of the specified zoom in the specified Waveform View. It is freely movable within the confines of the acquired waveform. It is measured from the top to bottom of the acquisition window.
The top of the zoom window is -5 * vertical zoom factor. The bottom of the zoom window is +5 * the vertical zoom factor. For a zoom of 5x, the position ranges from -25 to 25.
Category: Zoom

title: Define
body:
Command: MATH:MATH<x>:DEFine
Description: This command allows you to define new waveforms using mathematical expressions. The query form of this command returns the math definition for the specified math waveform. The math waveform is specified by x.
You can specify a math expression from waveforms, measurements and scalar sources, functions, operands, and numerical constants.
Math expressions can be simple, such as Ch1, which specifies that a waveform should show the signal source of Channel 1 with no mathematical computation. Math expressions can also be complex, consisting of 100 plus characters and comprising many sources (including other math waveforms), functions, and operands. As an example, you can enter the expression Log(Ch1+Ch2), which specifies that the signals from channels 1 and 2 are to be algebraically added, and the base 10 log of the sum is to be shown as the final math waveform.
Category: Math

Category: Math

title: Value
body:
Command: MATH:MATH<x>:SPECTral:SUPPress:VALue
Description: This command sets or queries in volts the value of suppression threshold of the specified math waveform. This requires the Spectral type to be Phase and the Suppression to be enabled for this PI command to have any affect. The math waveform is specified by x.

title: Type

title: Unwrap

title: Degrees
body:
Command: MATH:MATH<x>:SPECTral:UNWRap:DEGrees
Description: This command sets or queries how many degrees adjacent phase values can jump before being unwrapped. This requires the Spectral type to be Phase and the UNWRAP to be enabled for this PI command to have any affect. The math waveform is specified by x.
Category: Math

title: Window
body:
Command: MATH:MATH<x>:SPECTral:WINdow
Description: This command sets or queries the window function used to apply the specified FFT window to the input data for the specified math waveform. The Math waveform is specified by x. A spectral window determines what the filter shape of the spectral analyzer will be in the frequency domain. It can be described by a mathematical function that is multiplied point-by-point times the input data to the spectral analyzer.
Following is a list of arguments that specify the window function used to multiply the input data. The windows are listed in the order of their ability to resolve frequencies (resolution bandwidth).
Category: Math

title: Factory
body:
Command: FACtory
Description: This command (no query form) resets the instrument to its factory default settings. This command is equivalent to pressing the DEFAULT SETUP button located on the instrument front panel or selecting Default Setup from the File menu.
This command Performs the following in addition to what is done for the *RST command:
Clears any pending OPC operations.
Resets the following IEEE488.2 registers:
*ESE 0 (Event Status Enable Register)
*SRE 0 (Service Request Enable Register)
DESE 255 (Device Event Status Enable Register)
*PSC 1 (Power-on Status Clear Flag)
Deletes all defined aliases.
Enables command headers (:HEADer 1).
Category: Save and Recall

title: Mask
body:
Command: SAVe:MASK
Description: Saves the given Waveview Mask to the specified file. Use the format [<path>]"<filename><.ext>" for the argument. Specifying a path is optional. If no path is entered, the file is saved to the current working directory set by FILESystem:CWD.
<path> ues the form "<drive>/<dir>.../". You can specify a relative path or a complete path:
Relative path specification: If the file argument begins with "." or ".." or has a file path separator appearing anywhere other than the first character position, then the file name is treated as a path that is relative to the current working directory.
Complete path specification: If the file argument begins with a file path separator (forward slash character) or a drive designator (such as C:), then the file name is interpreted as a full path from the specified drive.
<filename> sets the file name to use to create the file. A file can have up to 125 characters.
<.ext> sets the file format for saving the mask data. Segment-based masks must be saved with a .xml extension, while tolerance masks must be saved with a .tol extension.
Category: Save and Recall

title: Plotdata
body:
Command: SAVe:PLOTData
Description: Saves the plot data of the currently selected plot to a specified file. Supported file format is CSV.
When specifying the file name with this command, use the correct file extension (.CSV). If a file name or path is specified, the file is expected to be located in a directory relative to the current working directory (specified by FILESystem:CWD) unless a complete path is specified.
If the file argument begins with a drive designator (such as C:), then the file name is interpreted as a full path. If the file argument begins with "." or "", or has a file path separator appearing anywhere other than the first character position, then the file name is treated as a path that is relative to the current working directory.
To export an eye diagram plot data to a .csv file, the prerequisite command is MEASUrement:ADDMEAS TIE
Category: Save and Recall

title: Filesystem
body:
Command: FILESystem?
Description: This query-only command returns the directory listing of the current working directory. This query is the same as the FILESystem:DIR? query.
Category: File System

title: Copy
body:
Command: FILESystem:COPy
Description: This command (no query form) copies a named file to a new file. The new file might be in a totally separate directory than the old file. You can only copy one file at a time using this command. Wild card characters are not allowed.
Category: File System

title: Cwd
body:
Command: FILESystem:CWD
Description: This command sets or queries the current working directory. CWD is short for Current Working Directory. It changes the directory (folder) that the other FILESystem commands operate on.
Category: File System

title: Delete
body:
Command: FILESystem:DELEte
Description: This command (no query form) deletes a named file or directory from a mass storage device. Once removed, the data in that file or directory can no longer be accessed. If the specified file is a directory, it must be empty before it can be deleted.
Category: File System

title: Dir
body:
Command: FILESystem:DIR?
Description: This query-only command returns a comma separated list of quoted strings. Each string contains the name of a file or directory in the folder referred to by the FILESYSTEM:CWDFILESystem:CWD command.
Category: File System

title: Homedir
body:
Command: FILESystem:HOMEDir?
Description: This query returns the current user's home directory.
Category: File System

title: Ldir
body:
Command: FILESystem:LDIR?
Description: Returns a comma separated list of every file, file size, type, modification date and time, and directory in the folder referred to by the FILESYSTEM:CWDFILESystem:CWD command. This is different than the :DIR query in that it provides a long output format with the file size, type, and modification date/time. Each entry is a semicolon separated list: < file name>;<type>;<size in bytes>;<date>;<time>
Category: File System

title: Mkdir
body:
Command: FILESystem:MKDir
Description: This command (no query form) creates a new directory.
Category: File System

title: Drive
body:
Command: FILESystem:MOUNT:DRIVE
Description: The command form mounts a network drive specified by the quoted string argument. The quoted string argument is a semicolon separated list of the following fields:
Drive name - The drive name to be mounted. It is a case insensitive single letter followed by a colon. The drive name must be a letter between 'L:' and 'Z:', inclusive.
Server identity - The server identity is the DNS name of the server or the IP address of the server.
Path - The path to be mounted (e.g. /level1/level2/mydirectory). User name - The user name for the drive.
User password - The password for the drive.
Domain name - The domain/workgroup of the target mount.
Verbose - The verbose option to capture mount failure messages.
Domain name, user name, user password, and verbose are optional and are only used for mounts requiring SMB/CIFS interworking (MS Windows and MacOS).
The query form returns a 0 or 1 to indicate that the drive name (quoted string) is currently mounted or not. A return of 1 indicates the drive is mounted. A return of 0 indicated the drive is not mounted.
Category: File System

title: Tekdrive
body:
Command: FILESystem:MOUNT:TEKDrive
Description: This command mounts the TekDrive specified by the quoted string arguments. The argument must contain the drive name, AutoDisconnectMode, RestrictToCurrentIP, and AutoDisconnectTime.
The drive name is the TekDrive name to be mounted. It is case insensitive. There are three options available for AutoDisconnectMode:
Select Power Cycle to unmount the TekDrive after power cycling the oscilloscope. There is no time restriction when this option is selected.
Select Never to mount the TekDrive connection permanently.
Select Custom to disconnect the TekDrive after a chosen duration. The default selection is Power Cycle. RestrictToCurrentIP restricts the connection to current network IP only. This may be used for additional network security.
AutoDisconnectTime is the required time for the Auto Disconnect. The TekDrive gets disconnected automatically from the instrument after the specified time. The duration is in hours. The minimum is 0.25 hours and the maximum is 744 hours.
The query form of this command returns whether or not the specified TekDrive is mounted.
Category: File System

title: Readfile
body:
Command: FILESystem:READFile
Description: This command writes the contents of the specified file to the current interface. If the specified file does not exist or is not readable, an appropriate error event is posted.
Category: File System

title: Rename
body:
Command: FILESystem:REName
Description: This command (no query form) assigns a new name to an existing file or folder.
Category: File System

title: Rmdir
body:
Command: FILESystem:RMDir
Description: This command (no query form) deletes a named directory. The directory must be empty.
Category: File System

title: Code
body:
Command: FILESystem:TEKDrive:CODE?
Description: This command returns short code in string format. This code must be entered (or pasted) at drive.tekcloud.com/activate. After the code is entered click the Activate button to complete the mounting of the TekDrive.
Category: File System

title: Expirytime
body:
Command: FILESystem:TEKDrive:CODE:EXPirytime?
Description: This command returns expiry time of short code. It is the absolute time that the expiry command returns. For example, if 2:11 pm is the time the user initiated the TekDrive mounting, then the expiry query returns + 5 minutes (2:16 pm).
Category: File System

title: Status
body:
Command: FILESystem:TEKDrive:CODE:STATus?
Description: This command returns status of short code.
Category: File System

title: Drive
body:
Command: FILESystem:UNMOUNT:DRIve
Description: This command unmounts the USB drive specified by the quoted string argument.
Category: File System

title: Tekdrive
body:
Command: FILESystem:UNMOUNT:TEKDrive
Description: This command unmounts the TekDrive specified by the quoted string argument and the drive name is case insensitive.
Category: File System

title: Writefile
body:
Command: FILESystem:WRITEFile
Description: This command (no query form) writes the specified block data to the specified file on the instruments file system. If the destination file cannot be written, an error event is posted.
Category: File System

title: Addnew
body:
Command: HISTogram:ADDNew
Description: This command adds the specified waveform histogram.
Category: Histogram

title: Delete
body:
Command: HISTogram:DELete
Description: This command deletes the specified histogram.
Category: Histogram

title: Deleteall
body:
Command: HISTogram:DELETEALL
Description: This command deletes all the active instances of histograms defined in the scope application.
Category: Histogram

title: Box
body:
Command: HISTogram:HISTogram<x>:BOX
Description: This command sets or queries the top, left, bottom and right positions of the histogram box in source waveform coordinates. The argument is of the form <top>,<left>,<bottom>,<right>.
Category: Histogram

title: Bstate
body:

title: Data
body:

Category: Histogram

title: Results
body:
Command: HISTogram:HISTogram<x>:MEASurement:RESUlts?
Description: This query only command returns the measurement results from the specified histogram. The argument must be in the form of {argument 1},{argument 2},{argument 3}. Argument 1 specifies which histogram measurement to return results for. Argument 2 specifies which acquisitions to return measurement results for. Argument 3 specifies which statistic to return measurement results for.
Category: Histogram

title: Size
body:
Command: HISTogram:HISTogram<x>:SIZe
Description: This command sets or queries the height or width of the specified histogram bins in divisions. This size can be larger than the box, which allows you to view the histogram bins easily.

title: Source
body:

export the eye diagram plot data.

Description: This command sets or queries the measurement type of the specified power measurement number. If the measurement number does not exist, this command creates a new power measurement, assigns the specified measurement number to the new measurement, and then assigns the measurement type to the new measurement.
Category: Power

title: Dest
body:
Command: SAVEON:FILE:DEST
Description: This command sets or queries the location where files are saved when SAVEON:TRIGGER is ON and SAVEON:WAVEFORM is ON. You can save the files to a local drive or network path by entering the desired location in <QString>. You can also select to save the files to a USB drive.
Category: Save on

title: Name
body:
Command: SAVEON:FILE:NAME
Description: Sets or queries the file name to use when SAVEON:TRIGer is ON.
Category: Save on

title: Image
body:
Command: SAVEON:IMAGe
Description: This command sets or queries whether to save a screen capture when a trigger occurs and SAVEON:TRIGer is ON and
SAVEON:IMAGE is ON.
Category: Save on

title: Fileformat
body:
Command: SAVEON:IMAGe:FILEFormat
Description: This command sets or queries the file format to be used for saved image files when :SAVEON:IMAGe is set to 1.
Category: Save on

title: Trigger
body:
Command: SAVEON:TRIGger
Description: Sets or queries whether to save a file when a trigger occurs. You can define the trigger using Trigger commands or the instrument user interface.
This command is longer necessary. Please see Act On Event commands for future development.
The trigger will cause the instrument to save an image or a waveform to a file, depending on what you specified. For example, if you have set SAVEON:IMAGe to On, and a trigger event occurs, the instrument will save a screen capture.
You can set options for file storage (such as file name, file destination, and auto increment), using the SAVEON:FILE commands. Use the instrument interface to select whether to save one or more analog channels, digital channels, or math waveforms
Analog and math waveforms are saved using one file per waveform. Digital waveforms are all saved to a single file.
Category: Save on

title: Waveform
body:
Command: SAVEON:WAVEform
Description: Sets or queries whether to save a waveform when a trigger occurs when SAVEON:TRIGger is ON.
The waveform will be saved to the file you selected with SAVEON:FILE:NAME, in the location that you selected using SAVEON:FILE:DEST. You can set options for file storage (such as file name, file destination, and autoincrement), using the SAVEON:FILE commands.
Category: Save on

title: Fileformat
body:
Command: SAVEON:WAVEform:FILEFormat
Description: This command sets or queries the file format for saving waveforms when :SAVEON:WAVEform is set to 1.
Category: Save on

title: Source
body:
Command: SAVEON:WAVEform:SOURce
Description: This command sets or queries the sources for saving waveforms when SAVEON:TRIGger is ON.
Category: Save on

title: Addnew
body:
Command: SEARCH:ADDNew
Description: This command adds the specified search.
Category: Search and Mark

title: Delete
body:
Command: SEARCH:DELete
Description: This command deletes the specified search.
Category: Search and Mark

title: Deleteall
body:
Command: SEARCH:DELETEALL
Description: This command deletes all the active instances of search definitions defined in the scope application.
Category: Search and Mark

title: List
body:
Command: SEARCH:LIST?
Description: This command returns a comma separated list of all currently defined searches.
Category: Search and Mark

title: Copy
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:COPy
Description: This command (no query form) copies the search criteria to or from the trigger. The search number is specified by x.
Category: Search and Mark

title: Navigate
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:NAVigate
Description: This command sets the navigation action for search marks. The NONE action is the default setting when no action is being taken. The search number is specified by x.
Category: Search and Mark

title: Total
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TOTAL?
Description: This query-only command returns the total number of found search marks for this search. The search number is specified by x.
Category: Search and Mark

title: Condition
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:ARINC429A:CONDition
Description: This command specifies a field or condition for an ARINC429 bus to search on. The search number is specified by x. The type of error searched for is specified by SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:ARINC429A:ERRTYPe.
Category: Search and Mark

title: Hivalue
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:ARINC429A:DATa:HIVALue
Description: This command sets or queries the high value when searching on an ARINC429 data field. The search number is specified by x. The search condition must be set to DATa or LABELANDDATA, and the data qualifier must be INrange or OUTrange.
Category: Search and Mark

title: Qualifier
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:ARINC429A:DATa:QUALifier
Description: This command sets or queries the qualifier to be used when searching on data in the DATA field for an ARINC429 bus signal. The search number is specified by x. The search condition must be set to DATa or LABELANDDATA. The search qualifier only applies to the bits defined as the data field via the bus data field format specifier (using BUS:B<x>:ARINC429A:DATAFORmat).
Category: Search and Mark

title: Value
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:ARINC429A:DATa:VALue
Description: This command sets or queries the low value when searching on an ARINC429 data field. The search number is specified by x. The search condition must be set to DATa or LABELANDDATA.
Category: Search and Mark

title: Errtype
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:ARINC429A:ERRTYPe
Description: This command sets or queries the error type when searching on an ARINC429 bus signal. The search number is specified by x. The search condition must be set to ERRor.
Category: Search and Mark

title: Hivalue
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:ARINC429A:LABel:HIVALue
Description: This command sets or queries the high value when searching on an ARINC429 label field. The search number is specified by x. The search condition must be set to LABel, and the label qualifier must be INrange or OUTrange.
Category: Search and Mark

title: Qualifier
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:ARINC429A:LABel:QUALifier
Description: This command sets or queries the qualifier to be used when searching on label data for an ARINC429 bus signal. The search number is specified by x. The search condition must be set to LABel or LABELANDDATA. If the search condition is set to LABELANDDATA, the label qualifier will be locked to Equal until the search condition is changed again.
Category: Search and Mark

title: Value
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:ARINC429A:LABel:VALue
Description: This command sets or queries the low value when searching on an ARINC429 label field. The search number is specified by x. The search condition must be set to LABel or LABELANDDATA.
Category: Search and Mark

title: Value
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:ARINC429A:SDI:VALue
Description: This command sets or queries the label when searching on an ARINC429 SDI field. The search number is specified by x. The search condition must be set to DATa or LABELANDDATA, and the data format must be set to DATA.
Category: Search and Mark

title: Value
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:ARINC429A:SSM:VALue
Description: This command sets or queries the label when searching on an ARINC429 SSM field. The search number is specified by x. The search condition must be set to DATa or LABELANDDATA, and the data format must be set to DATA or SDIDATA.
Category: Search and Mark

title: Condition
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:AUDio:CONDition
Description: This command sets or queries the condition (word select / frame sync, or matching data) to be used when searching on an audio bus signal. The search number is specified by x.
Category: Search and Mark

title: Hitdmvalue
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:AUDio:DATa:HITDMVALue
Description: This command sets or queries the binary data string for the high data word to be used when searching on an TDM audio bus signal. The search condition must be set to DATa using SEARCH:SEARCH{x}:TRIGger:A:BUS:AUDio:CONDition.
Category: Search and Mark

title: Hivalue
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:AUDio:DATa:HIVALue
Description: This command sets or queries the binary data string for the high data word to be used when searching on an audio bus signal. The search condition must be set to DATa using :SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:AUDio:CONDition. The search number is specified by x.
Category: Search and Mark

title: Offset
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:AUDio:DATa:OFFSet
Description: This command sets or queries the data offset value (TDM channel) to be used when searching on a TDM type audio bus signal.
The search condition must be set to DATa using SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:AUDio:TDM:CONDition. The search number is specified by x.
Category: Search and Mark

title: Qualifier
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:AUDio:DATa:QUALifier
Description: This command sets or queries the qualifier to be used when searching on an audio bus signal. The search condition must be set to DATa using SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:AUDio:{NONTdm|TDM}:CONDition. The search number is specified by x.
Category: Search and Mark

title: Tdmvalue
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:AUDio:DATa:TDMVALue
Description: This command sets or queries the binary data string for the single or low data word to be used when searching on an TDM audio bus signal. The search condition must be set to DATA using SEARCH:SEARCH{x}:TRIGger:A:BUS:AUDio:CONDition.
Category: Search and Mark

title: Value
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:AUDio:DATa:VALue
Description: This command sets or queries the binary data string for the single or low data word to be used when searching on an audio
bus signal. The search condition must be set to DATA using SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:AUDio:{NONTdm| TDM}:CONDition. The search number is specified by x.
Category: Search and Mark

title: Word
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:AUDio:DATa:WORD
Description: This command sets or queries the alignment of the data (left, right or either) to be used when searching on a non-TDM type audio bus signal. The search condition must be set to DATA using
SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:AUDio:NONTdm:CONDition. The search number is specified by x.
Category: Search and Mark

title: Condition
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:AUTOETHERnet:CONDition
Description: This command sets or queries the field or condition for which to search an AutoEthernet.
Category: Search and Mark

title: Hivalue
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:AUTOETHERnet:DATa:HIVALue
Description: This command sets or queries the binary data value to be used when searching on an AutoEthernet bus signal. The search condition must be set to DATA and the data qualifier to inside or outside range.
Category: Search and Mark

title: Offset
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:AUTOETHERnet:DATa:OFFSet
Description: This command sets or queries the data offset value, in bytes, to use when searching on the AutoEthernet data field. The search condition needs to be set to DATa.
Category: Search and Mark

title: Size
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:AUTOETHERnet:DATa:SIZe
Description: This command sets or queries the length of the data string, in bytes, to use when searching on the AutoEthernet bus signal. The search condition needs to be set to DATa.
Category: Search and Mark

title: Value
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:AUTOETHERnet:DATa:VALue
Description: This command sets or queries the binary value to use when searching on the AutoEthernet bus signal. The search condition needs to be set to DATa.
Category: Search and Mark

title: Header
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:AUTOETHERnet:ETHer:HEADer
Description: This command sets the Header Type to be used when triggering for ether type on an Auto Ethernet bus signal when speed is selected as 10 Base-T1S and packet view is ON.
Category: Search and Mark

title: Type
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:AUTOETHERnet:ETHer:TYPe
Description: This command sets the Ether Type to be used when triggering on an Auto Ethernet bus signal when speed is selected as 10 Base-T1S and packet view is ON.
Category: Search and Mark

title: Errortype
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:AUTOETHERnet:ERRORTYPe
Description: This command sets the Error Type to be used when triggering on an Auto Ethernet bus signal when speed is selected as 10 Base-T1S and packet view is ON.
Category: Search and Mark

title: Size
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:AUTOETHERnet:PAYLoad:SIZe
Description: This command sets the length of the data string, in bytes, to be used when triggering on an Auto Ethernet bus signal when speed is 10 Base-T1S and packet view is ON.
Category: Search and Mark

title: Value
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:AUTOETHERnet:PAYLoad:VALue
Description: This command sets payload value to be used when triggering on an Auto Ethernet bus signal when speed is 10 Base-T1S and packet view is ON.
Category: Search and Mark

title: Value
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:AUTOETHERnet:QTAG:VALue
Description: This command sets or queries the binary Q-tag information to use when searching on an AutoEthernet bus signal. The search condition needs to be set to QTAG.
Category: Search and Mark

title: Qualifier
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:AUTOETHERnet:QUALifier
Description: This command sets or queries the qualifier to be used when searching on an AutoEthernet bus signal. The search condition must be set to Client Data or MAC Length/Type.
Category: Search and Mark

title: Type
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:CAN:ADS:TYPe
Description: This command sets or queries the trigger condition for a CAN bus on the ADS bits. The search number is specified by x.
Category: Search and Mark

title: Condition
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:CAN:CONDition
Description: This command sets or queries the search condition for a CAN bus. The search number is specified by x.
Category: Search and Mark

title: Crctype
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:CAN:CRCType
Description: This command specifies the CAN XL trigger type to be valid on PCRC and FCRC. The search number is specified by x.
Category: Search and Mark

title: Type
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:CAN:DAS:TYPe
Description: This command sets or queries the trigger condition for a CAN bus on the DAS bits. The search number is specified by x.
Category: Search and Mark

title: Direction
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:CAN:DATa:DIRection
Description: This command specifies the CAN search type to be valid on a Read, Write, or Either condition. The search number is specified by x.
Category: Search and Mark

title: Offset
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:CAN:DATa:OFFSet
Description: This command sets or queries the data offset value, in bytes, to use when searching on the CAN data field. The search number is specified by x. The search condition must be set to DATA or IDANDDATA.
Category: Search and Mark

title: Qualifier
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:CAN:DATa:QUALifier
Description: This command sets or queries the CAN bus trigger data qualifier to be used when searching on a CAN bus signal. The search number is specified by x.
Category: Search and Mark

title: Size
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:CAN:DATa:SIZe
Description: This command sets or queries the length of the data string, in bytes, to be used when searching on a CAN bus signal. The search condition must be set to IDANDDATA or DATA. The search number is specified by x.
Category: Search and Mark

title: Value
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:CAN:DATa:VALue
Description: This command sets or queries the binary data value to be used when searching on a CAN bus signal. The search condition must be set to IDANDDATA OR DATA.
Category: Search and Mark

title: Xlbittype
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:CAN:DATa:XLBITType
Description: This command sets or queries the trigger condition for a CAN bus on the XL Data bits. The search number is specified by x.
Category: Search and Mark

title: Errtype
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:CAN:ERRType
Description: This command sets or queries the type of error condition for a CAN bus to search on. The search number is specified by x. The search condition must be set to ERRor.
Category: Search and Mark

title: Brsbit
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:CAN:FD:BRSBit
Description: This command sets or queries the value of the bit rate switch bit (BRS bit) for a CAN bus to search on. The search number is specified by
x. The search condition must be set to FDBITS, and the CAN standard must be FDISO or FDNONISO.
Category: Search and Mark

title: Esibit
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:CAN:FD:ESIBit
Description: This command sets or queries the value of the error state indicator bit (ESI bit) for a CAN bus to search on. The search number is specified by x. The search condition must be set to FDBITS, and the CAN standard must be FDISO or FDNONISO.
Category: Search and Mark

title: Frametype
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:CAN:FRAMEtype
Description: This command sets or queries CAN bus trigger frame type to be used when searching on a CAN bus signal. The search condition must be set to FRAMEtype. The search number is specified by x.

title: Hivalue
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:USB:ADDress:HIVALue
Description: This command sets or queries the high binary address value used when searching on a USB bus signal to determine where to place a mark. The search number is specified by x. The search condition must be set to TOKEN.
The VALue and HIVALue set a range that the INrange and OUTrange qualifiers use to decide when to trigger. For example, if the QUALIFER is set to INrange, and the address is within the range set by VALue and HIVALue, then a trigger can be generated.
Category: Search and Mark
