title: PREset
body:
Command: ERRORDetector:PREset
Description: This command sets or queries error detector font preset selection. A number of preset setups are selected by this parameter to cover the more common cases. The preset names attempt to indicate the standard, signal pattern, and test type employed. The bit rate appropriate for the standard is used. The text files containing the preset setups are located in the C:\Users\Public\Tektronix\TekScope\ErrorDetector directory on WIN7. You may select CUSTOM as a preset value, and save or recall your own custom setups. You may want to recall one of the standard preset setups, modify some of the parameters, and then save it as a custom setup for recall at a later time. This same behavior is supported on the error detector User Interface. The SATA3_FRAME preset expects the SATA3 Compliance Pattern. USB3_SYMBOL preset expects the USB3 standard CP0_SKP signal. You can set a PATTERNNAME for each setup.
Category: Error Detector

title: APPLY
body:
Command: ERRORDetector:PREset:APPLY
Description: This command causes selected preset setup to be applied. Until this command is received by the instrument, the selected preset has not been applied. This mimics the user interface operation, which allows window shopping various preset setups, without actually applying them to the instrument setup.
Category: Error Detector

title: SAVEIMAGE
body:
Command: ERRORDetector:SAVEIMAGE
Description: Sets or queries error detector Save Image control. When set to ON, a screen shot will be made when the error detector detects an error (because detecting an error triggers the scope). The images are saved in the C:\Users\<yourName> \Tektronix\TekScope\SaveOnTrigger directory. A default limit of 10 screen shots prevents overflowing your disk drive should the error detector sense massive errors, such as when you disconnect the signal. If you also set the SendEmail parameter to ON, the saved image (screen shot) will be emailed to the recipient (set elsewhere in the trigger PI). SaveImage is an alternate way of setting the Save on Trigger actions defined elsewhere in the PI.
Category: Error Detector

title: SAVEWFM
body:
Command: ERRORDetector:SAVEWFM
Description: This command sets or queries error detector Save Waveform (WFM) control. When set to ON, a waveform object will be made when the error detector detects an error (because detecting an error triggers the instrument). The waveforms are saved in the C:\Users\<yourName>\Tektronix\TekScope\SaveOnTrigger directory. A default limit of 10 screen shots prevents overflowing your disk drive should the error detector sense massive errors, such as when you disconnect the signal. If you also set the SendEmail parameter to ON, the saved waveform (wfm object) is emailed to the recipient (set elsewhere in the trigger PI) as an attachedment. SaveImage is an alternate way of setting the Save on Trigger actions defined elsewhere in the PI.
Category: Error Detector

title: SCRAMBLED
body:
Command: ERRORDetector:SCRAMBLED
Description: This command sets or queries the status of the error detection data scrambling option.
Category: Error Detector

title: SENDEMAIL
body:
Command: ERRORDetector:SENDEMAIL
Description: This command sets or queries error detector Send Email control. When set to ON, a email will be sent to the recipient, defined elsewhere in the PI, when the error detector detects an error (because detecting an error triggers the instrument). The default number of emails sent is 1, so that you do not overflow your inbox. If you also set the SaveImage or SaveWfm parameters to ON, the email will contain these items. Send Email is an alternate way of setting the E-mail on Trigger actions defined elsewhere in the PI.
Category: Error Detector

title: SIGnaltype
body:
Command: ERRORDetector:SIGnaltype
Description: This command sets or queries error detector Signal Type control. Setting the signal type establishes the bit rate appropriate for the standard, as well as establishing the testing algorithm. Custom bit rates may be used as well. See the ERRORDetector:BITRATE and ERRORDetector:BITRATE:VALue commands.
Category: Error Detector

title: MINus
body:
Command: ERRORDetector:SKIPSETPRIMitive:MINus<x>?
Description: Queries the RD- skip order set array of bit string values. The PI array elements may be accessed individual via MINUS1, MINUS2, MINUS3.
Category: Error Detector

title: PLUS
body:
Command: ERRORDetector:SKIPSETPRIMitive:PLUS<x>?
Description: Queries the skip order RD+ array of bit string values. The PI array elements may be accessed individual via PLUS1, PLUS2, PLUS3, and PLUS4.
Category: Error Detector

title: STATE
body:
Command: ERRORDetector:SKIPSETPRIMitive:STATE
Description: This command sets or queries the state (ON/OFF) of the skip order set primitive.
Category: Error Detector

title: SYMBOLS
body:
Command: ERRORDetector:SKIPSETPRIMitive:SYMBOLS
Description: Sets or queries the skip order set values in symbolic form. You can set one or more symbols with the command, but they must be done in order. The query returns all four symbols. A symbol may be set or queried as “X” to indicate “don’t care.”
Category: Error Detector

title: SYMBOL
body:
Command: ERRORDetector:SKIPSETPRIMitive:SYMBOL<x>
Description: Sets or queries the skip order set symbolic characters. The PI array syntax may be used to access the individual elements (SYMBOL1, SYMBOL2, etc.). For example, a skip set symbolic value may be "K28.5," or "X" to indicate "don't care."
Category: Error Detector

title: SSC
body:
Command: ERRORDetector:SSC
Description: This command sets or queries the status of the spread spectrum clock tracking option.
Category: Error Detector

title: STANdard
body:
Command: ERRORDetector:STANdard
Description: This command sets or queries the standard selection for error testing.
Category: Error Detector

title: STATE
body:
Command: ERRORDetector:STATE
Description: This command sets or queries the status of the error option. STATE must be ON to use the error detector feature.
Category: Error Detector

title: STATus
body:
Command: ERRORDetector:STATus?
Description: Queries only the “most significant” or “summary” status of the error detector. All of the status flags for each test type may be obtained from the ERRORdetector:<TESTTYPE>:TEST:STATUS commands. LOCK refers to the recovered clock. Signal refers to the cable carrying the signal to the scope. SYNC refers to bit error tests that require a sync pattern. STOPPED/COUNTING refer to whether the error detector is testing for errors. MAX_AP refers to whether the error detector has detected the maximum consecutive Align (or SkipSets) Primitives as specified in the ERRORDetector:<TESTTYPE>MAXALIGNS command
Category: Error Detector

title: STOPWHEN
body:
Command: ERRORDetector:STOPWHEN
Description: This command sets or queries the stopping condition. The test can be stopped when the count, time, or number of errors elapses. If the STOPWHEN value is MANUAL, the test runs until a TEST STOP command is received.
Category: Error Detector

title: SYMBOL
body:
Command: ERRORDetector:SYMBOL?
Description: This command queries all symbol error settings, status, and results.
Category: Error Detector

title: TEST
body:
Command: ERRORDetector:SYMBOL:TEST
Description: This command initiates and terminates symbol error testing for the arguments START and STOP. Zeroes the symbol error results for the argument CLEAR. Re-synchronizes the recovered clock for the argument SYNC. This command also queries all of the symbol test settings and results for symbol error testing.
Category: Error Detector

title: BADCHARS
body:
Command: ERRORDetector:SYMBOL:TEST:BADCHARS?
Description: This command queries the illegal character count for symbol error testing.
Category: Error Detector

title: BITCOUNT
body:
Command: ERRORDetector:SYMBOL:TEST:BITCOUNT?
Description: This command queries the bit error count (number of bad bits) for symbol error testing. Commands listed in alphabetical order
Category: Error Detector

title: BITDURATION
body:
Command: ERRORDetector:SYMBOL:TEST:BITDURATION?
Description: This command queries the elapsed duration in units of bits tested for symbol error testing.
Category: Error Detector

title: BITRate
body:
Command: ERRORDetector:SYMBOL:TEST:BITRate?
Description: This command queries the calculated bit error rate for symbol error testing.
Category: Error Detector

title: COUNt
body:
Command: ERRORDetector:SYMBOL:TEST:COUNt?
Description: This command queries the symbol error count for symbol error testing.
Category: Error Detector

title: DISParity
body:
Command: ERRORDetector:SYMBOL:TEST:DISParity?
Description: This command queries the disparity error count for symbol error testing.
Category: Error Detector

title: DURATION
body:
Command: ERRORDetector:SYMBOL:TEST:DURATION?
Description: This command queries the elapsed duration (in units of symbols) for symbol error testing.
Category: Error Detector

title: MAXALIGNS
body:
Command: ERRORDetector:SYMBOL:TEST:MAXALIGNS?
Description: This command queries the maximum consecutive skip order sets encountered for symbol error testing.
Category: Error Detector

title: RATE
body:
Command: ERRORDetector:SYMBOL:TEST:RATE?
Description: This command queries the calculated symbol error rate for symbol error testing.
Category: Error Detector

title: RESults
body:
Command: ERRORDetector:SYMBOL:TEST:RESults?
Description: This command queries all of the results for symbol error testing. Commands listed in alphabetical order
Category: Error Detector

title: SECOnds
body:
Command: ERRORDetector:SYMBOL:TEST:SECOnds?
Description: This command queries the elapsed duration time (in seconds) for symbol error testing.
Category: Error Detector

title: STATUS
body:
Command: ERRORDetector:SYMBOL:TEST:STATUS?
Description: This command queries all of the status for the symbol error test.
Category: Error Detector

title: LOCK
body:
Command: ERRORDetector:SYMBOL:TEST:STATUS:LOCK?
Description: This command queries the LOCK status for the symbol error test.
Category: Error Detector

title: SIGNAL
body:
Command: ERRORDetector:SYMBOL:TEST:STATUS:SIGNAL?
Description: This command queries the SIGNAL status for the symbol error test.
Category: Error Detector

title: START
body:
Command: ERRORDetector:SYMBOL:TEST:STATUS:START?
Description: This command queries the START status for symbol error testing.
Category: Error Detector

title: TIME
body:
Command: ERRORDetector:SYMBOL:TEST:TIME?
Description: This command queries the elapsed time (in days, hours, minutes, and seconds) for symbol error testing.
Category: Error Detector

title: DAYS
body:
Command: ERRORDetector:SYMBOL:TEST:TIME:DAYS?
Description: This command queries the elapsed time days component for symbol error testing. Commands listed in alphabetical order
Category: Error Detector

title: HOURS
body:
Command: ERRORDetector:SYMBOL:TEST:TIME:HOURS?
Description: This command queries the elapsed time hours component for symbol error testing.
Category: Error Detector

title: MINUTES
body:
Command: ERRORDetector:SYMBOL:TEST:TIME:MINUTES?
Description: This command queries the elapsed time minutes component for symbol error testing.
Category: Error Detector

title: SECOnds
body:
Command: ERRORDetector:SYMBOL:TEST:TIME:SECOnds?
Description: This command queries the elapsed time seconds component for symbol error testing.
Category: Error Detector

title: TIMEformat
body:
Command: ERRORDetector:TIMEformat
Description: This command sets or queries error detector Elapsed Time Format as DDHHMMSS or Seconds.
Category: Error Detector

title: TYPe
body:
Command: ERRORDetector:TYPe
Description: This command sets or queries the error detector type.
Category: Error Detector

title: FILEName
body:
Command: EXPort:FILEName
Description: This command sets or queries the file/path that will be sent export data on the next EXPort command.
Category: Hard copy

title: FORMat
body:
Command: EXPort:FORMat
Description: This command sets or queries the image format for exporting waveforms to a file.
Category: Hard copy

title: PALEtte
body:
Command: EXPort:PALEtte
Description: This command sets or queries the export color palette.
Category: Hard copy

title: READOuts
body:
Command: EXPort:READOuts
Description: This command sets or queries the area on the screen where the readout appear for export.
Category: Hard copy

title: VIEW
body:
Command: EXPort:VIEW
Description: This command sets or queries the area of the screen to be exported.
Category: Hard copy

title: FILEName
body:
Command: HARDCopy:FILEName
Description: This command sets or queries the file that will be sent hardcopy data on the next HARDCopy command (if the HARDCopy:PORT is set to FILE).
Category: Hard copy

title: LAYout
body:
Command: HARDCopy:LAYout
Description: This command sets or queries the page orientation for hard copy.
Category: Hard copy

title: PALEtte
body:
Command: HARDCopy:PALEtte
Description: This command sets or queries the hard copy color palette. Commands listed in alphabetical order
Category: Hard copy

title: PORT
body:
Command: HARDCopy:PORT
Description: This command selects or returns whether the hard copy data will be sent to a file or printed on the next hard copy command (for example, the HARDCopy STARt command). This is equivalent to selecting Print in the File menu and then either choosing Print to file or specifying the default printer. If FILE is selected then the EXPORT setup commands are used to create the file. If PRINTER is selected, then the HARDCOPY commands are used to send the data to the printer.
Category: Hard copy

title: READOuts
body:
Command: HARDCopy:READOuts
Description: This command sets or queries the area on a hard copy where the readout appears. This command is equivalent to selecting Page Setup from the File menu and setting the Readouts Below Graticule in the control window.
Category: Hard copy

title: VIEW
body:
Command: HARDCopy:VIEW
Description: This command sets or queries the area of the screen to be hard copied.
Category: Hard copy

title: COPy
body:
Command: FILESystem:COPy
Description: This command (no query form) copies a named file to a new file. The new file might be in a totally separate directory than the old file. You can only copy one file at a time using this command. Wild card characters are not allowed.
Category: File system

title: CWD
body:
Command: FILESystem:CWD
Description: This command sets or queries the current working directory for FILESystem GPIB commands. The default working directory is “C:\Users\Public\Tektronix\TekScope”. Anytime that you use this command to change the directory, the directory that you specify is retained as the current working directory until you either change the directory or you delete the directory. If you delete the current working directory, the instrument resets current working directory to the default directory (C:\Users\Public\Tektronix\TekScope) the next time the instrument is powered on or the next time you execute a file system command. The current working directory is retained between power cycles. Commands listed in alphabetical order This command supports the permutations of file and directory names that are supported by Microsoft Windows: Relative path names; for example, “.\Temp” Absolute path names; for example, “C:\Windows\System” Implied relative path names; for example “newfile.text” becomes “C:\Users\Public\Tektronix\TekScope\newfile.txt” if the current working directory is “C:\Users\Public\Tektronix\TekScope”
Category: File system

title: DELEte
body:
Command: FILESystem:DELEte
Description: This command (no query form) deletes a named file. It does not delete directories. Use the FILESystem:RMDir command to delete a directory.
Category: File system

title: DIR
body:
Command: FILESystem:DIR?
Description: This query-only command returns a comma separated list of quoted strings. Each string contains the name of a file or directory in the current working directory.
Category: File system

title: MKDir
body:
Command: FILESystem:MKDir
Description: This command (no query form) creates a new directory.
Category: File system

title: PRInt
body:
Command: FILESystem:PRInt
Description: This command (no query form) prints a named file to a named port. This command is the same as the FILESystem:READFile command. This command is not IEEE 488.2 compliant for the following reasons: The command produces output on the 488.2 bus. Queries, not commands, are the only message units that should produce output on the 488.2 bus. Errors might not be recognized if they occur while data is being output in response to this command. This will be evident if a command is sent to the instrument before all of the data generated by this command is read. In such cases, you should send a device clear message to the instrument. The command does not use a standard 488.2 output format. The closest defined format is the indefinite format of ARBITRARY BLOCK PROGRAM DATA encoding. However, this command output omits the initial '#0' characters needed for that format. Newlines, ASCII value 10, can be included in the output data. Output on the bus can be terminated by newlines. The output of this command is only terminated by <EOI>.
Category: File system

title: READFile
body:
Command: FILESystem:READFile
Description: This command (no query form) prints a named file to a named port. It is identical to the FILESystem:PRInt command. Commands listed in alphabetical order This command is not IEEE 488.2 compliant for the following reasons: The command produces output on the 488.2 bus. Queries, not commands, are the only message units that should produce output on the 488.2 bus. Errors might not be recognized if they occur while data is being output in response to this command. This will be evident if a command is sent to the instrument before all of the data generated by this command is read. In such cases, you should send a device clear message to the instrument. The command does not use a standard 488.2 output format. The closest defined format is the indefinite format of ARBITRARY BLOCK PROGRAM DATA encoding. However, this command output omits the initial '#0' characters needed for that format. Newlines, ASCII value 10, can be included in the output data. Output on the bus can be terminated by newlines. The output of this command is only terminated by <EOI>.
Category: File system

title: REName
body:
Command: FILESystem:REName
Description: This command (no query form) assigns a new name to an existing file.
Category: File system

title: RMDir
body:
Command: FILESystem:RMDir
Description: This command (no query form) deletes a named directory. The directory cannot contain any files. If there are files in the directory, they must first be deleted. The directory must not be a read-only directory.
Category: File system

title: WRITEFile
body:
Command: FILESystem:WRITEFile
Description: This command (no query form) copies the block data from the GPIB port to a named file.
Category: File system

title: BOX
body:
Command: HIStogram:BOX
Description: This command defines or returns the left, top, right, and bottom boundaries of the histogram box, in source waveform coordinates. This command is equivalent to selecting Waveform Histograms from the Measure menu and then setting Limits for Left, Right, Top, and Bottom in the appropriate boxes. The command is similar to the HIStogram:BOXPcnt command except that command uses percentage coordinates to define the boundaries of the histogram box.
Category: Histogram

title: BOXPcnt
body:
Command: HIStogram:BOXPcnt
Description: This command defines or queries the left, top, right, and bottom positions of the histogram box in percentage coordinates. The upper left has the value 0,0 and the lower right has the value 100, 100. Any value outside the range of 0 to 100 is clipped. This command is similar to the HIStogram:BOX command except that this command uses percentage coordinates to define the boundaries of the histogram box.
Category: Histogram

title: COUNt
body:
Command: HIStogram:COUNt
Description: This command (no query form) clears the count and statistics for the histogram and the histogram source data. If the histogram is on, then counting restarts. This command is equivalent selecting Waveform Histograms from the Measure menu and then clicking the Reset button.
Category: Histogram

title: DATa
body:
Command: HIStogram:DATa?
Description: This query-only command returns an ASCII comma-separated list of histogram data numbers. This command returns 252 values for vertical histograms and 1000 values for horizontal histograms. The first and last value are not displayed, and are used for over range and under range tracking. The Histogram:DATa? query corresponds to the Histogram Data (CSV) controls included in the instrument user interface. To find these controls, select either Edit > Copy > Setup > Measurements tab or the File > Save As > Histogram button. This command returns only histogram data values separated by commas on a single line. The CSV file created by the user interface actions indicated above, writes out a line for each histogram data point, and contains the time relative to the trigger, a comma, and the histogram value.
Category: Histogram

title: DISplay
body:
Command: HIStogram:DISplay
Description: This command sets the scaling of the histogram data display to be the count of each histogram bin or the log of that count. The default scaling is Linear. The query form of this command returns either the histogram display scaling or the display status “off.” This command is equivalent to selecting Waveform Histogram from the Measure menu, and then selecting Linear or Log in the Histogram Scaling group.
Category: Histogram

title: FUNCtion
body:
Command: HIStogram:FUNCtion
Description: This command either selects the type of histogram to create or returns the current histogram type. This command is equivalent to selecting Waveform Histogram from the Measure menu and then choosing either Horizontal or Vertical from the Histogram Mode group box.
Category: Histogram

title: MODe
body:
Command: HIStogram:MODe
Description: This command selects the type of histogram to create or disables the histogram display. The query form either returns the current histogram type or that histogram display is disabled. This command is equivalent to selecting Waveform Histogram from the Measure menu and then choosing from the Histogram Mode group box. Commands listed in alphabetical order HIStogram:MODe is functionally equivalent to the HIStogram:FUNCtion and HIStogram:STATE commands and is included for backward compatibility purposes.
Category: Histogram

title: SIZe
body:
Command: HIStogram:SIZe
Description: This command sets or queries the width or height of the histogram on the screen. This is equivalent to selecting Waveform Histograms from the Measure menu and then entering a value in the Histogram Size box.
Category: Histogram

title: SOUrce
body:
Command: HIStogram:SOUrce
Description: This command sets or queries which source will be compared against the histogram box when the histogram testing is enabled. This is equivalent to selecting Waveform Histograms from the Measure menu and then choosing the desired waveform source. The waveform need not be displayed for histograms to run. You might want the channel displays disabled so you can see a full-screen histogram and not have waveform data confuse the display.
Category: Histogram

title: STATE
body:
Command: HIStogram:STATE
Description: This command sets or queries whether the histogram calculations are enabled. This is equivalent to selecting Waveform Histograms from the Measure menu.
Category: Histogram

title: ACQDURATION
body:
Command: HORizontal:ACQDURATION?
Description: This query returns the timebase duration.
Category: Horizontal

title: ACQLENGTH
body:
Command: HORizontal:ACQLENGTH?
Description: This query returns the record length.
Category: Horizontal

title: POSition
body:
Command: HORizontal:DIGital:MAGnivu:POSition?
Description: This command queries the horizontal position of the trigger in percent, within the MagniVu digital acquisition. The MagniVu digital acquisition always includes the trigger. When the trigger position is adjusted near the edges of the display, the horizontal position of the MagniVu acquisition is adjusted to keep as much of the MagniVu acquisition in the display region as possible.
Category: Horizontal

title: RECOrdlength
body:
Command: HORizontal:DIGital:RECOrdlength?
Description: This command queries the record length of the digital acquisition.
Category: Horizontal

title: MAGnivu
body:
Command: HORizontal:DIGital:RECOrdlength:MAGnivu?
Description: This command queries the record length of the MagniVu digital acquisition.
Category: Horizontal

title: MAIn
body:
Command: HORizontal:DIGital:RECOrdlength:MAIn?
Description: This command queries the record length of the main digital acquisition. This is the non-MagniVu, long record length acquisition.
Category: Horizontal

title: SAMPLERate
body:
Command: HORizontal:DIGital:SAMPLERate?
Description: This command queries the sample rate of the digital acquisition. HORizontal:DIGital:SAMPLERate? returns the sample rate of the currently selected digital acquisition (either the Main values or the MagniVu values, depending on the ACQuire:MAGnivu setting.
Category: Horizontal

title: MAGnivu
body:
Command: HORizontal:DIGital:SAMPLERate:MAGnivu?
Description: This command queries the sample rate of the MagniVu digital acquisition.
Category: Horizontal

title: MAIn
body:
Command: HORizontal:DIGital:SAMPLERate:MAIn?
Description: This command queries the sample rate of the MagniVu digital acquisition.
Category: Horizontal

title: DIVisions
body:
Command: HORizontal:DIVisions?
Description: This query-only command returns the number of graticule divisions over which the waveform is displayed.
Category: Horizontal

title: FASTframe
body:
Command: HORizontal:FASTframe?
Description: This query-only command returns all of the settings for the horizontal FastFrame commands.
Category: Horizontal

title: COUNt
body:
Command: HORizontal:FASTframe:COUNt
Description: This command sets or queries FastFrame frame count. This command is equivalent to selecting Fast Frame Setup from the Horiz/Acq menu and entering a value in the Frame Count box. FastFrame captures a series of triggered acquisitions with minimal intervening time. Commands listed in alphabetical order
Category: Horizontal

title: MAXFRames
body:
Command: HORizontal:FASTframe:MAXFRames?
Description: This query only command returns the maximum number of FastFrame frames which can be acquired at the current frame length.
Category: Horizontal

title: <wfm>
body:
Command: HORizontal:FASTframe:MULtipleframes:FRAMESTart:<wfm>
Description: This command sets or queries the start frame number on the specified waveform for the FastFrame multiple frames feature. Valid waveforms include CH<x>, MATH<x>, and REF<x>. The value of x can be an integer ranging from 1 through 4. The multiple frames feature supports displaying multiple frames in an overlaid manner.
Category: Horizontal

title: MODe
body:
Command: HORizontal:FASTframe:MULtipleframes:MODe
Description: This command sets or queries the mode for the FastFrame multiple frames feature. This feature displays multiple frames in an overlaid manner.
Category: Horizontal

title: <wfm>
body:
Command: HORizontal:FASTframe:MULtipleframes:NUMFRames:<wfm>
Description: This command sets or queries the number of frames on the specified waveform for the FastFrame multiple frames feature. The multiple frames feature supports displaying multiple frames in an overlaid manner. Valid waveforms include CH<x>, MATH<x> and REF<x>. The x variable can be expressed as an integer ranging from 1 through 4.
Category: Horizontal

title: FRAme
body:
Command: HORizontal:FASTframe:REF:FRAme
Description: This command sets or queries the FastFrame reference frame number. The Reference Frame number is used to calculate time differences for each frame and displays those differences in the graticule. This command is equivalent to selecting FastFrame Setup in the Horiz/Acq menu and entering a value in the Time Stamps/Frame box.
Category: Horizontal

title: SOUrce
body:
Command: HORizontal:FASTframe:REF:SOUrce
Description: This command sets or queries FastFrame Reference waveform source. This is equivalent to selecting FastFrame Setup from the Horiz/Acq menu and choosing the reference source.
Category: Horizontal

title: CH
body:
Command: HORizontal:FASTframe:SELECTED:CH<x>
Description: This command sets or queries the FastFrame selected frame number on the specified waveform.
Category: Horizontal

title: MATH
body:
Command: HORizontal:FASTframe:SELECTED:MATH<x>
Description: This command sets or queries the FastFrame selected frame number on the specified waveform.
Category: Horizontal

title: REF
body:
Command: HORizontal:FASTframe:SELECTED:REF<x>
Description: This command sets or queries the FastFrame selected frame number on the specified waveform.
Category: Horizontal

title: SOUrce
body:
Command: HORizontal:FASTframe:SELECTED:SOUrce
Description: This command sets or queries the FastFrame source waveform. This is equivalent to selecting FastFrame Setup from the Horiz/Acq menu, and then choosing the waveform source. Valid waveforms include CH<x> and MATH<x>. The x variable can be expressed as an integer ranging from 1 through 4.
Category: Horizontal

title: SEQuence
body:
Command: HORizontal:FASTframe:SEQuence
Description: This command sets or queries the FastFrame single-sequence mode stop condition.
Category: Horizontal

title: SINGLEFramemath
body:
Command: HORizontal:FASTframe:SINGLEFramemath
Description: This command sets or queries FastFrame single frame math.
Category: Horizontal

title: SIXteenbit
body:
Command: HORizontal:FASTframe:SIXteenbit
Description: This command sets or queries FastFrame sixteen bit.
Category: Horizontal

title: STATE
body:
Command: HORizontal:FASTframe:STATE
Description: This command sets or queries the state of FastFrame acquisition. This is equivalent to setting FastFrame to On in the FastFrame Setup menu. FastFrame lets users capture a series of triggered acquisitions with minimal time between them. The digitizing instrument in FastFrame mode is ready to accept a continuous burst of triggers 400 ms after the controller sends the ACQuire:STATE RUN command.
Category: Horizontal

title: SUMFrame
body:
Command: HORizontal:FASTframe:SUMFrame
Description: This command sets or queries the summary frame mode. When ENVelope is selected, the last frame in a FastFrame acquisition is an envelope of all the prior frames in the acquisition. When AVErage is selected, the last frame is replaced with a frame that is the computed average of all the prior frames in the acquisition. For the summary frame control to be active, the number of frames must be two or greater.
Category: Horizontal

title: <wfm>
body:
Command: HORizontal:FASTframe:TIMEStamp:ALL:<wfm>?
Description: This query-only command returns the frame number and time stamp for each frame between requested frames, inclusive, within the specified waveform. Argument order is unimportant. Valid waveforms include CH<x>, MATH<x> and REF<x>. The x variable can be expressed as an integer ranging from 1 through 4. The format of the output is as follows: DD MON YYYY HH:MM:SS.xxx xxx xxx xxx DD MON YYYY is day, month, and year HH:MM:SS is hours, minutes, and seconds .xxx xxx xxx xxx is fractions of a second to picoseconds Commands listed in alphabetical order
Category: Horizontal

title: <wfm>
body:
Command: HORizontal:FASTframe:TIMEStamp:BETWeen:<wfm>?
Description: This query-only command returns the relative trigger for the delta time between the specified frames, within the specified waveform. Valid waveforms include CH<x>, MATH<x> and REF<x>. The x variable can be expressed as an integer ranging from 1 through 4. The format of the delta time output is as follows: HH:MM:SS.xxx xxx xxx xxx HH:MM:SS is hours, minutes, and seconds .xxx xxx xxx xxx is fractions of a second to picoseconds
Category: Horizontal

title: <wfm>
body:
Command: HORizontal:FASTframe:TIMEStamp:DELTa:<wfm>?
Description: This query-only command returns the relative time between the triggers of the FastFrame Selected and the FastFrame Reference, within the specified waveform. Valid waveforms include CH<x>, MATH<x>, and REF<x>. The x variable can be expressed as an integer ranging from 1 through 4. The format of the output is as follows: HH:MM:SS.xxx xxx xxx xxx HH:MM:SS is hours, minutes, and seconds .xxx xxx xxx xxx is fractions of a second to picoseconds
Category: Horizontal

title: <wfm>
body:
Command: HORizontal:FASTframe:TIMEStamp:FRAMe:<wfm>?
Description: This query-only command returns the absolute trigger date and time for the specified frame and waveform. Valid waveforms include CH<x>, MATH<x> and REF<x>. The x variable can be expressed as an integer ranging from 1 through 4. The format of the output is as follows: DD MON YYYY HH:MM:SS.xxx xxx xxx xxx DD MON YYYY is day, month, and year HH:MM:SS is hours, minutes, and seconds .xxx xxx xxx xxx is fractions of a second to picoseconds
Category: Horizontal

title: REF
body:
Command: HORizontal:FASTframe:TIMEStamp:REF?
Description: This query-only command returns the absolute trigger date and time for FastFrame reference. The format of the output is as follows: DD MON YYYY HH:MM:SS.xxx xxx xxx xxx DD MON YYYY is day, month, and year HH:MM:SS is hours, minutes, and seconds .xxx xxx xxx xxx is fractions of a second to picoseconds
Category: Horizontal

title: <wfm>
body:
Command: HORizontal:FASTframe:TIMEStamp:SELECTED:<wfm>?
Description: This query-only command returns the absolute trigger date and time for the FastFrame Selected, within the specified waveform. Valid waveforms include CH<x>, MATH<x> and REF<x>. The x variable can be expressed as an integer ranging from 1 through 4. The format of the output is as follows: DD MON YYYY HH:MM:SS.xxx xxx xxx xxx DD MON YYYY is day, month, and year HH:MM:SS is hours, minutes, and seconds .xxx xxx xxx xxx is fractions of a second to picoseconds Commands listed in alphabetical order
Category: Horizontal

title: TRACk
body:
Command: HORizontal:FASTframe:TRACk
Description: This command sets up or queries the state of FastFrame tracking feature. This command is equivalent to selecting FastFrame Setup from the Horiz/Acq menu and then clicking the desired Frame Tracking state. When FastFrame Track is set to “live”, the channel and math waveforms are locked together. Adjusting a channel waveform also adjusts a related math waveform. All reference waveforms are also locked together but they are separate from channel and math waveforms. For example, when you set the Selected Frame Source Ch1 to Frame 3, then Selected Frame Ch2, Ch3, Ch4, Math1, Math2, Math3 and Math4 are also set to Frame 3. When you set the Selected Frame Source Ref1 to Frame 2, then Selected Frame Ref2, Ref3 and Ref4 are also set to Frame 2. If the Frame Tracking is set to Live, changing Selected Frame Ch1 will not affect the Selected Frame Ref1 frame of the Reference Frame setting. When FastFrame Track is set to “all”, the channel, math and reference waveforms are locked together. Adjusting a channel waveform also adjusts the related math and reference waveforms. For example, when you set the Selected Frame Source Ch1 to Frame 3, then Selected Frame Ch2, Ch3, Ch4, Math1, Math2, Math3, Math4, Ref1, Ref2, Ref3 and Ref4 are also set to Frame 3.
Category: Horizontal

title: CH
body:
Command: HORizontal:FASTframe:XZEro:ALL:CH<x>?
Description: This command queries the time from the trigger to the trigger sample of the specified frames on the specified channel.
Category: Horizontal

title: REF
body:
Command: HORizontal:FASTframe:XZEro:ALL:REF<x>?
Description: This command queries the time from the trigger to the trigger sample of the specified frames on the specified reference.
Category: Horizontal

title: CH
body:
Command: HORizontal:FASTframe:XZEro:FRAme:CH<x>?
Description: This command queries the time from the trigger to the trigger sample of the specified frame on the specified channel.
Category: Horizontal

title: REF
body:
Command: HORizontal:FASTframe:XZEro:FRAme:REF<x>?
Description: This command queries the time from the trigger to the trigger sample of the specified frame on the specified reference.
Category: Horizontal

title: REF
body:
Command: HORizontal:FASTframe:XZEro:REF?
Description: This command sets or queries the time from the trigger to the trigger sample on the reference waveform.
Category: Horizontal

title: CH
body:
Command: HORizontal:FASTframe:XZEro:SELECTED:CH<x>?
Description: This command sets or queries the time from the trigger to the trigger sample on the selected channel. CH<x> can be CH1, CH2, CH3, or CH4.
Category: Horizontal

title: REF
body:
Command: HORizontal:FASTframe:XZEro:SELECTED:REF<x>?
Description: This command sets or queries the time from the trigger to the trigger sample on the selected reference waveform. REF<x> can be REF1, REF2, REF3, or REF4.
Category: Horizontal

title: MAIn
body:
Command: HORizontal:MAIn?
Description: This query-only command returns the time per division of the time base. This command is equivalent to selecting Position/Scale from the Horiz/Acq menu.
Category: Horizontal

title: INTERPRatio
body:
Command: HORizontal:MAIn:INTERPRatio?
Description: This query-only command returns the Horizontal Main time base interpolation ratio. The ratio is useful for calculating the maximum sampling rate when users request sampling rates that are faster than the maximum rate specified by the acquisition hardware. The default maximum sampling rate as specified by the acquisition hardware is 1. An interpolation ratio other than 1 means that displayed and retrieved waveforms contain additional points that are derived through interpolation. At such times, the retrieved waveform data is actually 16-bit data. You should set the data width accordingly.
Category: Horizontal

title: UNIts
body:
Command: HORizontal:MAIn:UNIts
Description: This command sets or queries the units for the horizontal time base. It is equivalent to setting the HORizontal:MAIn:UNIts:STRing.
Category: Horizontal

title: STRing
body:
Command: HORizontal:MAIn:UNIts:STRing
Description: This command sets or queries the units string for the horizontal time base.
Category: Horizontal

title: MODE
body:
Command: HORizontal:MODE
Description: This command set or queries the horizontal mode. Auto mode is the factory default.
Category: Horizontal

title: LIMITrecordlen
body:
Command: HORizontal:MODE:AUTO:LIMITrecordlen
Description: This command sets or queries the record length limit used by the auto horizontal mode.
Category: Horizontal

title: RECOrdlength
body:
Command: HORizontal:MODE:RECOrdlength
Description: This command sets or queries the record length.
Category: Horizontal

title: SAMPLERate
body:
Command: HORizontal:MODE:SAMPLERate
Description: This command sets or queries the sample rate. Commands listed in alphabetical order
Category: Horizontal

title: SCAle
body:
Command: HORizontal:MODE:SCAle
Description: This command sets or queries the horizontal scale.
Category: Horizontal

title: POSition
body:
Command: HORizontal:POSition
Description: This command either positions the waveform horizontally on the display or returns its horizontal position on the display. When Horizontal Delay Mode is turned off, this command is equivalent to adjusting the HORIZONTAL POSITION knob on the front panel.
Category: Horizontal

title: ROLL
body:
Command: HORizontal:ROLL
Description: This command sets or queries the Roll Mode status. Use Roll Mode when you want to view data at very slow sweep speeds. It is useful for observing data samples on the screen as they occur. This command is equivalent to selecting Horizontal/Acquisition Setup from the Horiz/Acq menu, selecting the Acquisition tab, and setting the Roll Mode to Auto or Off.
Category: Horizontal

title: CH
body:
Command: HORizontal:TIMEStamp:CH<x>?
Description: This query returns the horizontal timebase for the channel. The channel is specified by x. The value of x can range from 1 through 4.
Category: Horizontal

title: REF
body:
Command: HORizontal:TIMEStamp:REF<x>?
Description: This query returns the horizontal timebase for the reference waveform. The reference is specified by x. The value of x can range from 1 through 4.
Category: Horizontal

title: BEEP
body:
Command: LIMit:BEEP
Description: This command causes the instrument to beep when the waveform data exceeds the limits set in the limit test (LIMit:STATE must be on).
Category: Limit Test

title: COMpare
body:
Command: LIMit:COMpare
Description: This command resets the Limit Test comparison template.
Category: Limit Test

title: CH
body:
Command: LIMit:COMpare:CH<x>
Description: This command sets or queries the template against which to compare the waveform acquired from the channel specified by x. The template can be a waveform saved in any of the reference locations (REF1 through REF4) or none.
Category: Limit Test

title: MATH
body:
Command: LIMit:COMpare:MATH<x>
Description: This command sets or queries the template against which to compare the math waveform specified by x, which can be 1 through 4. The template can be a waveform saved in any of the reference locations (REF1 through REF4) or none.
Category: Limit Test

title: REF
body:
Command: LIMit:COMpare:REF<x>
Description: This command sets or queries the template against which to compare the reference waveform specified by x, which can be 1 through 4. The template can be a waveform saved in any of the reference locations (REF1 through REF4) or none.
Category: Limit Test

title: EMail
body:
Command: LIMit:EMail
Description: This command sets or queries whether an e-mail is generated when the source waveform data exceeds the limits specified for the limit test.
Category: Limit Test

title: HARDCopy
body:
Command: LIMit:HARDCopy
Description: This command sets or queries whether a hard copy operation is executed on the waveform when any waveform data exceeds the limit set in the limit test. LIMit:STATE must be set to ON for the hard copy operation to execute. The hard copy operation uses the port and prints in the format and layout specified by the HARDCopy commands.
Category: Limit Test

title: HIGHLIGHTHits
body:
Command: LIMit:HIGHLIGHTHits
Description: This command sets or queries whether violation highlighting occurs when limit testing is active, and, if the RESET argument is set, clears the highlighting.
Category: Limit Test

title: RESet
body:
Command: LIMit:HIGHLIGHTHits:RESet
Description: This command resets the hits highlighting for limit testing.
Category: Limit Test

title: LOCk
body:
Command: LIMit:LOCk
Description: This command sets or queries whether vertical scaling and positioning affect both source and template for template comparison pairs.
Category: Limit Test

title: LOG
body:
Command: LIMit:LOG
Description: This command sets or queries whether a log file is saved when the source waveform data exceeds the test limits.
Category: Limit Test

title: SAVEWFM
body:
Command: LIMit:SAVEWFM
Description: This command sets or queries whether the source waveform is saved when the source waveform data exceeds the test limits.
Category: Limit Test

title: FILEName
body:
Command: LIMit:SAVEWFM:FILEName
Description: This command sets or queries the path where waveforms or log files will be saved when the waveform data exceeds the limits set by the limit test.
Category: Limit Test

title: SRQ
body:
Command: LIMit:SRQ
Description: This command sets or queries whether a Service Request Interrupt (SRQ) is generated when the waveform data falls outside of the test limits.
Category: Limit Test

title: STATE
body:
Command: LIMit:STATE
Description: This command sets limit testing on or off or queries whether limit testing is in effect.
Category: Limit Test

title: STATus
body:
Command: LIMit:STATus?
Description: This command queries the state of limit testing.
Category: Limit Test

title: STOPOnviolation
body:
Command: LIMit:STOPOnviolation
Description: This command sets or queries whether acquisitions are stopped when the waveform data exceeds the test limits.
Category: Limit Test

title: DESTination
body:
Command: LIMit:TEMPlate:DESTination
Description: This command sets or queries the destination reference waveform that the LIMit:TEMPlate:STORe command will use.
Category: Limit Test

title: SOUrce
body:
Command: LIMit:TEMPlate:SOUrce
Description: This command sets or queries the channel, math waveform, or reference waveform that the LIMit:TEMPlate:STORe command will use.
Category: Limit Test

title: STORe
body:
Command: LIMit:TEMPlate:STORe
Description: This command (no query form) saves the specified source waveform to the specified reference or file name.
Category: Limit Test

title: HORizontal
body:
Command: LIMit:TEMPlate:TOLerance:HORizontal
Description: This command sets or queries the amount in units of horizontal divisions, by which the source waveform is varied horizontally when creating the destination waveform. Commands listed in alphabetical order
Category: Limit Test

title: VERTical
body:
Command: LIMit:TEMPlate:TOLerance:VERTical
Description: This command sets or queries the amount in units of vertical divisions, by which the source waveform is varied vertically when creating the destination waveform.
Category: Limit Test

title: CREATE
body:
Command: MARK:CREATE
Description: This command creates a mark on a specified waveform or all waveforms in a column.
Category: Search and Mark

title: DELEte
body:
Command: MARK:DELEte
Description: This command deletes a mark on a particular waveform, all waveforms in a column, the selected mark, or all marks.
Category: Search and Mark

title: FREE
body:
Command: MARK:FREE?
Description: This query-only command returns how many user marks are available for use.
Category: Search and Mark

title: END
body:
Command: MARK:SELECTED:END?
Description: This query-only command returns the end of the selected mark, 0 to 100% of the waveform.
Category: Search and Mark

title: FOCUS
body:
Command: MARK:SELECTED:FOCUS?
Description: This query-only command returns the focus of the selected mark, 0 to 100% of the waveform.
Category: Search and Mark

title: LABel
body:
Command: MARK:SELECTED:LABel
Description: This command sets or queries the label for the selected mark.
Category: Search and Mark

title: MARKSINCOLumn
body:
Command: MARK:SELECTED:MARKSINCOLumn?
Description: This query-only command returns the number of marks in the current zoom pixel column.
Category: Search and Mark

title: OWNer
body:
Command: MARK:SELECTED:OWNer?
Description: This query-only command returns the owner of the selected mark. Commands listed in alphabetical order
Category: Search and Mark

title: SOUrce
body:
Command: MARK:SELECTED:SOUrce?
Description: This query-only command returns the source waveform for the selected mark.
Category: Search and Mark

title: STARt
body:
Command: MARK:SELECTED:STARt?
Description: This query-only command returns the starting point of the selected mark, 0 to 100% of the waveform.
Category: Search and Mark

title: STATE
body:
Command: MARK:SELECTED:STATE
Description: This command sets or queries the on or off state of the selected mark. The selected mark is at or near the center of the display. If you push the Set/Clear button, this mark will disappear.
Category: Search and Mark

title: POSition
body:
Command: MARK:SELECTED:ZOOm:POSition?
Description: This query-only command returns the position of the selected mark, of the zoom overview window. If the return value is less than zero then the zoom scale will not be changed when Next or Prev makes this the selected mark.
Category: Search and Mark

title: SCAle
body:
Command: MARK:SELECTED:ZOOm:SCAle?
Description: This query-only command returns the scale of the selected mark of the zoom overview window. The returned value might be < 0 (for example from Search), which means that the zoom scale will not be changed when Next or Prev makes this the selected mark.
Category: Search and Mark

title: TOTal
body:
Command: MARK:TOTal?
Description: This query-only command returns how many marks are currently in use.
Category: Search and Mark

title: MARKALLevents
body:
Command: SEARCH:MARKALLevents
Description: This command sets or queries the mark all trigger events setting.
Category: Search and Mark

title: SEARCH
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>
Description: This command converts the specified search marks to the user marks. <x> can be 1 to 8.
Category: Search and Mark

title: COPy
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:COPy
Description: This command (no query form) copies the search criteria to the trigger, the trigger criteria to a search, or copy search to search. <x> is the search number.
Category: Search and Mark

title: STATE
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:STATE
Description: This command sets the search state to on or off. <x> is the search number, which can be 1 to 8. The query form returns the search state.
Category: Search and Mark

title: TOTAL
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TOTAL?
Description: This query-only command returns the total number of matches for the search. The total number of matches can be more than the number of marks placed. <x> is the search number.
Category: Search and Mark

title: CONDition
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:CAN:CONDition
Description: This command sets or queries the CAN bus trigger condition. The search number is specified by x.
Category: Search and Mark

title: FORMat
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:CAN:DATa:FORMat
Description: This command sets or queries the CAN bus trigger data format. The search number is specified by x.
Category: Search and Mark

title: QUALifier
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:CAN:DATa:QUALifier
Description: This command sets or queries the CAN bus trigger data qualifier. The search number is specified by x. Commands listed in alphabetical order
Category: Search and Mark

title: SIZe
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:CAN:DATa:SIZe
Description: This command sets or queries the CAN bus trigger data size in bytes. The search number is specified by x.
Category: Search and Mark

title: VALue
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:CAN:DATa:VALue
Description: This command sets or queries the CAN bus trigger data value. The search number is specified by x. Commands listed in alphabetical order
Category: Search and Mark

title: FRAMEtype
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:CAN:FRAMEtype
Description: This command sets or queries CAN bus trigger frame type. The search number is specified by x.
Category: Search and Mark

title: DIRection
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:CAN:IDentifier:DIRection
Description: This command sets or queries the CAN bus identifier direction. The search number is specified by x.
Category: Search and Mark

title: FORMat
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:CAN:IDentifier:FORMat
Description: This command sets or queries the CAN bus trigger identifier format. The search number is specified by x.
Category: Search and Mark

title: MODe
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:CAN:IDentifier:MODe
Description: This command sets or queries the CAN bus trigger identifier mode. The search number is specified by x.
Category: Search and Mark

title: VALue
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:CAN:IDentifier:VALue
Description: This command sets or queries CAN bus trigger identifier value. The search number is specified by x.
Category: Search and Mark

title: CONDition
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:ETHERnet:CONDition
Description: This command specifies a field or condition within an Ethernet frame to search on.
Category: Search and Mark

title: FORMat
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:ETHERnet:DATa:FORMat
Description: This command specifies the format (binary or hex) of the bit pattern to use when searching on the Ethernet data field. The search condition needs to be set to DATa.
Category: Search and Mark

title: OFFSet
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:ETHERnet:DATa:OFFSet
Description: This command specifies the data offset value, in bytes, to use when searching on the Ethernet data field. The search condition needs to be set to DATa.
Category: Search and Mark

title: SIZe
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:ETHERnet:DATa:SIZe
Description: This command specifies the number of contiguous TCP/IPv4/MAC client bytes to use when searching on the Ethernet data field. The default is all X’s (don’t care). The search condition needs to be set to DATa.
Category: Search and Mark

title: VALue
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:ETHERnet:DATa:VALue
Description: This command specifies the binary value to use when searching on the Ethernet data field. The default is all X’s (don’t care). The search condition needs to be set to DATa.
Category: Search and Mark

title: FORMat
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:ETHERnet:IPHeader:DESTinationaddr:FORMat
Description: This command specifies the format (binary or hex) of the bit pattern to use when searching on the Ethernet IPv4 header address destination field. The search condition needs to be set to IPHeader.
Category: Search and Mark

title: VALue
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:ETHERnet:IPHeader:DESTinationaddr:VALue
Description: This command specifies the 32-bit value to use when searching on the Ethernet IPv4 header address destination field. The default is all X’s (don’t care). The search condition needs to be set to IPHeader.
Category: Search and Mark

title: FORMat
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:ETHERnet:IPHeader:PROTOcol:FORMat
Description: This command specifies the data format (binary or hex) of the bit pattern to use when searching on the Ethernet IPv4 header protocol field. The search condition needs to be set to MACADDRess.
Category: Search and Mark

title: VALue
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:ETHERnet:IPHeader:PROTOcol:VALue
Description: This command specifies the 8-bit value to use when searching on the Ethernet IPv4 header protocol field. The default is all X’s (don’t care). The search condition needs to be set to IPHeader.
Category: Search and Mark

title: FORMat
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:ETHERnet:IPHeader:SOUrceaddr:FORMat
Description: This command specifies the data format (binary or hex) of the bit pattern to use when searching on the Ethernet IPv4 header address source field. The search condition needs to be set to IP header.
Category: Search and Mark

title: VALue
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:ETHERnet:IPHeader:SOUrceaddr:VALue
Description: This command specifies the 32-bit value to use when searching on the Ethernet IPv4 header address source field. The default is all X’s (don’t care). The search condition needs to be set to IPHeader. Commands listed in alphabetical order
Category: Search and Mark

title: FORMat
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:ETHERnet:MAC:ADDRess:DESTination:FORMat
Description: This command specifies the data format (binary or hex) of the bit pattern to use when searching on the Ethernet MAC address destination field. The search condition needs to be set to MACADDRess.
Category: Search and Mark

title: VALue
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:ETHERnet:MAC:ADDRess:DESTination:VALue
Description: This command specifies the 48-bit value to use when searching on the Ethernet MAC address destination field. The default is all X’s (don’t care). The search condition needs to be set to MACADDRess.
Category: Search and Mark

title: FORMat
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:ETHERnet:MAC:ADDRess:SOUrce:FORMat
Description: This command specifies the format (binary or hex) of the bit pattern to use when searching on the Ethernet MAC address source field. The search condition needs to be set to MACADDRess.
Category: Search and Mark

title: VALue
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:ETHERnet:MAC:ADDRess:SOUrce:VALue
Description: This command specifies the 48-bit value to use when searching on the Ethernet MAC address source field. The default is all X’s (don’t care). The search condition needs to be set to MACADDRess.
Category: Search and Mark

title: FORMat
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:ETHERnet:QTAG:FORMat
Description: This command specifies the data format (binary or hex) to use when searching on the Q-Tag field. The search condition needs to be set to QTAG.
Category: Search and Mark

title: VALue
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:ETHERnet:QTAG:VALue
Description: This command specifies the 32-bit value to use when searching on the Ethernet Q-Tag field. The default is all X’s (don’t care). The search condition needs to be set to QTAG.
Category: Search and Mark

title: QUALifier
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:ETHERnet:QUALifier
Description: This command specifies the qualifier to be used when the Ethernet search condition is set to MACLENgth or DATa. Normally, the Ethernet qualifier is set to “equal to”.
Category: Search and Mark

title: FORMat
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:ETHERnet:TCPHeader:ACKnum:FORMat
Description: This command specifies the format (binary or hex) of the bit pattern to use when searching on the Ethernet TCP header acknowledgement number. The search condition needs to be set to TCPHeader.
Category: Search and Mark

title: VALue
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:ETHERnet:TCPHeader:ACKnum:VALue
Description: This command specifies the 32-bit value to use when searching on the Ethernet TCP header acknowledgement number. The default is all X’s (don’t care). The search condition needs to be set to TCPHeader.
Category: Search and Mark

title: FORMat
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:ETHERnet:TCPHeader:DESTinationport:FORMat
Description: This command specifies the format (binary or hex) of the bit pattern to use when searching on the Ethernet TCP header destination port number. The search condition needs to be set to TCPHeader.
Category: Search and Mark

title: VALue
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:ETHERnet:TCPHeader:DESTinationport:VALue
Description: This command specifies the 16-bit value to use when searching on the Ethernet TCP header destination port number. The default is all X’s (don’t care). The search condition needs to be set to TCPHeader.
Category: Search and Mark

title: FORMat
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:ETHERnet:TCPHeader:SEQnum:FORMat
Description: This command specifies the format (binary or hex) of the bit pattern to use when searching on the Ethernet TCP header sequence number. The search condition needs to be set to TCPHeader.
Category: Search and Mark

title: VALue
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:ETHERnet:TCPHeader:SEQnum:VALue
Description: This command specifies the 32-bit value to use when searching on the Ethernet TCP header sequence number. The default is all X’s (don’t care). The search condition needs to be set to TCPHeader.
Category: Search and Mark

title: FORMat
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:ETHERnet:TCPHeader:SOUrceport:FORMat
Description: This command specifies the format (binary or hex) of the bit pattern to use when searching on the Ethernet TCP header source port number. The search condition needs to be set to TCPHeader.
Category: Search and Mark

title: VALue
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:ETHERnet:TCPHeader:SOUrceport:VALue
Description: This command specifies the 16-bit value to use when searching on the Ethernet TCP header source port number. The default is all X’s (don’t care). The search condition needs to be set to TCPHeader. Commands listed in alphabetical order
Category: Search and Mark

title: CONDition
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:FLEXRAY:CONDition
Description: This command sets or queries FLEXRAY bus search trigger condition. The search number is specified by x.
Category: Search and Mark

title: FORMat
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:FLEXRAY:CYCLEcount:FORMat
Description: This command sets or queries the FLEXRAY bus search cycle count format. The search number is specified by x.
Category: Search and Mark

Category: Search and Mark

title: QUALifier
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:MIL1553B:COMMAND:ADDRess:QUALifier
Description: This command specifies the qualifier to use when searching on a MIL-STD-1553 bus command word remote terminal address field. The search condition needs to be set to COMMAND.
Category: Search and Mark

title: VALue
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:MIL1553B:COMMAND:ADDRess:VALue
Description: This command specifies the value of the 5-bit remote terminal address to use in a MIL-STD-1553 bus command word search. The search condition needs to be set to COMMAND.
Category: Search and Mark

title: COUNt
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:MIL1553B:COMMAND:COUNt
Description: This command specifies the bit pattern for the 5-bit Word Count/Mode Code sub-address field to use in a MIL-STD-1553 bus command word trigger. (Use the commandSEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:MIL1553B:COMMAND: SUBADdress to specify Word Count or Mode Code.) In Word Count mode, this field defines the number of data words to be transmitted or received, depending on the T/R bit setting. (Use the commandSEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A: BUS:MIL1553B:COMMAND:TRBit to set the T/R bit.) A word count value of 0 actually indicates a transfer of 32 data words.
Category: Search and Mark

title: FORMat
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:MIL1553B:COMMAND:COUNt:FORMat
Description: This command specifies the data format (binary or hex) of the bit pattern for the 5-bit Word Count/Mode Code field to use in a MIL-STD-1553 bus command word search. The search condition needs to be set to COMMAND.
Category: Search and Mark

title: PARity
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:MIL1553B:COMMAND:PARity
Description: This command specifies the parity to use in a MIL-STD-1553 bus command word search. The search condition needs to be set to COMMAND.
Category: Search and Mark

title: SUBADdress
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:MIL1553B:COMMAND:SUBADdress
Description: This command specifies the 5 bit sub-address to use in a MIL-STD-1553 bus command word sub-address search. When the sub-address value is set to 00000 or 11111 binary, it specifies that the command is a “Mode Code” command. Any other value specifies that it is a “Word Count” command. The default is all X’s (don’t care). The search condition needs to be set to COMMAND.
Category: Search and Mark

title: FORMat
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:MIL1553B:COMMAND:SUBADdress:FORMat
Description: This command specifies the data format (binary or hex) of the sub-address field bit pattern to use in a MIL-STD-1553 bus command word sub-address search. The search condition needs to be set to COMMAND.
Category: Search and Mark

title: TRBit
body:
Command: SEARCH:SEARCH<x>:TRIGger:A:BUS:MIL1553B:COMMAND:TRBit
Description: This command specifies to search on either a “transmit” or a “receive” signal for the transmit/receive bit (bit 9), for a MIL-STD-1553 bus command word search. The search condition needs to be set to COMMAND.
Category: Search and Mark
