CALCulate:MARKer:ADD command.
The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).
Category: CALCulate

title: SYMBol (CALCulate)
body:
Command: CALCulate:CONSte[:MEASview<y>]:MARKer<x>:SYMBol
Description: Queries the symbol readout of the selected marker in the Constellation measurement.
The parameter <x> = 1 to 4; MARKer0 (reference marker) is not valid. The specified marker must be activated using

CALCulate:DPX:MARKer<x>:X[:FREQuency] command.

CALCulate:MARKer:ADD command.

CALCulate:MARKer:ADD command.
The command is only valid for the Spectrum and Frequency plots.

Category: CALCulate

title: Y (CALCulate)
body:

Description: Queries the vertical position of the selected marker in the DPX spectrum view. The data occurrence rate is returned for the
bitmap trace, and the amplitude value for the +peak, -peak, average, and math traces. The horizontal position can be set by the
CALCulate:DPX:MARKer<x>:X:AMPLitude and CALCulate:DPX:MARKer<x>:X[:FREQuency] commands.
The parameter <x> = 1 to 4; MARKer0 (reference marker) is not valid. The specified marker must be activated using the
CALCulate:MARKer:ADD command.

Command: CALCulate:{FSETtling|PSETtling}:MARKer<x>:PEAK:LOWer
Description: Moves the selected marker to the next peak lower in amplitude on the Frequency or Phase Settling trace.
The parameter <x> = 1 to 4; MARKer0 (reference marker) is not valid. The specified marker must be activated using the
CALCulate:MARKer:ADD command.
Category: CALCulate

title: RIGHt (CALCulate)
body:
Command: CALCulate:{FSETtling|PSETtling}:MARKer<x>:PEAK:RIGHt
Description: Moves the selected marker to the next peak to the right on the trace in the Frequency or Phase Settling display.
The parameter <x> = 1 to 4; MARKer0 (reference marker) is not valid. The specified marker must be activated using the
CALCulate:MARKer:ADD command.
Category: CALCulate

title: TRACe (CALCulate)
body:
Command: CALCulate:{FSETtling|PSETtling}:MARKer<x>:TRACe
Description: Sets or queries the trace on which the specified marker is placed in the spectrum measurement.
The parameter <x> = 1 to 4; MARKer0 (reference marker) is not valid. The specified marker must be activated using the
CALCulate:MARKer:ADD command.
Category: CALCulate

title: X (CALCulate)
body:

CALCulate:MARKer:ADD command.

title: Y (CALCulate)
body:

Description: Queries the marker amplitude of the selected marker in the Frequency and Phase Settling display.

CALCulate:MARKer:ADD command.
Category: CALCulate

title: FREQuency (CALCulate)
body:
Command: CALCulate:WLAN:CONSte:MARKer<x>:FREQuency
Description: Sets or queries the frequency value of the marker on the WLAN Constellation display. The units are either Frequency in Hz or Subcarrier
number. To set the units, use the command [SENSe]:WLAN:UNIT:FREQuency.
Note that FREQuency is not an actual measurement of frequency, but is the subcarrier offset within the analyzed packet burst.

<x>, which is optional, is the desired marker to use. 0 indicates MR, the reference marker. 1, 2, 3, etc. indicate markers M1, M2, M3, etc.
If <x> is not supplied, the command works on the currently selected marker. Valid integral values range from 0 to the number of markers
active for the display. Commanding a marker which does not exist results in an execution error.
Category: CALCulate

title: MAGNitude (CALCulate)
body:
Command: CALCulate:WLAN:CONSte:MARKer<x>:MAGNitude
Description: Returns the magnitude value at the indicated marker position on the WLAN Constellation display.
<x> is optional. 0 indicates MR, the reference marker. 1, 2, 3, etc. indicate markers M1, M2, M3, etc. If <x> is not supplied, the command
works on the currently selected marker. Valid integral values range from 0 to the number of markers active for the display. Querying a
marker which does not exist results in an execution error.
Category: CALCulate

title: PHASe (CALCulate)
body:
Command: CALCulate:WLAN:CONSte:MARKer<x>:PHASe
Description: Returns the phase value at the indicated marker position on the WLAN Constellation display, in degrees.
<x>, which is optional, is the desired marker to query. 0 indicates MR, the reference marker. 1, 2, 3, etc. indicate markers M1, M2, M3, etc.
If <x> is not supplied, the command works on the currently selected marker. Valid integral values range from 0 to the number of markers
active for the display. Querying a marker which does not exist results in an execution error.
Category: CALCulate

title: TIME (CALCulate)
body:
Command: CALCulate:WLAN:CONSte:MARKer<x>:TIME
Description: Sets the time offset value at the selected marker position on the points trace on the WLAN Constellation display. The units are either
Symbols or Seconds. To set/query the units, use the command [SENSe]:WLAN:UNIT:TIME.
Note that TIME is a measurement of neither absolute nor elapsed time, but symbol offset within the analyzed packet burst.
<x>, which is optional, is the desired marker. 0 indicates MR, the reference marker. 1, 2, 3, etc. indicate markers M1, M2, M3, etc. If <x> is
not supplied, the command works on the currently selected marker. Valid integral values range from 0 to the number of markers active for
the display. Commanding a marker which does not exist results in an execution error.
Category: CALCulate

title: TYPE (CALCulate)
body:
Command: CALCulate:WLAN:CONSte:MARKer<x>:TYPE
Description: Returns the data type of the subcarrier for data at the indicated marker position - either pilot or data.
<x>, which is optional, is the desired marker. 0 indicates MR, the reference marker. 1, 2, 3, etc. indicate markers M1, M2, M3, etc. If <x> is
not supplied, the command works on the currently selected marker. Valid integral values range from 0 to the number of markers active for
the display. Querying a marker which does not exist results in an execution error.
Category: CALCulate

title: VALue (CALCulate)
body:
Command: CALCulate:WLAN:CONSte:MARKer<x>:VALue
Description: Queries the value readout at the selected marker position on the points trace of the WLAN Constellation display.
<x>, which is optional, is the desired marker to query. 0 indicates MR, the reference marker. 1, 2, 3, etc. indicate markers M1, M2, M3, etc.
If <x> is not supplied, the command works on the currently selected marker. Valid integral
values range from 0 to the number of markers active for the display. Querying a marker which does not exist results in an execution error.
Category: CALCulate

title: X (CALCulate)
body:
Command: CALCulate:WLAN:CRESponse:MARKer<x>:DELTa:X
Description: Queries the delta Subcarrier or frequency value (depending on which units have been set) at the selected marker position on the WLAN
Channel Response display.
Use the command [SENSe]:WLAN:UNIT:FREQuency to set the units.
<x> is the desired marker to query. Marker 0 is not valid for the DELTa queries. 1, 2, 3, etc. indicate markers M1, M2, M3, etc. Valid integral
values range from 1 to the number of markers active for the display. Querying a marker which does not exist results in an execution error.

title: Y (CALCulate)
body:

Description: Queries the vertical delta value for the points trace at the selected marker position on the WLAN Channel Response display, in dB or

<x> is the desired marker to query. Marker 0 is not valid for the DELTa queries. 1, 2, 3, etc. indicate markers M1, M2, M3, etc. Valid integral
values range from 1 to the number of markers active for the display. Querying a marker which does not exist results in an execution error.
Marker 0 and the selected marker must be on the same plot (magnitude or phase); otherwise, it will result in an execution error.

title: ABORt (CALibration)
body:
Command: CALibration:ABORt
Description: Aborts any actions related to the alignments in progress.
Category: CALibration

title: ABORt (CALibration)
body:
Command: CALibration:TXGain:ABORt
Description: Cancels a calibration for the Transmission Gain measurement
Category: CALibration

title: ALL (CALibration)
body:
Command: CALibration:TXGain[:ALL]
Description: Performs a calibration using the current settings for the Transmission Gain measurement
Category: CALibration

title: FINish (CALibration)
body:
Command: CALibration:TXGain:FINish
Description: Queries whether or not the calibration is completed for the Transmission Gain measurement
Category: CALibration

title: STATe (CALibration)
body:
Command: CALibration:TXGain:STATe
Description: Queries the calibration state for the Transmission Gain measurement.
Category: CALibration

title: AUTO (CALibration)
body:
Command: CALibration:AUTO
Description: Sets or queries whether or not to run alignments automatically.
Category: CALibration

title: NEW (CALibration)
body:
Command: CALibration:CORRection:EXTernal:EDIT<x>:NEW
Description: Creates a new external loss table.
The parameter <x> = 1 to 3 represent the External Loss Table 1 to 3, respectively.
Note: This command has been replaced with INPut:CORRection:EXTernal:EDIT<x>:NEW.
Category: CALibration

title: CONNect (CALibration)
body:
Command: CALibration:CORRection:EXTernal:PROBe:CONNect
Description: Queries whether the external probe is connected to the analyzer or not.
Category: CALibration

title: MAGNitude (CALibration)
body:
Command: CALibration:CORRection:EXTernal:PROBe[:MAGNitude]
Description: Queries the external probe attenuation.
Category: CALibration

title: STATe (CALibration)
body:
Command: CALibration:CORRection:EXTernal:PROBe:STATe
Description: Determines whether or not to correct data for the external probe attenuation.
Category: CALibration

title: TYPE (CALibration)
body:
Command: CALibration:CORRection:EXTernal:TYPE
Description: Sets or queries the data type to apply the external loss table corrections.
Note: This command has replaced with INPut:CORRection:EXTernal:TYPE
Category: CALibration

title: LABel (INPut)
body:
Command: INPut:CORRection:EXTernal:EDIT<x>:LABel
Description: Sets or queries the name of the external loss table.
1450
The parameter <x> = 1 to 3 represent the External Loss Table 1 to 3, respectively.
Note: This command has replaced
Category: INPut

title: STATe (INPut)
body:
Command: INPut:CORRection:EXTernal:EDIT<x>:STATe
Description: Determines whether to enable or disable the external loss table.
The parameter <x> = 1 to 3 represent the External Loss Table 1 to 3, respectively.
Note: This command has replaced CALIbration:CORRection:EXTernal:EDIT<x>:STATe
Category: INPut

title: MAGNitude (INPut)
body:
Command: INPut:CORRection:EXTernal:GAIN[:MAGNitude]
Description: Sets or queries the external gain value. It can be enabled or disabled using the INPut:CORRection:EXTernal:GAIN:STATe
command.
Note: This command has replaced CALIbration:CORRection:EXTernal:GAIN[:MAGNitude]
Category: INPut

title: INTerpolation (INPut)
body:
Command: INPut:CORRection:EXTernal:EDIT<x>:INTerpolation
Description: Sets or queries the interpolation setting to use with the indicated external gain table.
Category: INPut

title: NEW (INPut)
body:
Command: INPut:CORRection:EXTernal:EDIT<x>:NEW
Description: Creates the indicated external loss table from a series of frequency and gain value pairs.
Note: This command has replaced CALIbration:CORRection:EXTernal:EDIT<x>:NEW
Category: INPut

title: TYPE (INPut)
body:
Command: INPut:CORRection:EXTernal:TYPE
Description: Sets or queries the data type to use when applying the external loss table corrections.
Note: This command has replaced CALIbration:CORRection:EXTernal:TYPE
Category: INPut

title: MLEVel|RLEVel (INPut)
ATTenuation (INPut)

Command: INPut[:RF]:ATTenuation

Category: INPut

title: STATe (INPut)
body:

Description: Determines whether to enable or disable to monitor attenuator use.
Category: INPut

title: TYPE (INPut)
body:
Command: INPut:TYPE
Description: Sets or queries the source type.

Condition
Measurement views: All
Category: INPut

title: MEASurement (DISPlay)
body:
Command: DISPlay:WINDow:OPTimized:MEASurement
Description: Queries the measurement views that are optimized. "Optimized" means that there is a perfect match between the view's settings and the
actual acquisition parameters to meet the specifications. When multiple measurements are running at one time, the measurements can

have different requirements for setting the acquisition hardware. You can make a measurement optimized by selecting it using the following
commands:
• DISPlay:GENeral:MEASview:SELect for the general signal viewing
• DISPlay:DDEMod:MEASview:SELect for the digital modulation views
• DISPlay:GPRF:MEASview:SELect for the RF measurement views
• DISPlay:PULSe:MEASview:SELect for the pulsed RF measurement views
Category: DISPlay

title: SELect (DISPlay)
body:
Command: DISPlay:DDEMod[:MEASview<y>]:SELect
Description: Selects a measurement view in the general purpose digital modulation measurements on the screen. The query command returns the
currently selected view.
Selecting a measurement optimizes it. Other measurements may be optimized as a side effect. Refer to
DISPlay:WINDow:OPTimized:MEASurement? query.
The parameter <y> = 1 to 8 represents measurement index based on number of sources of connected instrument, where MEASview
keyword is optional. Default value is 1.
Category: DISPlay

Command: DISPlay:PULSe:MEASview<y>[:SOURce<x>]:NEW
Description: Selects a measurement view in the pulsed RF measurements on the screen. The query command returns the currently selected view.
Category: DISPlay

Description: Selects a measurement view in the pulsed RF measurements on the screen. The query command returns the currently selected view.
Category: DISPlay

conditions
Measurement views: Spectrum and DPX

Vertical offset = 0 dBm, Vertical scale = 100 dB,
Horizontal offset = Center frequency, and Horizontal scale = Default span

title: STATe (DISPlay)
body:

The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).

8 channels for MSO58).

8 channels for MSO58).

title: STATe (DISPlay)
body:
Command: DISPlay:WLAN:CRESponse:WINDow:TRACe:GRATicule:GRID:STATe
Description: Sets or queries whether to show the graticule grid on the WLAN Channel Response analysis display.
Category: DISPlay

title: AUTO (DISPlay)
body:
Command: DISPlay:WLAN:EVM:FREQuency:AUTO
Description: Automatically rescales the horizontal subcarrier (frequency) start and stop values to fit the waveform to the screen in the WLAN EVM
display.
Category: DISPlay

title: RESet (DISPlay)
body:
Command: DISPlay:WLAN:EVM:FREQuency:RESet
Description: Automatically resets the horizontal subcarrier (frequency) start and stop values for the WLAN EVM display.
Category: DISPlay

title: STARt (DISPlay)
body:
Command: DISPlay:WLAN:EVM:FREQuency:STARt
Description: Specifies or queries the horizontal start value (left edge) on the WLAN EVM display. The units are either Frequency (Hz) or Subcarriers,
and are set using the command [SENSe]:WLAN:UNIT:FREQuency.
Category: DISPlay

title: STOP (DISPlay)
body:
Command: DISPlay:WLAN:EVM:FREQuency:STOP
Description: Specifies or queries the horizontal stop value (right edge) on the WLAN EVM display. The units are either Frequency (Hz) or Subcarriers,
and are set using the command [SENSe]:WLAN:UNIT:FREQuency.
Category: DISPlay

title: STATe (DISPlay)
body:
Command: DISPlay:WLAN:EVM:MARKer:SHOW:STATe
Description: Shows or hides the Marker readout on the WLAN EVM display.
Category: DISPlay

title: AUTO (DISPlay)
body:
Command: DISPlay:WLAN:EVM[:SCALe]:AUTO
Description: Automatically adjusts all vertical and horizontal values to provide the best display.
Category: DISPlay

title: AUTO (DISPlay)
body:
Command: DISPlay:WLAN:EVM:TIME:AUTO
Description: Sets the horizontal start and stop values to fit the waveform to the WLAN EVM display, and also enables Auto horizontal scaling so the
waveform will remain properly scaled as burst lengths change.
Category: DISPlay

title: STATe (DISPlay)
body:
Command: DISPlay:WLAN:EVM:TIME:AUTO:STATe
Description: When enabled, sets the horizontal symbol scale value automatically for the WLAN EVM display.
Category: DISPlay

title: RESet (DISPlay)
body:
Command: DISPlay:WLAN:EVM:TIME:RESet
Description: Resets the horizontal symbol start and stop values for the WLAN EVM display.
Category: DISPlay

title: STARt (DISPlay)
body:
Command: DISPlay:WLAN:EVM:TIME:STARt
Description: Sets or queries the horizontal symbol start value for the WLAN EVM display. The units are either Symbols or Seconds, and are set using
the command [SENSe]:WLAN:UNIT:TIME.
Category: DISPlay

title: STOP (DISPlay)
body:
Command: DISPlay:WLAN:EVM:TIME:STOP
Description: Sets or queries the horizontal symbol stop value for the WLAN EVM display. The units are either Symbols or Seconds, and are set using
the command [SENSe]:WLAN:UNIT:TIME.
Category: DISPlay

title: PLOT (DISPlay)
body:
Command: DISPlay:WLAN:EVM:WINDow:SELect:PLOT
Description: Specifies or queries which graphs are displayed in the WLAN EVM display. Select from Symbols, Subcarriers, or Both.
To set the Time units, use [SENSe]:WLAN:UNIT:TIME. To set the Frequency units, use [SENSe]:WLAN:UNIT:FREQuency. To set the
Radix, use [SENSe]:WLAN:RADix.
NOTE.
Use this command only with multicarrier standards and not singlecarrier (802.11b).
Category: DISPlay

title: STATe (DISPlay)
body:
Command: DISPlay:WLAN:EVM:WINDow:TRACe:GRATicule:GRID:STATe
Description: Shows or hides the graticule grid on the screen.
Category: DISPlay

title: AUTO (DISPlay)
body:
Command: DISPlay:WLAN:EVM:Y:AUTO
Description: Automatically sets the vertical scale and position values for the WLAN EVM display.
Category: DISPlay

title: OFFSet (DISPlay)
body:
Command: DISPlay:WLAN:EVM:Y:OFFSet
Description: Sets or queries the value of the offset from the center (vertical position), in percent terms, for the WLAN EVM display. To set the vertical
scale, use the command DISPlay:WLAN:EVM:Y[:SCALe].
942
Category: DISPlay

title: RESet (DISPlay)
body:
Command: DISPlay:WLAN:EVM:Y:RESet
Description: Resets the vertical scale and position (offset) values for the WLAN EVM display.
Category: DISPlay

title: SCALe (DISPlay)
body:
Command: DISPlay:WLAN:EVM:Y[:SCALe]
Description: Sets or queries the vertical scale value for the WLAN EVM display. To set the vertical position (offset) value, use the command
DISPlay:WLAN:EVM:Y: OFFSet.
Category: DISPlay

title: AUTO (DISPlay)
body:
Command: DISPlay:WLAN:FLATness:AUTO
Description: Automatically rescales the horizontal and vertical axes for the best display in the WLAN Spectral Flatness view.
Note: WLAN Spectral Flatness is only available for OFDM (non-802.11b) signals.
Category: DISPlay

title: AUTO (DISPlay)
body:
Command: DISPlay:WLAN:FLATness:X:AUTO
Description: Rescales the horizontal axis automatically in the WLAN Spectral Flatness display.
Note: WLAN Spectral Flatness is only available for OFDM (non-802.11b) signals.
Category: DISPlay

title: OFFSet (DISPlay)
body:
Command: DISPlay:WLAN:FLATness:X:OFFSet
Description: Sets or queries the value of the offset of the horizontal axis from the center of the WLAN Spectral Flatness display.
Note: WLAN Spectral Flatness is only available for OFDM (non-802.11b) signals.
Category: DISPlay

title: SCALe (DISPlay)
body:
Command: DISPlay:WLAN:FLATness:X[:SCALe]
Description: Sets or queries the horizontal scale value in the WLAN Spectral Flatness display. The units are Frequency or Subcarrier. To set the units,
use [SENSe]:WLAN:UNIT:FREQuency
Note: WLAN Spectral Flatness is only available for OFDM (non-802.11b) signals.
Category: DISPlay

title: AUTO (DISPlay)
body:
Command: DISPlay:WLAN:FLATness:Y:AUTO
Description: Automatically rescales the vertical axis scale and position values in the WLAN Spectral Flatness display.
Note: WLAN Spectral Flatness is only available for OFDM (non-802.11b) signals.
Category: DISPlay

title: OFFSet (DISPlay)
body:
Command: DISPlay:WLAN:FLATness:Y:OFFSet
Description: Sets or queries the value of the offset from the center (vertical position), in percent terms, for the WLAN Spectral Flatness display. To set
the Vertical Scale, use the command DISPlay:WLAN:FLATness:Y[:SCALe].
Note: WLAN Spectral Flatness is only available for OFDM (non-802.11b) signals.
Category: DISPlay

title: SCALe (DISPlay)
body:
Command: DISPlay:WLAN:FLATness:Y[:SCALe]
Description: Sets or queries the vertical scale value for the WLAN Spectral Flatness display. To set the vertical position (offset) value, use the command
Category: DISPlay

title: DELete (DISPlay)
body:
Command: DISPlay:WLAN:MEASview:DELete
Description: Deletes the selected measurement view in the WLAN display.
If you attempt to delete a view that is currently displayed on screen, the error (-200, "Execution error; Measurement is already running") will
be returned.
Category: DISPlay

title: NEW (DISPlay)
body:
Command: DISPlay:WLAN:MEASview:NEW
Description: Displays a new measurement view in the WLAN display.
If you attempt to open a view that is currently displayed on screen, the error (-200, "Execution error; Measurement is already running") will
be returned.
Category: DISPlay

title: SELect (DISPlay)
body:
Command: DISPlay:WLAN:MEASview:SELect
Description: Selects a measurement view on the screen in the WLAN measurements. The query command returns the currently selected view.
If you attempt to select a view that is not displayed on screen, the error (-200, "Execution error; Measurement is already running") will be
returned.
Category: DISPlay

title: SCALe (DISPlay)
body:
Command: DISPlay:LTE:CONSte:SCALe
Description: Sets or queries the scale (zoom) in the LTE Constellation display.
Category: DISPlay

title: OFFSet (DISPlay)
body:
Command: DISPlay:LTE:CONSte:X:OFFSet
Description: Sets or queries the horizontal offset in the LTE Constellation display.
Category: DISPlay

title: OFFSet (DISPlay)
body:
Command: DISPlay:LTE:CONSte:Y:OFFSet
Description: Sets or queries the vertical offset in the LTE Constellation display.
Category: DISPlay

title: DELete (DISPlay)
body:
Command: DISPlay:LTE:MEASview<y>:DELete
Description: Deletes the selected LTE measurement display in the application
Category: DISPlay

title: NEW (DISPlay)
body:
Command: DISPlay:LTE:MEASview<y>:NEW
Description: Opens a new LTE measurement display
Category: DISPlay

title: SELect (DISPlay)
body:
Command: DISPlay:LTE:MEASview<y>:SELect
Description: Selects an LTE measurement display in the application. The query returns the currently selected display
Category: DISPlay

title: STATe (DISPlay)
body:
Command: DISPlay:LTE:PVTime:MARKer:SHOW:STATe
Description: Shows or hides the marker readout on the LTE Power vs. Time display.
Category: DISPlay

title: STATe (DISPlay)
body:
Command: DISPlay:LTE:PVTime:WINDow:TRACe:GRATicule:GRID:STATe
Description: Determines whether to show or hide the graticule in the LTE Power vs Time display.
Category: DISPlay

title: SCALe (DISPlay)
body:
Command: DISPlay:LTE:PVTime:X:SCALe
Description: Sets or queries the vertical range of the LTE Power vs Time graph.
Category: DISPlay

title: AUTO (DISPlay)
body:
Command: DISPLay:LTE:PVTime:X:SCALe:AUTO
Description: Rescales the horizontal axis automatically to fit the waveform to the screen in the LTE Power vs Time display.
Category: DISPlay

title: RESet (DISPlay)
body:
Command: DISPlay:LTE:PVTime:X:SCALe:RESet
Description: Resets the horizontal scale to the default value for the time measurements for the LTE Power vs Time display.
Category: DISPlay

title: SCALe (DISPlay)
body:
Command: DISPlay:LTE:PVTime:Y:SCALe
Description: Sets or queries the horizontal range of the LTE Power vs Time graph.
Category: DISPlay

title: AUTO (DISPlay)
body:
Command: DISPlay:LTE:PVTime:Y:SCALe:AUTO
Description: Rescales the vertical scale automatically to fit the waveform on the screen in the LTE Power vs Time display.
Category: DISPlay

title: OFFSet (DISPlay)
body:
Command: DISPlay:LTE:PVTime:Y:SCALe:OFFSet
Description: Sets or queries the vertical offset (top edge of the vertical axis) of the trace in the LTE Power vs Time display.
Category: DISPlay

title: PDIVision (DISPlay)
body:
Command: DISPlay:LTE:PVTime:Y:SCALe:PDIVision
Description: Sets or queries the value of the vertical scale for the LTE Power vs. Time display in dB/division. This is only a visual control for panning the
graph.
Category: DISPlay

title: RESet (DISPlay)
body:
Command: DISPlay:LTE:PVTime:Y:SCALe:RESet
Description: Resets the vertical scale and position (offset) to the default value for the time measurements for the LTE Power vs Time display.
Category: DISPlay

title: AVERage (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:TXGain:AVERage
Description: Returns the mean value of the trace.
Category: FETCh

title: MAX (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:TXGain:MAX
Description: Returns the maximum trace value.
Category: FETCh

title: MAXLocation (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:TXGain:MAXLocation
Description: Returns the frequency of the maximum trace value.
Category: FETCh

title: MIN (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:TXGain:MIN
Description: Returns the minimum trace value.
Category: FETCh

title: MINLocation (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:TXGain:MINLocation
Description: Returns the frequency of the minimum trace value.
Category: FETCh

title: TRACex (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:TXGain:TRACe<x>
Description: Returns the trace data of the selected trace.
Category: FETCh

title: IQ (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:RFIN:IQ
Description: Returns time-domain IQ data for a specific acquisition data record. You can set a range of IQ pairs optionally. The instrument needs to be
in stopped mode. If not in stopped mode, it returns the execution error (-200).
Category: FETCh

title: FTX (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:BIBEmissions:FTX
Description: Returns the band number corresponding to the transmitted FTX detected in the Bluetooth InBand Emission display.
Category: FETCh

title: POWer (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:BIBEmissions:POWer
Description: Returns all channel power results from the Bluetooth InBand Emission display.
Category: FETCh

title: STATus (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:BIBEmissions:RESUlts:STATus
Description: Returns the Pass/Fail result status of the Bluetooth InBand Emission display.
Category: FETCh

title: FERRor (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:BLUEtooth:CONSte:FERRor
Description: Returns the frequency error in Hz in the Bluetooth Constellation. The frequency error is the difference between the measured carrier
frequency of the signal and the user-selected center frequency.
Category: FETCh

title: TRACe (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:BLUEtooth:CONSte:TRACe
Description: Queries the Bluetooth Constellation trace data.
Category: FETCh

title: FDEViation (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:BLUEtooth:EDIagram:FDEViation
Description: Returns the frequency deviation vs. time trace data with the X values in the Bluetooth eye diagram.
Category: FETCh

title: FERRor (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:BLUEtooth:EDIagram:FERRor
Description: Returns the frequency error in the Bluetooth eye diagram measurement.
Category: FETCh

title: SCALe (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:BLUEtooth:EDIagram:HORiz:SCALe
Description: Returns the value of the horizontal scale in the Bluetooth eye diagram.
Category: FETCh

title: I (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:BLUEtooth:EDIagram:I
Description: Returns the I vs. time trace in the Bluetooth eye diagram.
Category: FETCh

title: Q (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:BLUEtooth:EDIagram:Q
Description: Returns the Q vs. time trace in the Bluetooth eye diagram.
Category: FETCh

title: TRACe (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:BLUEtooth:FDVTime:TRACe
Description: Returns the Bluetooth Frequency Deviation vs. Time trace data.
Category: FETCh

title: ERROr (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:BLUEtooth:FREQuency:ERROr
Description: Returns the frequency error from the Bluetooth Constellation diagram.
Category: FETCh

title: TYPE (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:BLUEtooth:FREQuency:ERROr:TYPE
Description: Returns the frequency error type in the Bluetooth Constellation display.
Category: FETCh

title: F1FZero (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:BLUEtooth:FREQuency:OFFSet:DRIFt:F1FZero
Description: Returns the drift in frequency offset (f1–f0 — the frequency offset calculated in the first interval in the payload — the frequency offset
calculated in the preamble) in the Bluetooth Frequency Deviation vs. Time display.
Category: FETCh

title: FNFN5 (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:BLUEtooth:FREQuency:OFFSet:DRIFt:FNFN5
Description: Returns the maximum drift of the frequency offset in payload intervals spaced 50 μs away in the Bluetooth Frequency Deviation vs. Time
display.
Category: FETCh

title: INTErval (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:BLUEtooth:FREQuency:OFFSet:DRIFt:FNFN5:INTErval
Description: Returns the interval at which the maximum drift of frequency offset in a duration of 50 μs occurred.
Category: FETCh

title: FNFZERO (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:BLUEtooth:FREQuency:OFFSet:DRIFt:FNFZERO
Description: Returns the maximum drift of the frequency offset of the intervals in the payload from the preamble packet in the Bluetooth Frequency
Deviation vs. Time display.
Category: FETCh

title: INTErval (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:BLUEtooth:FREQuency:OFFSet:DRIFt:FNFZERO:INTErval
Description: Returns the interval (n) at which the maximum drift fn-f0 occurred in the Bluetooth Frequency Deviation vs. Time display. (The fn-f0
indicates the drift of the frequency offset calculated in each payload interval from the offset calculated in the preamble.)
Category: FETCh

title: MAX (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:BLUEtooth:FREQuency:OFFSet:MAX
Description: Returns the maximum frequency offset in the Frequency Deviation vs. Time and the Center Frequency Offset and Drift displays.
Category: FETCh

title: INTErval (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:BLUEtooth:FREQuency:OFFSet:MAX:INTErval
Description: Returns the maximum frequency offset interval in the Frequency Deviation vs. Time and the Center Frequency Offset and Drift displays.
Category: FETCh

title: PREAmble (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:BLUEtooth:FREQuency:OFFSet:PREAmble
Description: Returns the offset calculated in the preamble region in the Frequency Deviation vs. Time and the Center Frequency Offset and Drift
displays.
1018
Category: FETCh

title: F (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:BLUEtooth:MODUlation:CHARacteristics:AVErage:F
Description: Returns the average frequency value when the selected test pattern is Other in the Bluetooth Frequency Deviation vs. Time display.
Category: FETCh

title: F2F1ratio (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:BLUEtooth:MODUlation:CHARacteristics:AVErage:F2F1ratio
Description: Returns the average ratio value for the high and low frequency deviation in the Bluetooth Frequency deviation versus Time display
Category: FETCh

title: FONE (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:BLUEtooth:MODUlation:CHARacteristics:AVErage:FONE
Description: Returns the average frequency deviation for the low deviation pattern in the Bluetooth Frequency deviation versus Time display
Category: FETCh

title: FTWO (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:BLUEtooth:MODUlation:CHARacteristics:AVErage:FTWO
Description: Returns the average frequency deviation for the high deviation pattern in the Bluetooth Frequency Deviation versus. Time display
Category: FETCh

title: COUNt (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:BLUEtooth:SUMMary:FREQuency:OFFSet:DRIFt:COUNt
Description: Returns the number of packets analyzed to obtain average drift results from the Bluetooth summary.
Category: FETCh

title: F1FZero (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:BLUEtooth:SUMMary:FREQuency:OFFSet:DRIFt:F1FZero
Description: Returns the drift f1–f0 from the Bluetooth summary.
Category: FETCh

title: STATus (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:BLUEtooth:SUMMary:FREQuency:OFFSet:DRIFt:F1FZero:STATus
Description: Returns the Pass/Fail drift f1–f0 result status in the packet from the Bluetooth summary.
Category: FETCh

title: FNFN5 (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:BLUEtooth:SUMMary:FREQuency:OFFSet:DRIFt:FNFN5
Description: Returns the maximum drift of the fn–fn-5 value from the Bluetooth summary.
Category: FETCh

title: STATus (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:BLUEtooth:SUMMary:FREQuency:OFFSet:DRIFt:FNFN5:STATus
Description: Returns the Pass/Fail status of the maximum fn–fn-5 drift in the packet from the Bluetooth summary.
Category: FETCh

title: FNFZero (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:BLUEtooth:SUMMary:FREQuency:OFFSet:DRIFt:FNFZero
Description: Returns the maximum drift fn–f0 from the Bluetooth summary.
Category: FETCh

title: STATus (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:BLUEtooth:SUMMary:FREQuency:OFFSet:DRIFt:FNFZero:STATus
Description: Returns the Pass/Fail result status of the fn–f0 maximum drift in the packet from the Bluetooth summary.
Category: FETCh

title: MAX (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:BLUEtooth:SUMMary:FREQuency:OFFSet:MAX
Description: Returns the maximum frequency offset present in the packet from the Bluetooth summary.
Category: FETCh

title: STATus (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:BLUEtooth:SUMMary:FREQuency:OFFSet:MAX:STATus
Description: Returns the Pass/Fail result status of the frequency offset maximum in the packet from the Bluetooth summary.
Category: FETCh

title: PREAmble (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:BLUEtooth:SUMMary:FREQuency:OFFSet:PREAmble

Category: FETCh

title: STATus (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:BLUEtooth:SUMMary:FREQuency:OFFSet:PREAmble:STATus
Description: Returns the Pass/Fail result status of the frequency offset in the preamble region.
1030
Category: FETCh

title: COUNt (FETCh)

title: COUNt (FETCh)
body:

title: STATus (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:BLUEtooth:SUMMary:MODUlation:CHARacteristics:AVErage:DELTa:FTWO:MAX:PERCentage:STATus
Description: Returns the Pass/Fail result status of the ΔF2Max% field
Category: FETCh

title: F (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:BLUEtooth:SUMMary:MODUlation:CHARacteristics:AVErage:F
Description: Returns the ΔFavg value from the Bluetooth summary.
Category: FETCh

title: F2F1ratio (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:BLUEtooth:SUMMary:MODUlation:CHARacteristics:AVErage:F2F1ratio
Description: Returns the ratio of the ΔF2avg to ΔF1avg from the Bluetooth summary.
1034
Category: FETCh

title: STATus (FETCh)

Category: FETCh

title: FONE (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:BLUEtooth:SUMMary:MODUlation:CHARacteristics:AVErage:FONE
Description: Returns the ΔF1avg value from the Bluetooth summary.
Category: FETCh

title: STATus (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:BLUEtooth:SUMMary:MODUlation:CHARacteristics:AVErage:FONE:STATus
Description: Returns the Pass/Fail results status of the ΔF1avg
Category: FETCh

title: FTWO (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:BLUEtooth:SUMMary:MODUlation:CHARacteristics:AVErage:FTWO
Description: Returns the ΔF2avg value from the Bluetooth summary.

title: AVErage (FETCh)
body:

body:

body:

Command: FETCh:BLUEtooth:SUMMary:PACKet:BDR:PAYLoad:LENgth

body:

Command: FETCh:BLUEtooth:SUMMary:PACKet:BLE:PDU:HEADer:LENGth

body:

body:

body:
Command: FETCh:BLUEtooth:SUMMary:PACKet:BLE:PDU:HEADer:TYPE

body:
Command: FETCh:BLUEtooth:SUMMary:PACKet:BLE:PREAmble

title: RESult (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:NOISe:GAIN:TRACe<x>:RESult
Description: This query returns the gain value in mdB of the specified trace in the Gain display.
Category: FETCh

title: VALue (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:NOISe:TABLe:VALue
Description: Returns Noise Table data. A total of 7 values per frequency (28 bytes per frequency) is returned. The number of frequencies returned
depends on the settings.
Category: FETCh

title: XDBBandwidth (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:OBWidth:BOBW:XDBBandwidth
Description: Returns the difference between the higher and lower frequency points corresponding to the value where it is X dB less from the value at
the center frequency.
Set the measurement direction using the appropriate inward or outward command before issuing this command.
Category: FETCh

title: BANDwidth (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:OBWidth:BOBW:XDBMbandwidth:IN:BANDwidth
Description: Returns the difference between the higher and lower frequency points corresponding to the value which it is the X dBm value measured
inwards (coming from the ends to the center frequency).
Category: FETCh

title: FREQuency (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:OBWidth:BOBW:XDBMbandwidth:IN:LEFT:FREQuency
Description: Returns the frequency corresponding to the X dBm value to the left of the center frequency measured in the inward direction.
Category: FETCh

title: FREQuency (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:OBWidth:BOBW:XDBMbandwidth:IN:RIGHt:FREQuency
Description: Returns the frequency corresponding to the X dBm value to the right of the center frequency measured in the inward direction.
Category: FETCh

title: BANDwidth (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:OBWidth:BOBW:XDBMbandwidth:OUT:BANDwidth
Description: Returns the difference between the higher and lower frequency points corresponding to the value which it is the X dBm value measured
outwards (from the center frequency to the ends).
Category: FETCh

title: FREQuency (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:OBWidth:BOBW:XDBMbandwidth:OUT:LEFT:FREQuency
Description: Returns the frequency corresponding to the X dBm value to the left of the center frequency measured in the outward direction.
Category: FETCh

title: FREQuency (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:OBWidth:BOBW:XDBMbandwidth:OUT:RIGHt:FREQuency
Description: Returns the frequency corresponding to the X dBm value to the right of the center frequency measured in the outward direction.
Category: FETCh

title: FERRor (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:P25:CONSte:FERRor
Description: Returns the frequency error in Hz. The frequency error is the difference between the measured carrier frequency of the signal and the
user-selected center frequency of the analyzer.
When Frequency Error is in Auto mode, this query returns the frequency error value. When in Manual mode, it returns the frequency offset.
Category: FETCh

title: TRACe (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:P25:CONSte:TRACe
Description: Returns the P25 Constellation display trace data.
Category: FETCh

title: FDEViation (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:P25:EDIagram:FDEViation
Description: Returns the Frequency Deviation versus Time trace data with the X value
Category: FETCh

title: FERRor (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:P25:EDIagram:FERRor
Description: Returns the frequency error in the P25 Eye Diagram display.
When Frequency Error is in Auto mode, this query returns the frequency error value. When in Manual mode, it returns the frequency offset.
Category: FETCh

title: I (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:P25:EDIagram:I
Description: Returns the I versus Time trace data in the P25 Eye Diagram display.
Category: FETCh

title: WIDTh (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:P25:PVTime:BURSt:WIDTh
Description: Returns the value of the measured time width of the burst packet from poweron to powerdown (or the end of waveform, if that occurs
before powerdown), in seconds, for the P25 Power vs. Time trace.
Category: FETCh

title: ACCUracy (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCH:P25:SUMMary:MODUlation:MEASurement:FREQuency:ACCUracy
Description: Returns the Operational Frequency Accuracy measurement results in the P25 Summary display.
Category: FETCh

title: STATus (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:P25:SUMMary:MODUlation:MEASurement:FREQuency:ACCUracy:STATus
Description: Returns the status of Frequency Accuracy measurement’s result.
Category: FETCh

title: NEGPeak (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCH:P25:SUMMary:MODUlation:MEASurement:FREQuency:DEVIation:NEGPeak
Description: Returns the Frequency Deviation, negative peak, measurement results
Category: FETCh

title: STATus (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:P25:SUMMary:MODUlation:MEASurement:FREQuency:DEVIation:NEGPeak:STATus
Description: Returns the Status of Frequency Deviation, negative peak measurement status
Category: FETCh

title: POSPeak (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCH:P25:SUMMary:MODUlation:MEASurement:FREQuency:.DEVIation:POSPeak
Description: Returns the status of Frequency Deviation, positive peak resutls
Category: FETCh

title: STATus (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:P25:SUMMary:MODUlation:MEASurement:FREQuency:.DEVIation:POSPeak:STATus

body:

Description: Returns the header information for a specific acquisition data record. The instrument needs to be in stopped mode. If not in stopped mode,
it returns the execution error (-200).

body:

Description: Returns the scaling factor contained in the .tiq file header
Category: FETCh

Description: Returns the beginning and end ID numbers of acquisition data
Category: FETCh

body:

Description: Returns the Channel power and ACPR measurement results for all available channels.
Category: FETCh

body:
Command: FETCh:ACPower:CHANnel:POWer
Description: Returns the average power of the main channel (power reference) in the Channel power and ACPR measurement.
Category: FETCh

Description: Returns spectrum trace data of the Channel power and ACPR measurement.

1090

Category: FETCh

title: FERRor (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:AUDio:FERRor
Description: Returns the carrier frequency error in the audio measurement. This command is only available for FM and PM measurements.
Category: FETCh

title: FREQuency (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:AUDio:FREQuency
Description: Returns the audio frequency.
Category: FETCh

title: COUNt (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:AUDio:HARMonic:COUNt
Description: Returns the number of harmonics in the audio spectrum measurement.
The number of harmonics appear in the results table in the audio spectrum view.
Category: FETCh

title: AMPLitude (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:AUDio:HARMonic<x>:AMPLitude
Description: Returns the amplitude of the specified harmonic in the audio spectrum measurement.
The value <x> represents the specific harmonic, from 1 to the value specified by the CALCulate:AUDio:HARMonic:HNUMber
command.
Category: FETCh

title: FREQuency (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:AUDio:HARMonic<x>:FREQuency
Description: Returns the frequency of the specified harmonic in the audio spectrum measurement.
The value <x> represents the specific harmonic, from 1 to the value specified by the CALCulate:AUDio:HARMonic:HNUMber
command.
Category: FETCh

title: HNOise (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:AUDio:HNOise
Description: Returns the difference between the current RMS modulation value and the reference value.
This information is only available when the Hum & Noise is measurement is enabled; see the [SENSe]:AUDio:HNOise:ENABle
command. The reference value is stored after capturing the reference by pressing the Capture Reference button or by enabling the
[SENSe]:AUDio:HNOise:REFerence command.
Category: FETCh

title: HNREFerence (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:AUDio:HNREFerence
Description: Returns the Hum and Noise RMS modulation reference value.
988
The value is stored after capturing the reference by pressing the Capture Reference button or by enabling the
[SENSe]:AUDio:HNOise:REFerence command. This information is only available when the Hum & Noise measurement
is enabled; see the [SENSe]:AUDio:HNOise:ENABle command.
For AM signal types, the reference represents modulation depth. For FM signal types, the reference represents frequency deviation. For
PM signal types the reference represents phase. For Direct signal types the reference represents power.
Category: FETCh

title: HPTPeak (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:AUDio:HPTPeak
Description: Returns the half peak-to-peak modulation excursion (where the modulation excursion depends on the signal type).
For AM signal types, the modulation excursion is “% Modulation Depth.” For FM signal types, the modulation excursion is “Frequency
Deviation.” For PM signal types, the modulation excursion is “Phase Deviation.” For Direct signal types, there is no modulation excursion; it
is actually “signal excursion.”
Category: FETCh

title: MODDist (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:AUDio:MODDist
Description: Returns the modulation distortion for the audio measurement.
Category: FETCh

title: COUNt (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:AUDio:NHARmonic:COUNt
Description: Returns the number of non-harmonics in the audio spectrum measurement.
The number of non-harmonics appear in the results table in the audio spectrum view.
Category: FETCh

title: AMPLitude (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:AUDio:NHARmonic<x>:AMPLitude
Description: Returns the amplitude of the specified non-harmonic in the audio spectrum measurement.
The value <x> represents the specific non-harmonic, from 1 to the value specified by the
CALCulate:AUDio:HARMonic:NHNumber command.
Category: FETCh

title: FREQuency (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:AUDio:NHARmonic<x>:FREQuency
Description: Returns the frequency of the specified non-harmonic in the audio spectrum measurement.
The value <x> represents the specific non-harmonic, from 1 to the value specified by the
CALCulate:AUDio:HARMonic:NHNumber command.
Category: FETCh

title: PNEGative (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:AUDio:PNEGative
Description: Returns the minus peak modulation excursion (where the modulation excursion depends on the signal type).
For AM signal types, the modulation excursion is “% Modulation Depth.” For FM signal types, the modulation excursion is “Frequency
Deviation.” For PM signal types, the modulation excursion is “Phase Deviation.” For Direct signal types, there is no modulation excursion; it
is actually “signal excursion.”
Category: FETCh

title: POWer (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:AUDio:POWer
Description: Returns the carrier power or signal power depending the signal type.
If the signal type is Direct, the returned value is the average power of the input signal. If the signal type is AM, FM, or PM, the returned
value is the carrier power; the average power of the carrier signal with the modulation removed.
Category: FETCh

title: PPOSitive (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:AUDio:PPOSitive
Description: Returns the positive peak modulation excursion (where the modulation excursion depends on the signal type).
For AM signal types, the modulation excursion is “% Modulation Depth.” For FM signal types, the modulation excursion is “Frequency
Deviation.” For PM signal types, the modulation excursion is “Phase Deviation.” For Direct signal types, there is no modulation excursion; it
is actually “signal excursion.”
Category: FETCh

title: RMS (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:AUDio:RMS
Description: Returns the RMS modulation excursion for the audio measurement (where the modulation excursion depends on the signal type).
For AM signal types, the modulation excursion is “% Modulation Depth.” For FM signal types, the modulation excursion is “Frequency
Deviation.” For PM signal types, the modulation excursion is “Phase Deviation.” For Direct signal types, there is no modulation excursion; it
is actually “signal excursion.”
Category: FETCh

title: SINad (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:AUDio:SINad
Description: Returns the signal-to-noise and distortion for the audio measurement.
994
Category: FETCh

title: SNOise (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:AUDio:SNOise
Description: Returns the signal level to noise level with the harmonic distortion and non-harmonic distortion components removed.
Category: FETCh

title: TRACex (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:AUDio:SPECtrum:TRACe<x>
Description: Returns the audio spectrum trace data for the audio measurement.

The <x> represents trace 1 for this measurement. No other traces are supported.
Category: FETCh

title: THDist (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:AUDio:THDist
Description: Returns the percentage of the total harmonic distortion in the audio measurement.
Category: FETCh

title: DB (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:AUDio:THDist:DB
Description: Returns the total harmonic distortion in dB in the audio measurement.
Category: FETCh

title: TNHDist (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:AUDio:TNHDist
Description: Returns the percentage of the total non-harmonic distortion in the audio measurement.
Category: FETCh

title: DB (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:AUDio:TNHDist:DB
Description: Returns the total non-harmonic distortion in dB in the audio measurement.
Category: FETCh

title: AVERage (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:AVTime[:MEASview<y>]:AVERage
Description: Returns the RMS (root-mean-square) value for the selected trace in the Amplitude versus Time measurement. Select the trace using the
TRACe<x>:AVTime[:MEASview<y>]:SELect command.
998
Category: FETCh

title: FIRSt|SECond|THIRd|FOURth (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:AVTime[:MEASview<y>]:{FIRSt|SECond|THIRd|FOURth}
Description: Returns the trace data in the Amplitude versus Time measurement.
The mnemonics FIRSt, SECond, THIRd, and FOURth represent Trace 1, Trace 2, Trace 3, and Math trace, respectively. The traces can be
specified by the TRACe<x>:AVTime command subgroup.
Category: FETCh

title: MAXLocation (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:AVTime[:MEASview<y>]:MAXLocation
Description: Returns the time at which the amplitude is maximum for the selected trace in the Amplitude versus Time measurement. Select the trace
using the TRACe<x>:AVTime[:MEASview<y>]:SELect command.
1000
Category: FETCh

title: MINimum (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:AVTime[:MEASview<y>]:MINimum
Description: Returns the minimum value for the selected trace in the Amplitude versus Time measurement. Select the trace using the
TRACe<x>:AVTime[:MEASview<y>]:SELect command.
Category: FETCh

title: MINLocation (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:AVTime[:MEASview<y>]:MINLocation
Description: Returns the time at which the amplitude is minimum for the selected trace in the Amplitude versus Time measurement. Select the trace
using the TRACe<x>:AVTime[:MEASview<y>]:SELect command.
Category: FETCh

title: RESult (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:AVTime[:MEASview<y>]:RESult
Description: Returns the measurement results for the selected trace in the Amplitude versus Time measurement. Select the trace using the
TRACe<x>:AVTime[:MEASview<y>]:SELect command.
Category: FETCh

title: CCDF (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:CCDF
Description: Returns the CCDF measurement results.
Category: FETCh

title: X (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:CCDF:{FIRSt|SECond|THIRd}:X
Description: Returns the horizontal values of the specified trace in the CCDF measurement.
The mnemonics FIRSt, SECond, and THIRd represent Trace 1, Trace 2, and Gaussian reference curve, respectively.
Category: FETCh

title: XY (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:CCDF:{FIRSt|SECond|THIRd}:XY
Description: Returns the horizontal and vertical value pairs of the specified trace in the CCDF measurement.
The mnemonics FIRSt, SECond, and THIRd represent Trace 1, Trace 2, and Gaussian reference curve, respectively.
Category: FETCh

title: Y (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:CCDF:{FIRSt|SECond|THIRd}[:Y]
Description: Returns the vertical values of the specified trace in the CCDF measurement.
The mnemonics FIRSt, SECond, and THIRd represent Trace 1, Trace 2, and Gaussian reference curve, respectively.
Category: FETCh

title: FERRor (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:CONSte[:MEASview<x>]:FERRor
Description: Returns the frequency error in Hz. The frequency error is the difference between the measured carrier frequency of the signal and the
user-selected center frequency of the analyzer.
The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).
Category: FETCh

title: RESults (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:CONSte[:MEASview<x>]:RESults
Description: Returns the constellation measurement results of EVM RMS, peak, and location displayed on the bottom of the screen.
The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).
Category: FETCh

title: TRACe (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:CONSte[:MEASview<x>]:TRACe
Description: Returns the constellation trace data.
The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).
Category: FETCh

title: STABle (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:DDEMod[:MEASview<y>]:STABle
Description: Returns the symbol table data.
The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).
Category: FETCh

title: LENGth (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:DDEMod:SYNCh:WORD:LENGth
Description: Returns the length of the synch word in the symbol table.
1060
Category: FETCh

title: POSition (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:DDEMod:SYNCh:WORD:POSition
Description: Returns the position of the synch word in the symbol table.
Category: FETCh

title: FERRor (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:DIQVtime[:MEASview<y>]:FERRor
Description: Returns the frequency error in the Demod I&Q versus Time measurement.
The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).
Category: FETCh

title: I (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:DIQVtime[:MEASview<y>]:I
Description: Returns the I versus Time trace data.
The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).
Category: FETCh

title: Q (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:DIQVtime[:MEASview<y>]:Q
Description: Returns the Q versus Time trace data.
The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).
Category: FETCh

title: DDENsity (FETCh)
body:

title: FERRor (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:PERRor[:MEASview<y>]:FERRor
Description: Returns the frequency error in the Phase error versus Time measurement.
The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).
Category: FETCh

title: PEAK (FETCh)

8 channels for MSO58).

8 channels for MSO58).
1236

Command: FETCh:SEM:MHITs<x>:INTeg:RELative

Category: FETCh

title: ABSolute (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:SEM:MHITs<x>:MARGin:ABSolute
Description: Returns the absolute margin for the specified mask hit signal in the Spectral Emissions Mask measurement.
Category: FETCh

title: RELative (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:SEM:MHITs<x>:MARGin:RELative
Description: Returns the relative margin for the specified mask hit signal in the Spectral Emissions Mask measurement.
Category: FETCh

title: OFFSet (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:SEM:MHITs<x>:OFFSet
Description: Returns the offset for the specified mask hit signal in the Spectral Emissions Mask measurement.
Category: FETCh

title: PASS (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:SEM:PASS
Description: Returns the pass/fail limit test result in the Spectral Emissions Mask measurement.
Category: FETCh

title: POWer (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:SEM:REF:POWer
Description: Returns the reference power level in the Spectral Emissions Mask measurement.
Category: FETCh

title: X (FETCh)
body:

Description: Returns the frequencies of the spectrum trace in the Spectral Emissions Mask measurement.
Category: FETCh

title: XY (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:SEM:SPECtrum:XY
Description: Returns the frequency and amplitude pairs of the spectrum trace in the Spectral Emissions Mask measurement.
Category: FETCh

title: Y (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:SEM:SPECtrum[:Y]
Description: Returns the amplitude of the spectrum trace in the Spectral Emissions Mask measurement.
Category: FETCh

title: SGRam (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:SGRam
Description: Returns trace data of a line in the spectrogram. The line is selected using the TRACe:SGRam[:MEASview<y>]:SELect:LINE
command.
Category: FETCh

title: COUPling (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:SIGNalvu:ACQuisition:COUPling
Description: Returns the coupling value of the acquisition.
Category: FETCh

title: BW (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:SIGNalvu:ACQuisition:MAX:RT:BW
Description: Queries the maximum real time bandwidth value of the instrument.
Conditons
Should be connected to MSO Query
Category: FETCh

title: MAXFreq (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCH:SIGNalvu:ACQuisition[:SOURce<x>]:DOWNconverter:INJEction:HIGHSide:MAXFreq
Description: Returns the maximum range of input center frequency in case of high injection side
Category: FETCh

title: MINFreq (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCH:SIGNalvu:ACQuisition[:SOURce<x>]:DOWNconverter:INJEction:HIGHSide:MINFreq
Description: Returns the minimum range of input center frequency in case of high injection side

title: MAXFreq (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCH:SIGNalvu:ACQuisition[:SOURce<x>]:DOWNconverter:INJEction:LOWSide:MAXFreq
Description: Returns the maximum range of input center frequency in case of low injection side
Category: FETCh

title: MINFreq (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCH:SIGNalvu:ACQuisition[:SOURce<x>]:DOWNconverter:INJEction:LOWSide:MINFreq

Category: FETCh

body:
Command: FETCh:SIGNalvu:ACQuisition:TERMination
Description: Queries the termination value of the acquisition.
Conditons
Should be connected to MSO Query
Category: FETCh

title: TRACex (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:SPECtrum:TRACe<x>
Description: Returns the trace data in the Spectrum measurement.
The parameter <x> = 1 to 5.
Note: TRACe5 (spectrogram) is valid when the spectrum and spectrogram measurements are running.
Category: FETCh

title: POWer (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:SPURious:CARRier:POWer
Description: Returns the carrier power in the Spurious measurement.
Category: FETCh

title: COUNt (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:SPURious:COUNt
Description: Returns the number of spurious signals in the Spurious measurement.
Category: FETCh

title: PASS (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:SPURious:PASS
Description: Returns the pass/fail limit test result in the Spurious measurement.
Category: FETCh

title: X (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:SPURious:SPECtrum:X
Description: Returns the frequencies of the spectrum trace in the Spurious measurement.
Category: FETCh

title: XY (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:SPURious:SPECtrum:XY
Description: Returns the frequency and amplitude pairs of the spectrum trace in the Spurious measurement.
Category: FETCh

title: Y (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:SPURious:SPECtrum[:Y]
Description: Returns the amplitudes of the spectrum trace in the Spurious measurement.
Category: FETCh

title: ABSolute (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:SPURious:SPUR<x>:AMPLitude:ABSolute
Description: Returns the absolute amplitude of the specified spurious signal in the Spurious measurement.
Category: FETCh

title: RELative (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:SPURious:SPUR<x>:AMPLitude:RELative
Description: Returns the relative amplitude of the specified spurious signal in the Spurious measurement.
Category: FETCh

title: ABSolute (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:SPURious:SPUR<x>:FREQuency:ABSolute
Description: Returns the absolute frequency of the specified spurious signal in the Spurious measurement.
Category: FETCh

title: RELative (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:SPURious:SPUR<x>:FREQuency:RELative
Description: Returns the relative frequency of the specified spurious signal to the carrier in the Spurious measurement. This command is valid when
[SENSe]:SPURious:REFerence is set to CARRier.
Category: FETCh

title: ABSolute (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:SPURious:SPUR<x>:LIMit:ABSolute
Description: Returns the absolute amplitude of the limit for the specified spurious signal in the Spurious measurement.
Category: FETCh

title: RELative (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:SPURious:SPUR<x>:LIMit:RELative
Description: Returns the relative amplitude of the limit for the specified spurious signal in the Spurious measurement.
Category: FETCh

title: VIOLation (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:SPURious:SPUR<x>:LIMit:VIOLation
Description: Returns whether the specified spurious signal exceeds the limit or not.
Category: FETCh

title: RANGe (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:SPURious:SPUR<x>:RANGe
Description: Returns the frequency range in which the specified spurious signal occurred.
Category: FETCh

title: DEViation (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:SQUality[:MEASview<y>]:FREQuency:DEViation
Description: Returns the frequency deviation in the signal quality measurement.
This command is valid when [SENSe]:DDEMod:MODulation:TYPE is set to C4FM, FSK2, FSK4, FSK8, or FSK16.
The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).
Category: FETCh

title: TABLe (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:SQUality[:MEASview<y>]:FREQuency:DEViation:TABLe
Description: Returns the number of columns and the values in the frequency deviation table for a signal quality measurement.
This command is valid when [SENSe]:DDEMod:MODulation:TYPE is set to C4FM, FSK2, FSK4, FSK8, or FSK16.
The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).
Category: FETCh

title: ERRor (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:SQUality[:MEASview<y>]:FREQuency:ERRor
Description: Returns the frequency error in the signal quality measurement.
The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).
Category: FETCh

The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).
Category: FETCh

body:

The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).

body:

The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).
Category: FETCh

body:
Command: FETCh:SQUality[:MEASview<y>]:PEAK:EVM:DB

1338
The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).

body:

Category: FETCh

body:

8 channels for MSO58).
1340
Category: FETCh

title: OFFSet (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:SQUality[:MEASview<y>]:PEAK:EVM:OFFSet
Description: Returns the peak offset EVM (%) in the signal quality measurement.
This command is valid when [SENSe]:DDEMod:MODulation:TYPE is set to OQPSK or SOQPSK.
The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to

8 channels for MSO58).
Category: FETCh

title: DB (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:SQUality[:MEASview<y>]:PEAK:MERRor:DB

Category: FETCh

title: LOCation (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:SQUality[:MEASview<y>]:PEAK:MERRor:LOCation

The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).

body:

8 channels for MSO58).

title: LOCation (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:SQUality[:MEASview<y>]:PEAK:PERRor:LOCation
Description: Returns the time at which the phase error is peak.
The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).

title: ERRor (FETCh)
body:

The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).
Category: FETCh

title: RHO (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:SQUality[:MEASview<y>]:RHO
Description: Returns the ρ (waveform quality) value in the signal quality measurement
Category: FETCh

body:

The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to

body:
Command: FETCh:SQUality[:MEASview<y>]:RMS:EVM:DB
Description: Returns the RMS EVM (dB) in the signal quality measurement.
The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).

body:
Command: FETCh:SQUality[:MEASview<y>]:RMS:EVM:DB:OFFSet

The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).
Category: FETCh

title: OFFSet (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:SQUality[:MEASview<y>]:RMS:EVM:OFFSet
Description: Returns the RMS offset EVM (%) in the signal quality measurement.
This command is valid when [SENSe]:DDEMod:MODulation:TYPE is set to OQPSK or SOQPSK.
The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).
Category: FETCh

body:
Command: FETCh:SQUality[:MEASview<y>]:RMS:FERRor
Description: Returns the RMS FSK error percent in the signal quality measurement.
This command is valid when [SENSe]:DDEMod:MODulation:TYPE is set to FSK2, FSK4, FSK8, or FSK16.
The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).

title: DB (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:SQUality[:MEASview<y>]:RMS:MER:DB
Description: Returns the RMS MER (Modulation Error Ratio) in dB in the signal quality measurement.
The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).
Category: FETCh

title: MERRor (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:SQUality[:MEASview<y>]:RMS:MERRor
Description: Returns the RMS magnitude error (%) in the signal quality measurement.
The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).
Category: FETCh

title: DB (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:SQUality[:MEASview<y>]:RMS:MERRor:DB
Description: Returns the RMS magnitude error (dB) in the signal quality measurement.
The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).
Category: FETCh

body:
Command: FETCh:SQUality[:MEASview<y>]:RMS:PERRor
Description: Returns the RMS phase error in the signal quality measurement.
The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).
Category: FETCh

body:
Command: FETCh:SQUality[:MEASview<y>]:SYMBol:LENGth
Description: Returns the number of analyzed symbols.
1352
The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).
Category: FETCh

title: RATE (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:SQUality[:MEASview<y>]:SYMBol:RATE
Description: Returns the value of the calculated symbol rate in Hz.
The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).
Category: FETCh

body:
Command: FETCh:SQUality[:MEASview<y>]:SYMBol:RATE:ERRor
Description: Returns the value of the symbol rate error in percent (%).
The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).

title: FERRor (FETCh)
body:

The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).
Category: FETCh

title: MEASviewy (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:TOVerview:MEASview<y>
Description: Returns the trace data
Category: FETCh

title: MAGNitude (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:WLAN:CONSte:MAGNitude
Description: Returns the magnitude data for the WLAN Constellation measurement.
1416
Category: FETCh

title: PHASe (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:WLAN:CONSte:PHASe
Description: Returns the phase values for the WLAN Constellation measurement.
Category: FETCh

title: TYPE (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:WLAN:CONSte:TYPE
Description: Returns the type (context) for the WLAN Constellation measurement.
Category: FETCh

title: VALue (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:WLAN:CONSte:VALue
Description: Returns the value (decoded symbol) for the WLAN Constellation measurement.
Category: FETCh

title: MAGNitude (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:WLAN:CRESponse:MAGNitude
Description: Returns the magnitude data of the WLAN Channel Response measurement.
Category: FETCh

title: PHASe (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:WLAN:CRESponse:PHASe
Description: Returns the phase data of the WLAN Channel Response measurement.
Category: FETCh

title: TRACex (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:WLAN:EVM:TRACe<x>
Description: Returns the trace data for the selected WLAN EVM trace. When <x> is 1, the parameter is Matrix.
When <x> is 2, the parameter is Average versus Symbol. When <x> is 3, the parameter is Average versus Subcarrier
Category: FETCh

title: PASS (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:WLAN:FLATness:PASS
Description: Queries whether the average power level values of the subcarriers across the signal bandwidth on the WLAN Spectral Flatness display
remain within the limits defined for a particular standard.
Note: WLAN Spectral Flatness is only available for OFDM (non-802.11b) signals.
Category: FETCh

title: RESult (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:WLAN:FLATness:RESult
Description: Returns the summary results of each segment of the WLAN Spectral Flatness display.
Note: WLAN Spectral Flatness is only available for OFDM (non-802.11b) signals.
Category: FETCh

title: TRACex (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:WLAN:FLATness:TRACe<x>
Description: Returns the WLAN Spectral Flatness trace data. When <x> is 1, the parameter is Matrix (symbol deviation per subcarrier). When<x> is 2,
the parameter is Average Deviation vs Subcarrier.
Category: FETCh

title: TRACex (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:WLAN:MERRor:TRACe<x>
Description: Returns the trace data for the selected WLAN Magnitude Error trace.
When <x> is 1, the parameter is Matrix.
When <x> is 2, the parameter is Average versus Symbol.
When <x> is 3, the parameter is Average versus Subcarrier
Category: FETCh

title: TRACex (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:WLAN:PERRor:TRACe<x>
Description: Returns the trace data for the selected WLAN Phase Error trace.
When <x> is 1, the parameter is Matrix.
When <x> is 2, the parameter is Average versus Symbol.
When <x> is 3, the parameter is Average versus Subcarrier.
Category: FETCh

title: POWer (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:WLAN:PVTime:BURSt:POWer
Description: Returns the average power of the burst packet, in dBm, for the WLAN Power vs. Time trace.
Category: FETCh

title: WIDTh (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:WLAN:PVTime:BURSt:WIDTh
Description: Returns the value of the measured time width of the burst packet from Power-On to Power-Down (or end of waveform, if that occurs before
Power-Down), in seconds, for the WLAN Power vs. Time trace.
1424
Category: FETCh

title: TIME (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:WLAN:PVTime:FALL:TIME
Description: Returns the value of the width of the falling edge, in seconds.
Category: FETCh

title: STARt (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:WLAN:PVTime:FALL:TIME:STARt
Description: Returns the value of the start time of the falling edge, in seconds.
Category: FETCh

title: STOP (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:WLAN:PVTime:FALL:TIME:STOP
Description: Returns the value of the stop time of the falling edge, in seconds.
Category: FETCh

title: FTIMe (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:WLAN:PVTime:FTIMe
Description: Returns the value of the width of the falling edge, in seconds.
Category: FETCh

title: TIME (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:WLAN:PVTime:RISE:TIME
Description: Returns the value of the width of the rising edge, in seconds.
Category: FETCh

title: STARt (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:WLAN:PVTime:RISE:TIME:STARt
Description: Returns the value of the start time of the rising edge, in seconds.
Category: FETCh

title: STOP (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:WLAN:PVTime:RISE:TIME:STOP
Description: Returns the value of the stop time of the rising edge, in seconds.
Category: FETCh

title: RTIMe (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:WLAN:PVTime:RTIMe
Description: Returns the value of the width of the rising edge, in seconds.
Category: FETCh

title: X (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:WLAN:PVTime:TRACe:X
Description: Returns the horizontal values (time in seconds) for the WLAN Power vs. Time trace.
Category: FETCh

title: XY (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:WLAN:PVTime:TRACe:XY
Description: Returns the horizontal value (time in seconds) and vertical value (power) for the WLAN Power vs. Time trace.
<x(n)><y(n)> is the horizontal value and vertical value pair at the nth data point.
Category: FETCh

title: Y (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:WLAN:PVTime:TRACe[:Y]
Description: Returns the vertical values (power) for the WLAN Power vs. Time trace.
Category: FETCh

title: VALUe (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:WLAN:STABle:VALUe
Description: Returns the number of bytes in the symbol table for the WLAN measurement.
1430
Category: FETCh

title: INDex (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:WLAN:SUMMary:BURSt:INDex
Description: Returns the index of the analyzed packet within the analysis record.
Category: FETCh

title: POWer (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:WLAN:SUMMary:BURSt:POWer
Description: Returns the average power of all symbols in the packet, including Preamble and Data segments.
Category: FETCh

title: CFACtor (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:WLAN:SUMMary:BURSt:POWer:CFACtor
Description: Returns the peak-to-average burst power factor in the WLAN measurement. The ratio of the highest instantaneous signal power level to
the average signal power.
Category: FETCh

title: CERRor (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:WLAN:SUMMary:CERRor
Description: Returns the symbol clock error in the WLAN measurement. Not available for 802.11b.
Category: FETCh

title: CPE (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:WLAN:SUMMary:CPE
Description: Returns the RMS magnitude error of the pilots over all data symbols.
Not available for 802.11b.
Category: FETCh

title: ALL (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:WLAN:SUMMary:EVM:PEAK:ALL
Description: Returns the peak EVM value for all subcarriers in the WLAN measurement or for all chips depending on the selected standard.
Category: FETCh

title: ALL (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:WLAN:SUMMary:EVM:PEAK:CHIP:ALL
Description: Returns the chip at which the reported peak EVM value occurred. Available only for 802.11b
Category: FETCh

title: ONEKchips (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:WLAN:SUMMary:EVM:PEAK:CHIP:ONEKchips
Description: Returns the chip at which the reported peak EVM value over the first 1000 chips occurred.
Only available for 802.11b.
Category: FETCh

title: DATA (FETCh)
body:
Command: FETCh:WLAN:SUMMary:EVM:PEAK:DATA
Description: Returns the peak EVM value for the data subcarriers in the WLAN measurement. Not available for 802.11b.
Category: FETCh

title: ONEKchips (FETCh)
body:

title: CONTinuous (INITiate)
body:
Command: INITiate:CONTinuous
Description: Determines whether to place the analyzer in the single or the continuous acquisition mode.
Category: INITiate

title: IMMediate (INITiate)
body:
Command: INITiate[:IMMediate]
Description: Starts input signal acquisition.
Note: It is an overlapped command, which does not finish executing before the next command starts executing. Use the *OPC(?)
and *WAI commands to synchronize all pending operations to the execution of this command.
Category: INITiate

title: RESume (INITiate)
body:
Command: INITiate:RESume
Description: Restarts signal processing.
Resume is only applicable when FastFrame is enabled, and when the instrument is stopped while analyzing the frames. When the
instrument is stopped, measurements will finish processing and the system might stop before all of the frames of an acquisition are
processed (when acquisition is stopped, the system stops as soon as all measurements say they are finished processing the current
acquisition). However, the instrument saves all of the frames to the Acquisition History (Data Cache), and when INITiate:RESume is sent, it
will begin processing at the first frame of the last acquisition that was not processed (the one after the last frame processed), and process
all of these remaining frames before starting a new acquisition. If INITiate is sent instead, any remaining frames are discarded and a
new acquisition is started immediately. If FastFrame is off, or there are no remainder frames, or the acquisition was aborted rather than
stopped, then INITiate:RESume and INITiate are the same.
Category: INITiate
