Command: MMEMory:SGRam[:MEASview<y>]:SHOW:TRACe
Description: Enables display of a recalled trace file in Trace. The result is the same as selecting “Show recalled trace” in the Recall traces dialog box or
selecting Show in the Trace Overview display.
Category: MMEMory

title: TRACe (MMEMory)
body:
Command: MMEMory:SGRam[:MEASview<y>]:STORe:TRACe
Description: Stores the Spectrogram trace data in the specified file. No trace number is needed or allowed.
Note: Only valid when the spectrum or spectrogram measurements are running.
Category: MMEMory

title: TRACe (MMEMory)
body:
Command: MMEMory:SPECtrum[:MEASview<y>]:LOAD:TRACe
Description: Loads the trace data from a specified file
Category: MMEMory

title: TRACex (MMEMory)
body:
Command: MMEMory:SPECtrum[:MEASview<y>]:SHOW:TRACe<x>
Description: Enables display of a recalled trace file in Trace<x>. The result is the same as selecting “Show recalled trace” in the Recall traces dialog
box or selecting Show in the Trace Overview display.
Category: MMEMory

title: TRACex (MMEMory)
body:
Command: MMEMory:SPECtrum[:MEASview<y>]:STORe:TRACe<x>
Description: Stores the spectrum trace data in the specified file
Category: MMEMory

title: TABLe (MMEMory)
body:
Command: MMEMory:SPURious:LOAD:TABLe
Description: Loads the spurious table containing the limits for enabled ranges from the specified file.
Category: MMEMory

title: TABLe (MMEMory)
body:
Command: MMEMory:SPURious:STORe:TABLe
Description: Stores the spurious table containing the limits for enabled ranges in a specified file in the CSV (Comma Separated Values) format, allowing
you to export the file into Microsoft Excel or other database systems.
Category: MMEMory

title: IQ (MMEMory)
body:
Command: MMEMory:STORe:IQ
Description: Saves time-domain IQ waveform in the acquisition memory to a specified file.
Category: MMEMory

title: CSV (MMEMory)
body:
Command: MMEMory:STORe:IQ:CSV
Description: Saves time-domain IQ waveform in the acquisition memory to a specified file in the CSV (Comma Separated Values) format, allowing you
to export the file into Microsoft Excel or other database systems.
Category: MMEMory

title: MAT (MMEMory)
body:
Command: MMEMory:STORe:IQ:MAT
Description: Saves time-domain IQ waveform in the acquisition memory to a specified file in the MATLAB format, allowing you to export the file into the
MATLAB technical computing environment.
Category: MMEMory

title: DATA (MMEMory)
body:
Command: MMEMory:STORe:IQ:SELEct:DATA
Description: Sets or queries the data type stored when saving acquisition data files.
Category: MMEMory

title: FRAMes (MMEMory)
body:
Command: MMEMory:STORe:IQ:SELEct:FRAMes
Description: Sets or queries which frames are saved when saving TIQ acquisition data files.
Category: MMEMory

title: LENGth (MMEMory)
body:
Command: MMEMory:STORe:IQ:SELEct:LENGth
Description: Sets or queries what portion of each record is saved when saving acquisition data.
Category: MMEMory

title: MSTate (MMEMory)
body:
Command: MMEMory:STORe:MSTate
Description: Stores the measurement parameters to a specified file in the ASCII text format for the currently selected view, allowing you to export the
file into other applications.
Category: MMEMory

title: RESults (MMEMory)
body:
Command: MMEMory:STORe:RESults
Description: Stores the measurement results including measurement parameters and trace data to a specified file in the CSV (Comma Separated
Values) format for the currently selected view, allowing you to export the file into Microsoft Excel or other database systems.
Category: MMEMory

title: SCReen (MMEMory)
body:
Command: MMEMory:STORe:SCReen
Description: Stores the current display as a bitmap image file in one of several standard formats. When no format is specified, the system uses the
default, .png.
Category: MMEMory

title: STATe (MMEMory)
body:
Command: MMEMory:STORe:STATe
Description: Stores the instrument setup to a specified file for the currently selected view.
Category: MMEMory

title: TRACe (MMEMory)
body:
Command: MMEMory:STORe:TRACe
Description: Stores the trace data in a specified file for the currently selected view.
Category: MMEMory

title: TRACe1 (MMEMory)
body:
Command: MMEMory:TOVerview[:MEASview<y>]:LOAD:TRACe1
Description: Loads the trace data from a specified file into Trace1. Refer to (See Table 2-34.) for the correct file extensions for each trace type.
Category: MMEMory

title: TRACe1 (MMEMory)
body:
Command: MMEMory:TOVerview[:MEASview<y>]:SHOW:TRACe1
Description: Enables display of a recalled trace file in Trace<x>. The result is the same as selecting “Show recalled trace” in the Recall traces dialog
box or selecting Show in the Trace Overview display.
Category: MMEMory

title: TRACe1 (MMEMory)
body:
Command: MMEMory:TOVerview[:MEASview<y>]:STORe:TRACe1
Description: Stores the trace data for Trace1 into the specified file.
Category: MMEMory

title: WLANsubbroup (MMEMory)
body:
Command: MMEMory:WLANsubbroup
Description: Wireless LAN measurements
Category: MMEMory

title: TRACex (MMEMory)
body:
Command: MMEMory:WLAN:CRESponse:LOAD:TRACe<x>
Description: Loads the selected WLAN Channel Response trace data from the specified file.
<x> is the selected trace, 1 or 2. 1 is the magnitude trace. 2 is the phase trace.
Category: MMEMory

title: TRACex (MMEMory)
body:
Command: MMEMory:WLAN:CRESponse:SHOW:TRACe<x>
Description: Enables the display of a recalled WLAN Channel Response trace.
<x> is the selected trace, 1 or 2. 1 is the magnitude trace. 2 is the phase trace.
Category: MMEMory

title: TRACex (MMEMory)
body:
Command: MMEMory:WLAN:CRESponse:STORe:TRACe<x>
Description: Stores the WLAN Channel Response measurement trace data in the specified file.
<x> is the selected trace, 1 or 2. 1 is the magnitude trace. 2 is the phase trace.
Category: MMEMory

title: LOAD (MMEMory)
body:
Command: MMEMory:MAPit:LOAD
Description: Loads the MapIt measurement results from the specified file.
Category: MMEMory

title: STORe (MMEMory)
body:
Command: MMEMory:MAPit:STORe
Description: Save the selected MapIt measurement results to a file. The results to save are selected by the <result_index>. The saved files are
compressed .zip files.
Category: MMEMory

title: ALL (MMEMory)
body:
Command: MMEMory:MAPit:STORe:ALL
Description: Saves all of the MapIt measurement results from memory to the specified file.
Category: MMEMory

title: USERx (MMEMory)
body:
Command: MMEMory:DDEMod:LOAD:FILTer:MEASurement:USER<x>
Description: Loads the user-defined measurement filter from the specified file of the measurement source. The parameter <y> specifies one of the User
Meas Filters 1 to 3. The filter file must have a .csv file extension, though you may omit the file extension in the command line.
Category: MMEMory

title: UOTHer (MMEMory)
body:
Command: MMEMory:DDEMod:LOAD:FILTer:MEASurement:UOTHer
Description: Loads a user-defined measurement filter from the specified file of the measurement source.
Category: MMEMory

title: USERx (MMEMory)
body:
Command: MMEMory:DDEMod:LOAD:FILTer:REFerence:USER<x>
Description: Loads the specified user-defined reference filter, USER1 | 2 | 3 from a specified file of the measurement source.
Category: MMEMory

title: UOTHer (MMEMory)
body:
Command: MMEMory:DDEMod:LOAD:FILTer:REFerence:UOTHer
Description: Loads the specified user-defined reference filter, UOTHer, from a specified file of the measurement source.
1466
Category: MMEMory

title: MAP (MMEMory)
body:
Command: MMEMory:DDEMod:LOAD:SYMBol:MAP
Description: Loads the specified symbol map filename of the measurement source. The setting correlates to the field, User Symbol Map (per
Modulation Type) on the Advanced Params tab in the Digital Modulation settings.
Category: MMEMory

title: TRACex (MMEMory)
body:
Command: MMEMory:FVTime[:MEASview<y>]:SHOW:TRACe<x>
Description: Enables display of a recalled trace file in Trace<x>. The result is the same as selecting “Show recalled trace” in the Recall traces dialog
box or selecting Show in the Trace Overview display.
Category: MMEMory

title: BANDwidth|BWIDth (OUTPut)
STATe (OUTPut)
body:
Command: OUTPut:IF[:STATe]
Description: Determines whether to turn on or off the 500 MHz IF Out on the rear panel.
Category: OUTPut

title: SOURce (OUTPut)
body:
Command: OUTPut:IQ:SOURce
Description: Sets or queries the IQ data transfer state via the LVDS card in the CTRL7100A with option 05.
Category: OUTPut

title: STATe (OUTPut)
body:
Command: OUTPut:IQ[:STATe]
Description: Determines whether to enable or disable the digital IQ output data stream from the rear panel connectors.
Category: OUTPut

title: MODE (OUTPut)
body:
Command: OUTPut:TGen:BRIDge:MODE
Description: (Option 04 only; available for the RSA500A/ 600A series only))
Category: OUTPut

title: ENABle (OUTPut)
body:
Command: OUTPut:TGen:ENABle
Description: (Option 04 only; available for the RSA500A/ 600A series only))
Category: OUTPut

title: FREQuency (OUTPut)
body:
Command: OUTPut:TGen:FREQuency
Description: Sets or queries the tracking generator frequency in Hz. The spectrum analyzer center frequency will also be set to match (track) the
tracking generator frequency.
Category: OUTPut

title: INSTalled (OUTPut)
body:
Command: OUTPut:TGen:INSTalled
Description: (Option 04 only; available for the RSA500A/ 600A series only))
Category: OUTPut

title: LEVel (OUTPut)
body:
Command: OUTPut:TGen:LEVel
Description: (Option 04 only; available for the RSA500A/ 600A series only))
Category: OUTPut

title: ACPower (READ)
body:

title: HIGHer (READ)

Category: READ

title: LOWer (READ)
body:
Command: READ:PULSe:CUMulative:HISTogram[:MEASview<y>]:OUTLier:LOWer
Description: Returns the outliers value below the lower limit in pulse cumulative histogram.

title: COUNt (READ)
body:
Command: READ:PULSe:CUMulative:HISTogram[:MEASview<y>]:PULse:COUNt

Category: READ

title: MEASviewy (READ)
body:
Command: READ:PULSe:CUMulative:STATistics[:MEASview<y>]
Description: Returns the PulseCount, Max, Max TimeStamp, Min, Min TimeStamp, Peak to Peak, Average and Standard Deviation values for the
selected measurement in pulse cumulative statistics.
Category: READ

title: ADVanced (READ)
body:
Command: READ:PULSe[:RESult][:MEASview<y>]:ADVanced
Description: Returns the Pulse Count, Max, Max at Pulse number, Min, Min at Pulse number, Peak to Peak, Average and Standard Deviation values for
the chosen measurement in pulse table.
Category: READ

title: RANGe (READ)
body:
Command: READ:PULSe[:RESult][:MEASview<y>]:ADVanced:RANGe
Description: Queries the Pulse Count, Max, Max TimeStamp, Min, Min TimeStamp, Peak to Peak, Average, and Standard Deviation values for the
select measurement in the Pulse Table display.
Category: READ

title: FABS (READ)
body:
Command: READ:PULSe[:RESult][:MEASview<y>]:FABS
Description: Returns the computed absolute frequency from the carrier in the pulse table.
Category: READ

title: PPFD (READ)
body:
Command: READ:PULSe[:RESult][:MEASview<y>]:PPFD
Description: Returns the computed pulse to pulse frequency difference from the carrier in the pulse table.
Category: READ

title: PPPD (READ)
body:
Command: READ:PULSe[:RESult][:MEASview<y>]:PPPD
Description: Returns the computed pulse to pulse phase difference from the carrier in the pulse table.
Category: READ

title: ADVanced (READ)
body:
Command: READ:PULSe:STATistics[:MEASview<y>]:ADVanced
Description: Returns the Pulse Count, Max, Max at Pulse number, Min, Min at Pulse number, Peak to Peak, Average and Standard Deviation values for
the chosen measurement in Pulse Statistics display.
Category: READ

title: FABS (READ)
body:
Command: READ:PULSe:STATistics[:MEASview<y>]:FABS
Description: Returns the absolute frequency in pulse statistics.
Category: READ

title: NUMber (READ)
body:
Command: READ:PULSe:STATistics[:MEASview<y>]:MAX:PULSe:NUMber

title: NUMber (READ)
body:
Command: READ:PULSe:STATistics[:MEASview<y>]:MIN:PULSe:NUMber
Description: Returns the minimum value at a certain pulse number in pulse statistics.

title: PPFD (READ)
body:
Command: READ:PULSe:STATistics[:MEASview<y>]:PPFD
Description: Returns the pulse-pulse frequency difference in the Pulse Statistics measurement display.
Category: READ

title: PPPD (READ)
body:
Command: READ:PULSe:STATistics[:MEASview<y>]:PPPD

DISPlay:PULSe:STATistics[:MEASview<y>]:PLOT is set to TRENd or TTRend.

title: FTX (READ)

frequency of the signal and the user-selected center frequency.

title: Q (READ)
body:

Category: READ

title: F1FZero (READ)
body:
Command: READ:BLUEtooth:FREQuency:OFFSet:DRIFt:F1FZero
Description: Returns the drift in frequency offset (f1–f0 — the frequency offset calculated in the first interval in the payload— the frequency offset
calculated in the preamble) in the Bluetooth Frequency Deviation vs. Time display.
Category: READ

title: FNFN5 (READ)
body:
Command: READ:BLUEtooth:FREQuency:OFFSet:DRIFt:FNFN5
Description: Returns the maximum drift of the frequency offset in payload intervals spaced 50 μs away in the Bluetooth Frequency Deviation vs. Time
display.
Category: READ

body:
Command: READ:BLUEtooth:FREQuency:OFFSet:DRIFt:FNFN5:INTErval
Description: Returns the interval at which the maximum drift of frequency offset in a duration of 50 μs occurred.
Category: READ

title: FNFZERO (READ)
body:
Command: READ:BLUEtooth:FREQuency:OFFSet:DRIFt:FNFZERO
Description: Returns the maximum drift of the frequency offset of the intervals in the payload from the preamble packet in the Bluetooth Frequency
Deviation vs. Time display.
Category: READ

title: INTErval (READ)
body:
Command: READ:BLUEtooth:FREQuency:OFFSet:DRIFt:FNFZERO:INTErval
Description: Returns the interval (n) at which the maximum drift fn-f0 occurred in the Bluetooth Frequency Deviation vs. Time display. (The fn-f0
indicates the drift of the frequency offset calculated in each payload interval from the offset calculated in the preamble.)
Category: READ

title: MAX (READ)
body:
Command: READ:BLUEtooth:FREQuency:OFFSet:MAX

title: INTErval (READ)
body:

1572
Category: READ

displays.
Category: READ

title: VALue (READ)
body:
Command: READ:NOISe:TABLe:VALue
Description: Returns the noise table data. A total of 7 values per frequency (28 bytes per frequency) is returned. The number of frequencies returned
depends on the settings.
Category: READ

title: XDBBandwidth (READ)
body:
Command: READ:OBWidth:BOBW:XDBBandwidth
Description: Returns the difference between the higher and lower frequency points corresponding to the value where it is X dB less from the value at
the center frequency.
Set the measurement direction using the appropriate inward or outward command before issuing this command.
Category: READ

title: BANDwidth (READ)
body:
Command: READ:OBWidth:BOBW:XDBMbandwidth:IN:BANDwidth
Description: Returns the difference between the higher and lower frequency points corresponding to the value which it is the X dBm value measured
inwards (coming from the ends to the center frequency).
Category: READ

title: FREQuency (READ)
body:
Command: READ:OBWidth:BOBW:XDBMbandwidth:IN:LEFT:FREQuency
Description: Returns the frequency corresponding to the X dBm value to the left of the center frequency measured in the inward direction.
Category: READ

title: FREQuency (READ)
body:
Command: READ:OBWidth:BOBW:XDBMbandwidth:IN:RIGHt:FREQuency
Description: Returns the frequency corresponding to the X dBm value to the right of the center frequency measured in the inward direction.
Category: READ

title: BANDwidth (READ)
body:
Command: READ:OBWidth:BOBW:XDBMbandwidth:OUT:BANDwidth
Description: Returns the difference between the higher and lower frequency points corresponding to the value which it is the X dBm value measured
outwards (from the center frequency to the ends).
Category: READ

title: FREQuency (READ)
body:
Command: READ:OBWidth:BOBW:XDBMbandwidth:OUT:LEFT:FREQuency
Description: Returns the frequency corresponding to the X dBm value to the left of the center frequency measured in the outward direction.
Category: READ

title: FREQuency (READ)
body:
Command: READ:OBWidth:BOBW:XDBMbandwidth:OUT:RIGHt:FREQuency
Description: Returns the frequency corresponding to the X dBm value to the right of the center frequency measured in the outward direction.
Category: READ

title: FERRor (READ)
body:
Command: READ:P25:CONSte:FERRor
Description: Returns the frequency error in Hz. The frequency error is the difference between the measured carrier frequency of the signal and the
user-selected center frequency of the analyzer.
When Frequency Error is in Auto mode, this query returns the frequency error value. When in Manual mode, it returns the frequency offset.
Category: READ

title: AM|FM|PM (READ)
AMINdex (READ)
body:
Command: READ:AM:AMINdex
Description: Returns the modulation index which is (positive peak modulation factor - negative peak modulation factor)/2, returned as a percentage (%).
Category: READ

body:

Description: Returns the negative peak modulation factor (–AM) in the AM measurement.
Category: READ

Description: Returns the positive peak modulation factor (+AM) in the AM measurement.
Category: READ

Category: READ

title: AMPLitude (READ)
body:
Command: READ:AUDio:HARMonic<x>:AMPLitude
Description: Returns the amplitude of the specified harmonic in the audio spectrum measurement.
The value <x> represents the specific harmonic, from 1 to the value specified by the CALCulate:AUDio:HARMonic:HNUMber
command.
1540
Category: READ

title: FREQuency (READ)
body:
Command: READ:AUDio:HARMonic<x>:FREQuency
Description: Returns the frequency of the specified harmonic in the audio spectrum measurement.
The value <x> represents the specific harmonic, from 1 to the value specified by the CALCulate:AUDio:HARMonic:HNUMber
command.
Category: READ

title: HNOise (READ)
body:
Command: READ:AUDio:HNOise
Description: Returns the difference between the current RMS modulation value and the reference value.
This information is only available when the Hum & Noise is measurement is enabled; see the [SENSe]:AUDio:HNOise:ENABle
command. The reference value is stored after capturing the reference by pressing the Capture Reference button or by enabling the
[SENSe]:AUDio:HNOise:REFerence command.
Category: READ

title: HNREFerence (READ)
body:
Command: READ:AUDio:HNREFerence
Description: Returns the Hum and Noise RMS modulation reference value.
The value is stored after capturing the reference by pressing the Capture Reference button or by enabling the
[SENSe]:AUDio:HNOise:REFerence command. This information is only available when the Hum & Noise measurement
is enabled; see the [SENSe]:AUDio:HNOise:ENABle command.
For AM signal types, the reference represents modulation depth. For FM signal types, the reference represents frequency deviation. For
PM signal types the reference represents phase. For Direct signal types the reference represents power.
1542
Category: READ

title: HPTPeak (READ)
body:
Command: READ:AUDio:HPTPeak
Description: Returns the half peak-to-peak modulation excursion (where the modulation excursion depends on the signal type).
For AM signal types, the modulation excursion is “% Modulation Depth.” For FM signal types, the modulation excursion is “Frequency
Deviation.” For PM signal types, the modulation excursion is “Phase Deviation.” For Direct signal types, there is no modulation excursion; it
is actually “signal excursion.”
Category: READ

title: MODDist (READ)
body:
Command: READ:AUDio:MODDist
Description: Returns the modulation distortion for the audio measurement.
Category: READ

title: COUNt (READ)
body:
Command: READ:AUDio:NHARmonic:COUNt
Description: Returns the number of non-harmonics in the audio spectrum measurement.
The number of non-harmonics appear in the results table in the audio spectrum view.
Category: READ

title: AMPLitude (READ)
body:
Command: READ:AUDio:NHARmonic<x>:AMPLitude
Description: Returns the amplitude of the specified non-harmonic in the audio spectrum measurement.
The value <x> represents the specific non-harmonic, from 1 to the value specified by the
CALCulate:AUDio:HARMonic:NHNumber command.
Category: READ

title: FREQuency (READ)
body:
Command: READ:AUDio:NHARmonic<x>:FREQuency
Description: Returns the frequency of the specified non-harmonic in the audio spectrum measurement.
The value <x> represents the specific non-harmonic, from 1 to the value specified by the
CALCulate:AUDio:HARMonic:NHNumber command.
Category: READ

title: PNEGative (READ)
body:
Command: READ:AUDio:PNEGative
Description: Returns the minus peak modulation excursion (where the modulation excursion depends on the signal type).
For AM signal types, the modulation excursion is “% Modulation Depth.” For FM signal types, the modulation excursion is “Frequency
Deviation.” For PM signal types, the modulation excursion is “Phase Deviation.” For Direct signal types, there is no modulation excursion; it
is actually “signal excursion.”
Category: READ

title: POWer (READ)
body:
Command: READ:AUDio:POWer
Description: Returns the carrier power or signal power depending the signal type.
If the signal type is Direct, the returned value is the average power of the input signal. If the signal type is AM, FM, or PM, the returned
value is the carrier power; the average power of the carrier signal with the modulation removed.
Category: READ

title: PPOSitive (READ)
body:
Command: READ:AUDio:PPOSitive
Description: Returns the positive peak modulation excursion (where the modulation excursion depends on the signal type).
For AM signal types, the modulation excursion is “% Modulation Depth.” For FM signal types, the modulation excursion is “Frequency
Deviation.” For PM signal types, the modulation excursion is “Phase Deviation.” For Direct signal types, there is no modulation excursion; it
is actually “signal excursion.”
Category: READ

title: RMS (READ)
body:
Command: READ:AUDio:RMS
Description: Returns the RMS modulation excursion for the audio measurement (where the modulation excursion depends on the signal type).
For AM signal types, the modulation excursion is “% Modulation Depth.” For FM signal types, the modulation excursion is “Frequency
Deviation.” For PM signal types, the modulation excursion is “Phase Deviation.” For Direct signal types, there is no modulation excursion; it
is actually “signal excursion.”
Category: READ

title: SINad (READ)
body:
Command: READ:AUDio:SINad
Description: Returns the signal-to-noise and distortion for the audio measurement.
Category: READ

title: SNOise (READ)
body:
Command: READ:AUDio:SNOise
Description: Returns the signal level to noise level with the harmonic distortion and non-harmonic distortion components removed.
Category: READ

title: TRACex (READ)
body:
Command: READ:AUDio:SPECtrum:TRACe<x>
Description: Returns the audio spectrum trace data for the audio measurement.
The <x> represents trace 1 for this measurement. No other traces are supported.
Category: READ

title: DB (READ)
body:
Command: READ:AUDio:THDist:DB
Description: Returns the total harmonic distortion in dB in the audio measurement.
Category: READ

title: THDist (READ)
body:
Command: READ:AUDio:THDist
Description: Returns the percentage of the total harmonic distortion in the audio measurement.
Category: READ

title: DB (READ)
body:
Command: READ:AUDio:TNHDist:DB
Description: Returns the total non-harmonic distortion in dB in the audio measurement.
Category: READ

title: TNHDist (READ)
body:
Command: READ:AUDio:TNHDist
Description: Returns the percentage of the total non-harmonic distortion in the audio measurement.
Category: READ

title: AVERage (READ)
body:
Command: READ:AVTime[:MEASview<y>]:AVERage
Description: Returns the RMS (root-mean-square) value for the selected trace in the Amplitude versus Time measurement. Select the trace using the
TRACe<x>:AVTime[:MEASview<y>]:SELect command.
Category: READ

title: FIRSt|SECond|THIRd|FOURth (READ)
body:
Command: READ:AVTime[:MEASview<y>]:{FIRSt|SECond|THIRd|FOURth}
Description: Returns the trace data in the Amplitude versus Time measurement.
The mnemonics FIRSt, SECond, THIRd, and FOURth represent Trace 1, Trace 2, Trace 3, and Math trace, respectively. The traces can be
specified by the TRACe<x>:AVTime[:MEASview<y>] command subgroup.
Category: READ

title: MAXimum (READ)
body:
Command: READ:AVTime[:MEASview<y>]:MAXimum
Description: Returns the maximum value for the selected trace in the Amplitude versus Time measurement. Select the trace using
theTRACe<x>:AVTime[:MEASview<y>]:SELect command.
Category: READ

title: MAXLocation (READ)
body:
Command: READ:AVTime[:MEASview<y>]:MAXLocation
Description: Returns the time at which the amplitude is maximum for the selected trace in the Amplitude versus Time measurement. Select the trace
using the TRACe<x>:AVTime[:MEASview<y>]:SELect command.
Category: READ

title: MEASviewyMINimum (READ)
body:
Command: READ:AVTime:[:MEASview<y>]MINimum
Description: Returns the minimum value for the selected trace in the Amplitude versus Time measurement. Select the trace using the
TRACe<x>:AVTime[:MEASview<y>]:SELect command.
Category: READ

title: MINLocation (READ)
body:
Command: READ:AVTime[:MEASview<y>]:MINLocation
Description: Returns the time at which the amplitude is minimum for the selected trace in the Amplitude versus Time measurement. Select the trace
using the TRACe<x>:AVTime[:MEASview<y>]:SELect command.
Category: READ

title: RESult (READ)
body:
Command: READ:AVTime[:MEASview<y>]:RESult
Description: Returns the measurement results for the selected trace in the Amplitude versus Time measurement. Select the trace using the
TRACe<x>:AVTime[:MEASview<y>]:SELect command.
Category: READ

title: CCDF (READ)
body:
Command: READ:CCDF
Description: Returns the CCDF measurement results.
Category: READ

title: X (READ)
body:
Command: READ:CCDF:{FIRSt|SECond|THIRd}:X
Description: Returns the horizontal values of the specified trace in the CCDF measurement. The mnemonics FIRSt, SECond, and THIRd represent
Trace 1, Trace 2, and Gaussian reference curve, respectively.
Note: This query is invalid when [SENSe]:CCDF:TIME:TYPE is set to CONTinuous or TOTal.
Category: READ

title: XY (READ)
body:
Command: READ:CCDF:{FIRSt|SECond|THIRd}:XY
Description: Returns the horizontal and vertical value pairs of the specified trace in the CCDF measurement.
The mnemonics FIRSt, SECond, and THIRd represent Trace 1, Trace 2, and Gaussian reference curve, respectively.

Note: This query is invalid when [SENSe]:CCDF:TIME:TYPE is set to CONTinuous or TOTal.
Category: READ

title: Y (READ)
body:
Command: READ:CCDF:{FIRSt|SECond|THIRd}[:Y]
Description: Returns the vertical values of the specified trace in the CCDF measurement.
The mnemonics FIRSt, SECond, and THIRd represent Trace 1, Trace 2, and Gaussian reference curve, respectively.
Note: This query is invalid when [SENSe]:CCDF:TIME:TYPE is set to CONTinuous or TOTal.
Category: READ

title: FERRor (READ)
body:
Command: READ:CONSte[:MEASview<x>]:FERRor
Description: Returns the frequency error in Hz. The frequency error is the difference between the measured carrier frequency of the signal and the user
selected center frequency of the analyzer.
The parameter <x> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).
Category: READ

title: RESults (READ)
body:
Command: READ:CONSte[:MEASview<x>]:RESults
Description: Returns the constellation measurement results of EVM RMS, peak, and location displayed on the bottom of the screen.
The parameter <x> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).
Category: READ

title: TRACe (READ)
body:
Command: READ:CONSte[:MEASview<x>]:TRACe
Description: Returns the constellation trace data.
The parameter <x> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).
Category: READ

title: STABle (READ)
body:
Command: READ:DDEMod[:MEASview<y>]:STABle
Description: Returns the symbol table data.
The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).
Category: READ

title: FERRor (READ)
body:
Command: READ:DIQVtime[:MEASview<y>]:FERRor
Description: Returns the frequency error in the Demod I&Q versus Time measurement.
The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).
Category: READ

title: I (READ)
body:
Command: READ:DIQVtime[:MEASview<y>]:I
Description: Returns the I versus Time trace data.
The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).
Category: READ

title: Q (READ)
body:
Command: READ:DIQVtime[:MEASview<y>]:Q
Description: Returns the Q versus Time trace data.
The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).
Category: READ

title: DDENsity (READ)
body:
Command: READ:DPX:DDENsity
Description: Returns the calculated density for the selected region in the DPXogram measurement.
Category: READ

title: COUNt (READ)
body:
Command: READ:DPX:DGRam:LINE:COUNt
Description: Returns the number of lines in the DPXogram measurement.
Category: READ

title: OFFSet (READ)
body:
Command: READ:DPX:DGRam:TIME[:SCALe]:OFFSet
Description: Returns the time scale offset in the DPXogram measurement.
Category: READ

title: TRACex (READ)
body:
Command: READ:DPX:RESults:TRACe<x>
Description: Acquires a waveform and then returns waveform data of the specified trace <x> in the DPX spectrum measurement, where x is 1 to 6. The
traces 1–4 are in the standard form. Trace 5 is the bitmap trace and its data is returned in a binary block. Trace 6 is the DPXogram trace
on DPX spectrum plots. Trace 7 is the Ogram line in the DPXogram display.
Category: READ

title: AVERage (READ)
body:
Command: READ:DPX:TRACe:AVERage
Description: Acquires a waveform and then returns waveform data of the average trace in the DPX, Spectrum measurement.
Category: READ

title: BITMap (READ)
body:
Command: READ:DPX:TRACe:BITMap
Description: Acquires a waveform and then returns trace waveform data of the bitmap trace in the DPX, Spectrum measurement.
Category: READ

title: MATH (READ)
body:
Command: READ:DPX:TRACe:MATH
Description: Returns waveform data of the math trace in the DPX, Spectrum measurement.
Category: READ

title: MAXimum (READ)
body:
Command: READ:DPX:TRACe:MAXimum
Description: Returns waveform data of the maximum trace in the DPX, Spectrum measurement.
Category: READ

title: MINimum (READ)
body:
Command: READ:DPX:TRACe:MINimum
Description: Returns waveform data of the minimum trace in the DPX, Spectrum measurement.
Category: READ

title: FDEViation (READ)
body:
Command: READ:EDIagram[:MEASview<y>]:FDEViation
Description: Returns the frequency deviation versus Time trace data with the X values.
The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).
Category: READ

title: FERRor (READ)
body:
Command: READ:EDIagram[:MEASview<y>]:FERRor
Description: Returns the frequency error in the eye diagram measurement.
The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).
Category: READ

title: I (READ)
body:
Command: READ:EDIagram[:MEASview<y>]:I
Description: Returns the I versus Time trace data.
The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).
Category: READ

title: Q (READ)
body:
Command: READ:EDIagram[:MEASview<y>]:Q
Description: Returns the Q versus Time trace data.
The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).
Category: READ

title: FERRor (READ)
body:
Command: READ:EVM[:MEASview<y>]:FERRor
Description: Returns the frequency error in the EVM versus Time measurement.
1636
The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).
Category: READ

title: PEAK (READ)
body:
Command: READ:EVM[:MEASview<y>]:PEAK
Description: Returns the peak value in the EVM versus Time measurement.
The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8bchannels for MSO58).
Category: READ

title: PINDex (READ)
body:
Command: READ:EVM[:MEASview<y>]:PINDex
Description: Returns the time at the EVM peak.
The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).
Category: READ

title: RMS (READ)
body:
Command: READ:EVM[:MEASview<y>]:RMS
Description: Returns the RMS (Root-Mean-Square) value in the EVM versus Time measurement.
1638
The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).
Category: READ

title: TRACe (READ)
body:
Command: READ:EVM[:MEASview<y>]:TRACe
Description: Returns the EVM versus Time trace data.
The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).
Category: READ

title: FERRor (READ)
body:
Command: READ:FDVTime[:MEASview<y>]:FERRor
Description: Returns the frequency error in the Frequency deviation versus Time measurement.
The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).
Category: READ

title: TRACe (READ)
body:
Command: READ:FDVTime[:MEASview<y>]:TRACe
Description: Returns the Frequency deviation versus Time trace data.
The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).
1640
Category: READ

title: ERRor (READ)
body:
Command: READ:FSETtling:ERRor
Description: Returns the settled error in Hz in Frequency Settling. Only for Frequency Settling Time measurement. In Frequency Settling:
When Target Reference is set to Auto, Settled Error = 0.
Category: READ

title: FTTime (READ)
body:
Command: READ:{FSETtling|PSETtling}:FTTime
Description: Returns the settling time from the trigger position in seconds. The settling time is measured from the trigger point
(see :FETCh:FSETtling:TRIGger:TIME).
Category: READ

title: PASS (READ)
body:
Command: READ:{FSETtling|PSETtling}:MASK[:PASS]
Description: Returns whether the input signal passes the mask test.
Category: READ

title: FREQuency (READ)
body:
Command: READ:{FSETtling|PSETtling}:SETTled:FREQuency
Description: Returns the frequency at which the signal is considered settled.
Category: READ

title: PASS (READ)
body:
Command: READ:{FSETtling|PSETtling}:SETTled[:PASS]
Description: Returns whether the input signal is settled with the tolerance range.
Category: READ

title: TIME (READ)
body:
Command: READ:{FSETtling|PSETtling}:SETTled:TIME
Description: Returns the settled time in seconds. The settled time is measured from the measurement start point.
Category: READ

title: PASS (READ)
body:
Command: READ:{FSETtling|PSETtling}:SLMSd[:PASS]
Description: Returns whether the input signal is settled within the specified tolerance and the signal is settled longer than the Minimum Settled Duration
([SENSe]:{FSETtling|PSETtling}:SDURation:MINimum).
Category: READ

title: TIME (READ)
body:
Command: READ:{FSETtling|PSETtling}:STARt:TIME
Description: Returns the start time for the measurement in seconds. The start time is measured from the start of the analysis period.
Category: READ

title: TIME (READ)
body:
Command: READ:{FSETtling|PSETtling}:TIME
Description: Returns the settling time in seconds. The settling time is measured from the start time (see :FETCh:FSETtling:STARt:TIME).
Category: READ

title: X (READ)
body:
Command: READ:{FSETtling|PSETtling}:TRACe<x>:X
Description: Returns the Frequency or Phase values of the specified trace.
The parameter <x> = 1 and 2, representing Trace 1 and Trace 2, respectively.
Category: READ

title: XY (READ)
body:
Command: READ:{FSETtling|PSETtling}:TRACe<x>:XY
Description: Returns the time and frequency or phase value pairs of the specified trace.
The parameter <x> = 1 and 2, representing Trace 1 and Trace 2, respectively.
Category: READ

title: Y (READ)
body:
Command: READ:{FSETtling|PSETtling}:TRACe<x>[:Y]
Description: Returns the frequency or phase values of the specified trace.
The parameter <x> = 1 and 2, representing Trace 1 and Trace 2, respectively.
Category: READ

title: TIME (READ)
body:
Command: READ:{FSETtling|PSETtling}:TRIGger:TIME
Description: Returns the time when the trigger occurred in seconds.
Category: READ

title: VALue (READ)
body:
Command: READ:{FSETtling|PSETtling}:VALue
Description: Returns the settled value in Hz for Frequency Settling and in degrees for Phase Settling.
Category: READ

title: MEASviewy (READ)
body:
Command: READ:FVTime[:MEASview<y>]
Description: Returns the Frequency versus Time trace data.
Category: READ

title: MAXimum (READ)
body:
Command: READ:FVTime[:MEASview<y>]:MAXimum
Description: Returns the maximum value in the Frequency versus Time measurement.

title: MAXLocation (READ)
body:

Description: Returns the time at which the frequency drift is maximum.
Category: READ

title: MINimum (READ)
body:
Command: READ:FVTime[:MEASview<y>]:MINimum
Description: Returns the minimum value in the Frequency versus Time measurement.
Category: READ

title: MINLocation (READ)
body:
Command: READ:FVTime[:MEASview<y>]:MINLocation
Description: Returns the time at which the frequency drift is minimum.
Category: READ

title: RESult (READ)
body:
Command: READ:FVTime[:MEASview<y>]:RESult
Description: Returns the Frequency versus Time measurement results.
Category: READ

title: I (READ)

Command: READ:IQVTime[:MEASview<y>]:I
Description: Returns the I versus Time trace data.
Category: READ

title: MAXimum (READ)
body:
Command: READ:IQVTime[:MEASview<y>]:MAXimum
Description: Returns the maximum value in the RF I & Q versus Time measurement.
Category: READ

title: MAXLocation (READ)
body:
Command: READ:IQVTime[:MEASview<y>]:MAXLocation
Description: Returns the time at which the I or Q level is maximum.
Category: READ

title: MINimum (READ)
body:
Command: READ:IQVTime[:MEASview<y>]:MINimum
Description: Returns the minimum value in the RF I & Q versus Time measurement.
Category: READ

title: MINLocation (READ)
body:
Command: READ:IQVTime[:MEASview<y>]:MINLocation
Description: Returns the time at which the I or Q level is minimum.
Category: READ

title: Q (READ)
body:
Command: READ:IQVTime[:MEASview<y>]:Q
Description: Returns the Q versus Time trace data.
Category: READ

title: RESult (READ)
body:
Command: READ:IQVTime[:MEASview<y>]:RESult
Description: Returns the RF I & Q versus Time measurement results.
Category: READ

title: CHANnels (READ)

title: POWer (READ)

Category: READ

title: CHANnels (READ)

Command: READ:MCPower:MAIN:CHANnels
Description: Returns the power of main channels in order of increasing frequency.
Category: READ

title: SPECtrum (READ)

Description: Returns spectrum trace data of the MCPR measurement.
Category: READ

title: FERRor (READ)

Description: Returns the frequency error in the Magnitude error versus Time measurement.
The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).

title: PEAK (READ)

8 channels for MSO58).

The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).

title: RMS (READ)

title: POWer (READ)

title: FREQuency (READ)

body:

Category: READ

title: PEAK (READ)

command before executing this query.
Category: READ

command before executing this query.

8 channels for MSO58).

body:

Description: Returns the phase data for the WLAN Channel Response measurement.

title: TRACex (READ)
body:
Command: READ:WLAN:EVM:TRACe<x>
Description: Returns the trace data for the selected WLAN EVM trace.
When <x> is 1, the parameter is Matrix.
When <x> is 2, the parameter is Average versus Symbol.
When <x> is 3, the parameter is Average versus Subcarrier
Category: READ

title: PASS (READ)
body:
Command: READ:WLAN:FLATness:PASS
Description: Queries whether the average power level values of the subcarriers across the signal bandwidth on the WLAN Spectral Flatness display
remain within the limits defined for a particular standard.
Note: WLAN Spectral Flatness is only available for OFDM (non-802.11b) signals.
Category: READ

title: RESult (READ)
body:
Command: READ:WLAN:FLATness:RESult
Description: Returns the summary results of each segment of the WLAN Spectral Flatness display.
Note: WLAN Spectral Flatness is only available for OFDM (non-802.11b) signals.
1930
Category: READ

title: TRACex (READ)
body:
Command: READ:WLAN:FLATness:TRACe<x>
Description: Returns the WLAN Spectral Flatness trace data. When <x> is 1, the parameter is Matrix (symbol deviation per subcarrier). When <x> is 2,
the parameter is Average Deviation vs Subcarrier.
Note: WLAN Spectral Flatness is only available for OFDM (non-802.11b) signals.
Category: READ

title: TRACex (READ)
body:
Command: READ:WLAN:MERRor:TRACe<x>
Description: Returns the trace data for the selected WLAN Magnitude Error trace.
When <x> is 1, the parameter is Matrix.
When <x> is 2, the parameter is Average versus Symbol.
When <x> is 3, the parameter is Average versus Subcarrier
Category: READ

title: TRACex (READ)
body:

Description: Returns the trace data for the selected WLAN Phase Error trace.
When <x> is 1, the parameter is Matrix.
When <x> is 2, the parameter is Average versus Symbol.
When <x> is 3, the parameter is Average versus Subcarrier
Category: READ

<x(n)><y(n)> is the horizontal value and vertical value pair at the nth data point.

body:

Description: Returns the subcarrier on which the ALL peak EVM value occurred.
Category: READ

title: DATA (READ)
body:
Command: READ:WLAN:SUMMary:EVM:PEAK:SCARrier:DATA
Description: Returns the subcarrier on which the DATA peak EVM value occurred.
Not available for 802.11b.
Category: READ

title: PILots (READ)
body:
Command: READ:WLAN:SUMMary:EVM:PEAK:SCARrier:PILots
Description: Returns the subcarrier on which the PILOT peak EVM value occurred.
Category: READ

title: ALL (READ)
body:
Command: READ:WLAN:SUMMary:EVM:PEAK:SYMBol:ALL
Description: Returns the symbol on which the reported EVM value occurred.
Category: READ

title: DATA (READ)
body:
Command: READ:WLAN:SUMMary:EVM:PEAK:SYMBol:DATA
Description: Returns the symbol on which the DATA peak EVM value occurred.
Not available for 802.11b.
Category: READ

title: PILots (READ)
body:
Command: READ:WLAN:SUMMary:EVM:PEAK:SYMBol:PILots
Description: Returns the symbol on which the PILOT peak EVM value occurred.
Category: READ

title: ALL (READ)
body:
Command: READ:WLAN:SUMMary:EVM:RMS:ALL
Description: Returns the RMS EVM data for all subcarriers in the WLAN measurement and for all chips for 802.11b.
Category: READ

title: AVERage (READ)
body:
Command: READ:WLAN:SUMMary:EVM:RMS:AVERage
Description: Returns the average RMS EVM value in the WLAN measurement over the selected number of bursts.
Category: READ

title: DATA (READ)
body:
Command: READ:WLAN:SUMMary:EVM:RMS:DATA
Description: Returns the RMS EVM data for the data subcarriers in the WLAN measurement.
Not available for 802.11b.
Category: READ

title: MAXimum (READ)
body:
Command: READ:WLAN:SUMMary:EVM:RMS:MAXimum
Description: Returns the maximum RMS EVM value in the WLAN measurement over the selected number of bursts.
Category: READ

title: ONEKchips (READ)
body:
Command: READ:WLAN:SUMMary:EVM:RMS:ONEKchips
Description: Returns the RMS EVM data for the 1k chips in the WLAN measurement measured over the first 1000 chips.
Only available for 802.11b.
Category: READ

title: PILots (READ)
body:
Command: READ:WLAN:SUMMary:EVM:RMS:PILots
Description: Returns the RMS EVM data for the pilot subcarriers in the WLAN measurement.
Not available for 802.11b.
1950
Category: READ

title: FERRor (READ)
body:
Command: READ:WLAN:SUMMary:FERRor
Description: Returns the frequency difference between the measured carrier frequency of the signal and the measurement frequency setting.
Category: READ

title: HEADer (READ)
body:
Command: READ:WLAN:SUMMary:HEADer
Description: Returns the received packet header information for the WLAN measurement.
Applies to the 802.11b standard only.
Category: READ

title: HTSig (READ)
body:
Command: READ:WLAN:SUMMary:HTSig
Description: Returns the decimal values of the received packet HT-SIGNAL. Applies to the 802.11n standard only.
Category: READ

title: IQOFfset (READ)
body:
Command: READ:WLAN:SUMMary:IQOFfset
Description: Returns the average magnitude of the DC subcarrier level relative to total signal power.
Category: READ

title: LSIG (READ)
body:
Command: READ:WLAN:SUMMary:LSIG
Description: Returns the decimal values of the received packet (legacy) SIGNAL symbols fields. Applies to the 802.11n standard only.
Category: READ

title: PACKet (READ)
body:
Command: READ:WLAN:SUMMary:PACKet
Description: Returns the type and number of symbols, EVM-RMS and average power of the Preamble and Data portions of the packet.
Category: READ

title: DMODulation (READ)
body:
Command: READ:WLAN:SUMMary:PACKet:DMODulation
Description: Returns the modulation used in the Data symbols.
Category: READ

title: FORMat (READ)
body:
Command: READ:WLAN:SUMMary:PACKet:FORMat
Description: Returns the packet format setting for the WLAN measurement
Category: READ

title: GUARd (READ)
body:
Command: READ:WLAN:SUMMary:PACKet:GUARd
Description: Returns the packet guard interval used by the DATA symbols.
Not available for 802.11b.
Category: READ

title: SIG (READ)
body:
Command: READ:WLAN:SUMMary:SIG
Description: Returns the decimal values of the received packet SIGNAL symbols fields.
Available only for 802.11agjp.
Category: READ

title: A (READ)
body:
Command: READ:WLAN:SUMMary:VHTSig:A
Description: Returns the decimal values of the received packet VHT-SIGNAL(A) symbols' fields.
Available only for 802.11ac.
Category: READ

title: B (READ)
body:
Command: READ:WLAN:SUMMary:VHTSig:B
Description: Returns the decimal values of the received packet VHT-SIGNAL(B) symbols fields.
Available only for 802.11ac.
Category: READ

title: COUNt (READ)
body:
Command: READ:LTE:ACLR:MHITs:COUNt
Description: Returns the number of rows in the results table of the LTE ACLR display.
Category: READ

title: BANDwidth (READ)
body:
Command: READ:LTE:ACLR:MHITs<x>:BANDwidth
Description: Returns the bandwidth value of the channel (row) of the LTE ACLR display.
Category: READ

title: NAME (READ)
body:
Command: READ:LTE:ACLR:MHITs<x>:CHANnel:NAME
Description: Returns the channel name of the specified index in the results table of the LTE ACLR display. The minimum index will be 1 and the
maximum index will be the count of the channels in the results table.
Category: READ

title: OFFSet (READ)
body:
Command: READ:LTE:ACLR:MHITs<x>:FREQuency:OFFSet
Description: Returns the frequency offset of the channel of the LTE ACLR display.
Category: READ

title: ABSolute (READ)
body:
Command: READ:LTE:ACLR:MHITs<x>:INTeg:LOWEr:ABSolute
Description: Returns the lower absolute value of the channel of the LTE ACLR display.
Category: READ

title: RELative (READ)
body:
Command: READ:LTE:ACLR:MHITs<x>:INTeg:LOWEr:RELative
Description: Returns the lower relative value of the channel of the LTE ACLR display.
Category: READ

title: ABSolute (READ)
body:
Command: READ:LTE:ACLR:MHITs<x>:INTeg:UPPEr:ABSolute
Description: Returns the upper absolute value of the channel of the LTE ACLR display.
Category: READ

title: POWer (READ)
body:
Command: READ:LTE:ACLR:REFerence:POWer
Description: Returns the frequency and amplitude pairs of the spectrum trace
Category: READ

title: STATus (READ)
body:
Command: READ:LTE:ACLR:RESUlts:STATus
Description: Returns the pass or fail status of the LTE ACLR display measurement.
Category: READ

title: X (READ)
body:
Command: READ:LTE:ACLR:SPECtrum:X
Description: Returns the frequency of the spectrum trace in the LTE ACLR display.
Category: READ

title: XY (READ)
body:
Command: READ:LTE:ACLR:SPECtrum:XY
Description: Returns the frequency and amplitude pairs of the spectrum trace in the LTE ACLR display.
Category: READ

title: Y (READ)
body:
Command: READ:LTE:ACLR:SPECtrum:Y
Description: Returns the amplitude of the spectrum trace in the LTE ACLR display.
Category: READ

title: OBW (READ)
body:
Command: READ:LTE:CHSPectrum:OBW
Description: Returns the Occupied Bandwidth (Hz) of the LTE Channel Spectrum measurement view.
Category: READ

title: CHANnel (READ)
body:
Command: READ:LTE:CHSPectrum:POWer:CHANnel
Description: Returns the Channel Power value in LTE Channel Spectrum display. The result will be in dBm (by default) or in the units chosen from the
Units tab of the Analysis Control panel.
Category: READ

title: SPECtrum (READ)
body:
Command: READ:LTE:CHSPectrum:SPECtrum
Description: Returns spectrum trace data of the LTE Channel Spectrum measurement.
Category: READ

title: ID (READ)
body:
Command: READ:LTE:CONSte:CELL:ID
Description: Queries the Cell ID value LTE Constellation display.
Category: READ

title: ERRor (READ)
body:
Command: READ:LTE:CONSte:FREQuency:ERRor
Description: Queries Frequency Error value in Hz for the LTE Constellation display.
Category: READ

title: ID (READ)
body:
Command: READ:LTE:CONSte:GROUp:ID
Description: Queries the Group ID value for the LTE Constellation display.
Category: READ

title: ID (READ)
body:
Command: READ:LTE:CONSte:SECTor:ID
Description: Queries the Sector ID value for the LTE Constellation display.
Category: READ

title: POWer (READ)
body:
Command: READ:LTE:PVTime:OFFSlot:POWer
Description: Returns the TOff power measurement value in dBm/MHz for the LTE Power vs Time display.
Category: READ

title: STATus (READ)
body:
Command: READ:LTE:PVTime:RESUlts:STATus
Description: Returns the pass or fail status for the LTE Power vs Time measurement.
Category: READ

title: X (READ)
body:
Command: READ:LTE:PVTime:TRACe:X
Description: Returns the horizontal values (time in seconds) for the LTE Power vs. Time trace.
Category: READ

title: XY (READ)
body:
Command: READ:LTE:PVTime:TRACe:XY
Description: Returns the horizontal value (time in seconds) and vertical value (power) for the LTE Power vs. Time trace.
Category: READ

title: Y (READ)
body:
Command: READ:LTE:PVTime:TRACe:Y
Description: Returns the vertical values (power) for the LTE Power vs. Time trace.
Category: READ

title: BANDwidth|BWIDth (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:ACQuisition:{BANDwidth|BWIDth}
Description: Sets or queries the acquisition bandwidth (frequency range of the acquisition) when [SENSe]:ACQuisition:MODE is set to
SAMPles or LENGth.
Category: SENSe

title: LENGth (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:ACQuisition:RECord:FILE:LENGth
Description: Sets or queries how much data (time in milliseconds, seconds, or minutes) is recorded when recording starts. File Length uses the Max
saved files per run: setting to determine how many data files are captured per each recording session.
Category: SENSe

title: ENABle (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:ACQuisition:RECord:FILE:LENGth:ENABle
Description: Sets or queries the file length conditions to determine how much data is recorded. File Length uses theMax saved files per run: setting to
determine how many data files are captured per each recording session.
Category: SENSe

title: UNITs (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:ACQuisition:RECord:FILE:LENGth:UNITs
Description: Sets or queries the units used for the file length setting.
Category: SENSe

title: LOCation (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:ACQuisition:RECord:FILE:LOCation
Description: Sets the file location of the saved data records. To fully support data streaming, the drive selected for recording data must have a minimum
write speed of 300 MB/sec.
Category: SENSe

title: INDex (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:ACQuisition:RECord:FILE:NAME:INDex
Description: Sets or queries the record file name index value. (RSA7100A only)
Category: SENSe

title: MAXimum (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:ACQuisition:RECord:FILE:MAXimum
Description: Sets or queries how many times a record is created, of the specified File Length setting. If the File Length is not enabled, this setting is
ignored. See the command [:SENSe]:ACQuisition:RECord:FILE:LENGth:ENABle.
Category: SENSe

title: BASE (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:ACQuisition:RECord:FILE:NAME:BASE
Description: Sets or queries the base file name that is used for all data recordings. The base name is appended with additional information, creating
unique file names.
Category: SENSe

title: SUFFix (SENSe)
body:
Command: SENSe:ACQuisition:RECord:FILE:NAME:SUFFix
Description: Sets or queries the record file name suffix to append to the output base filename. (RSA7100A only)
Category: SENSe

title: STRucture (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:ACQuisition:RECord:FILE:STRucture
Description: Sets or queries the file structure to use for the recorded data files.
Category: SENSe

title: STOP (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:ACQuisition:RECord:STOP
Description: Starts the data recording. The Record to disk must the set to Record
now. See the command [:SENSe]:ACQuisition:RECord:TYPE. See the command
[:SENSe]:ACQuisition:RECord:FILE:LOCation to specify the file name and location.
Category: SENSe

title: TYPE (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:ACQuisition:RECord:TYPE
Description: Sets or queries when the recording starts streaming data to the specified file.
Category: SENSe

title: MUTE (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:AUDio:DEMod:MUTE
Description: Sets or queries the mute setting for the audio demodulation. Enabling Mute inhibits the audio from playing out through the PC speakers.
Category: SENSe

title: STATe (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:AUDio:DEMod[:STATe]
Description: Sets or queries the audio demodulation state.
Category: SENSe

title: ACTivate (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:AUDio:DEMod:STReam:ACTivate
Description: Sets or queries the Stream to File function. When checked, anytime the Run button is pressed, the audio stream-to-file begins.
Category: SENSe

title: FILE (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:AUDio:DEMod:STReam[:FILE]
Description: Sets the file name and file location of the audio file when streaming to disc.
Category: SENSe

title: TYPE (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:AUDio:DEMod:TYPE
Description: Sets or queries both the audio signal demodulation type and bandwidth.
Category: SENSe

title: VOLume (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:AUDio:DEMod:VOLume
Description: Sets or queries the volume of the demodulated audio signal when played out through the PC speakers.
2000
Category: SENSe

title: RESolution (SENSe)
body:
Command: SENSe:TXGain:{BANDwidth|BWIDth}[:RESolution]
Description: Sets or queries the resolution bandwidth.
Category: SENSe

title: CENTer (SENSe)
body:
Command: SENSe:TXGain:FREQuency:CENTer
Description: Sets or queries the frequency at the center of the selected Span.
Category: SENSe

title: POINts (SENSe)
body:
Command: SENSe:TXGain:FREQuency:POINts
Description: Sets or queries the number of frequency steps in the measurement range.
Category: SENSe

title: SPAN (SENSe)
body:
Command: SENSe:TXGain:FREQuency:SPAN
Description: Sets or queries the difference between the start and stop frequencies (span).
Category: SENSe

title: STARt (SENSe)
body:
Command: SENSe:TXGain:FREQuency:STARt
Description: Sets or queries the lowest frequency in the span.
2500
Category: SENSe

title: STEP (SENSe)
body:
Command: SENSe:TXGain:FREQuency:STEP
Description: Sets or queries the frequency increment. Measurements are made at frequency points from Start Freq to Stop Freq in Step Size
increments.
Category: SENSe

title: STOP (SENSe)
body:
Command: SENSe:TXGain:FREQuency:STOP
Description: Sets or queries the highest frequency in the span.
Category: SENSe

title: LENGth (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:BLUEtooth:ANALysis:LENGth
Description: Sets or returns the analysis length in the Bluetooth displays. Programming a specified length sets
[SENSe]:BLUEtooth:ANALysis:LENGth:AUTO to off.
Category: SENSe

title: ACTual (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:BLUEtooth:ANALysis:LENGth:ACTual
Description: Sets the actual analysis length in the Bluetooth displays.
Category: SENSe

title: AUTO (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:BLUEtooth:ANALysis:LENGth:AUTO
Description: Sets or queries the length mode of the Bluetooth displays.
Category: SENSe

title: BANDwidth|BWIDth (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:BLUEtooth:ANALysis:MEAS[:BANDwidth|BWIDth]
Description: Sets or returns the measurement bandwidth in the Bluetooth measurement.
Category: SENSe

title: SOURce (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:BLUEtooth:ANALysis:MEAS:SOURce
Description: Sets or returns the analysis measurement source in the Bluetooth measurement.
Category: SENSe

title: OFFSet (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:BLUEtooth:ANALysis:OFFSet
Description: Sets or returns the Bluetooth symbol analysis offset.
Category: SENSe

title: AUTO (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:BLUEtooth:ANALysis:OFFSet:AUTO
Description: Determines whether to set the carrier frequency error automatically or manually in the Bluetooth displays.
2020
Category: SENSe

title: UNITs (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:BLUEtooth:ANALysis:TIMe:UNITs
Description: Sets the time units for the Bluetooth Constellation settings.
Category: SENSe

title: MODE (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:BLUEtooth([:BANDwidth]|:BWIDth):MODE
Description: Sets or returns the measurement bandwidth mode
Category: SENSe

title: TINTerval (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:BLUEtooth([:BANDwidth]|:BWIDth):TINTerval
Description: Sets or returns the measurement bandwidth frequency span
Category: SENSe

title: AUTO (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:BLUEtooth([:BANDwidth]|:BWIDth):TINTerval:AUTO
Description: Sets or returns the bandwidth to Auto or Manual
Category: SENSe

title: SHOW (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:BLUEtooth:CONStellation:PREFs:GRATicule:SHOW
Description: Show or hides the graticule state in the Bluetooth Constellation display.
Category: SENSe

title: SHOW (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:BLUEtooth:CONStellation:PREFs:MARKERS:SHOW
Description: Show or hides the marker readout in the Bluetooth Constellation display.
Category: SENSe

title: RADIx (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:BLUEtooth:CONStellation:PREFs:RADIx
Description: Sets or returns the radix of the marker readout in the Bluetooth Constellation display.
Category: SENSe

title: CONTent (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:BLUEtooth:CONStellation:TRACE<x>:CONTent
Description: Sets or returns the appearance of the specified trace (Trace 1 or Trace 2) in the Bluetooth Constellation display.
Category: SENSe

title: FREEze (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:BLUEtooth:CONStellation:TRACe<x>:FREEze
Description: Sets or freezes the updates of the specified trace (Trace 1 or Trace 2) in the Bluetooth Constellation display.
Category: SENSe

title: POINtsPerSymbol (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:BLUEtooth:CONSTellation:TRACE<x>:POINtsPerSymbol
Description: Sets or returns the number of points per symbol (how many points to use between symbols) for the specified trace (Trace 1 or Trace 2) in
the Bluetooth Constellation display.
Category: SENSe

title: SHOW (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:BLUEtooth:CONStellation:TRACe<x>:SHOW
Description: Shows or hides the specified trace (Trace 1 or Trace 2) in the Bluetooth Constellation display.
Category: SENSe

title: FONE (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:BLUEtooth:DELTa:AVERage:FONE
Description: Sets or returns the average frequency for the low deviation pattern in the Bluetooth measurement.
Category: SENSe

title: FTWO (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:BLUEtooth:DELTa:AVERage:FTWO
Description: Sets or returns the average frequency for the high deviation pattern in the Bluetooth measurement.
Category: SENSe

title: OCTEt (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:BLUEtooth:FDVTime:TRACE:OCTEt
Description: Returns the selected octet number on the Frequency Deviation vs. Time display.
Category: SENSe

title: COUNt (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:BLUEtooth:FDVTime:TRACE:OCTEt:TOTAL:COUNt
Description: Returns the total octet value to be selected on the Frequency Deviation vs. Time display.
For the standard low-energy range the maximum number of octets available is 36. For standard basic rate range, the maximum number of
octets depends on the packet type.
2028
Category: SENSe

title: MODE (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:BLUEtooth:FDVTime:TRACE:VIEW:MODE
Description: Sets or queries view mode in the Frequency Deviation vs. Time display.
Category: SENSe

title: ALPHa (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:BLUEtooth:FILTer:ALPHa
Description: Sets or returns the alpha filter factor (α/BT) in the Bluetooth analysis.
Category: SENSe

title: MEASurement (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:BLUEtooth:FILTer:MEASurement
Description: Sets or returns the measurement filter in the Bluetooth measurements.
Category: SENSe

title: REFerence (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:BLUEtooth:FILTer:REFerence
Description: Sets or returns the reference filter used in the Bluetooth measurements.
Category: SENSe

title: DEViation (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:BLUEtooth:FREQuency:DEViation
Description: Sets or returns the frequency deviation in the Bluetooth analysis.
Category: SENSe

title: AUTO (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:BLUEtooth:FREQuency:DEViation:AUTO
Description: Determines whether to detect the frequency deviation automatically or manually for the Bluetooth measurement.
Category: SENSe

title: ERRor (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:BLUEtooth:FREQuency:ERRor
Description: Sets or returns the frequency error in the Bluetooth analysis parameters (when Auto is off).
2032
Category: SENSe

title: AUTO (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:BLUEtooth:FREQuency:ERRor:AUTO
Description: Determines whether the frequency error is set to Auto or Manual.
To enter a manual value, when Auto is Off, use the [SENSe]:BLUEtooth:FREQuency:ERRor command.
Category: SENSe

title: LIMItx (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:BLUEtooth:INBEmissions:POWEr:LIMIt<x>
Description: Sets or queries the limits of the Bluetooth InBand Emission settings.
When <x> = 1, then commands set or query the fTX ± 2 MHz limit.
When <x> = 2, then commands set or query the fTX ± (3 + n) MHz limit.
Category: SENSe

title: CLASs (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:BLUEtooth:POWer:CLASs
Description: Sets or queries the power class of the Bluetooth standard.
Category: SENSe

title: STANdard (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:BLUEtooth:STANdard
Description: Sets or queries the Bluetooth standard.
Category: SENSe

title: MAX (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:BLUEtooth:SUMMary:LIMIt:BDR:DELTa:FONE:AVERage:MAX
Description: Sets or returns the maximum limit of the Basic Rate ΔF1 average in the Bluetooth Constellation Summary display.
Category: SENSe

title: MIN (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:BLUEtooth:SUMMary:LIMIt:BDR:DELTa:FONE:AVERage:MIN
Description: Sets or returns the minimum limit of the Basic Rate ΔF1 average in the Bluetooth Constellation Summary display.
Category: SENSe

title: PERcent (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:BLUEtooth:SUMMary:LIMIt:BDR:DELTa:FTWO:PERcent
Description: Sets or returns the limit of the Basic Rate ΔF2 percentage in the Bluetooth Summary display.
Category: SENSe

title: RESult (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:BLUEtooth:SUMMary:LIMIt:BDR:DELTa:FTWO:RESult
Description: Sets or returns the limit of the Basic Rate ΔF2 result in the Bluetooth Constellation Summary display.
Category: SENSe

title: FNFZero (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:BLUEtooth:SUMMary:LIMIt:BDR:MAX:DRIFt:FNFZero
Description: Sets or returns the limit of the Basic Rate maximum drift fn–f0 in the Bluetooth Summary display.
Category: SENSe

title: OFFSet (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:BLUEtooth:SUMMary:LIMIt:BDR:MAX:FREQuency:OFFSet
Description: Sets or returns the limit of the Basic Rate maximum frequency offset in the Bluetooth Summary display.
Category: SENSe

title: PREAmBDR (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:BLUEtooth:SUMMary:LIMIt:BDR:MAX:FREQuency:OFFSet:PREAmBDR
Description: Sets or returns the limit of the Basic Rate maximum frequency offset value from the preamble region in the Bluetooth Summary display.
Category: SENSe

title: MAX (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:BLUEtooth:SUMMary:LIMIt:BDR:OPOWer:CLASs:ONE:AVERage:MAX
Description: Sets or returns the limit of the Basic Rate Power Class 1 maximum average outputvpower value in the Bluetooth Summary display.
Category: SENSe

title: MIN (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:BLUEtooth:SUMMary:LIMIt:BDR:OPOWer:CLASs:ONE:AVERage:MIN
Description: Sets or returns the limit of the Basic Rate Power Class 1 minimum average output power value in the Bluetooth Summary display.
Category: SENSe

title: MAX (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:BLUEtooth:SUMMary:LIMIt:BDR:OPOWer:CLASs:ONE:PEAK:MAX
Description: Sets or returns the limit of the Basic Rate Power Class 1 maximum peak output power value in the Bluetooth Constellation Summary
display.
Category: SENSe

title: MIN (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:BLUEtooth:SUMMary:LIMIt:BDR:OPOWer:CLASs:ONE:PEAK:MIN
Description: Sets or returns the limit of the Basic Rate Power Class 1 minimum peak output power value in the Bluetooth Summary display.
Category: SENSe

title: MAX (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:BLUEtooth:SUMMary:LIMIt:BDR:OPOWer:CLASs:THREe:AVERage:MAX
Description: Sets or returns the limit of the Basic Rate Power Class 3 maximum average output power value in the Bluetooth Summary display.
Category: SENSe

title: MIN (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:BLUEtooth:SUMMary:LIMIt:BDR:OPOWer:CLASs:THREe:AVERage:MIN
Description: Sets or returns the limit of the Basic Rate Power Class 3 minimum average output power value in the Bluetooth Constellation Summary
display.
Category: SENSe

title: MAX (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:BLUEtooth:SUMMary:LIMIt:BDR:OPOWer:CLASs:THREe:PEAK:MAX
Description: Sets or returns the limit of the Basic Rate Power Class 3 maximum peak output power value in the Bluetooth Constellation Summary

title: OPTimization (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:ACQuisition:OPTimization
Description: Sets or queries the method of the global gain and input bandwidth optimization.
This command replaces the following commands:
• [:SENSe]:ACPower:OPTimize:SPAN(?)
• [:SENSe]:DPX:{BANDwidth|BWIDth}:OPTimization(?)
• [:SENSe]:SGRam:{BANDwidth|BWIDTH}:OPTimization(?)
• [:SENSe]:SPECtrum:{BANDwidth|BWIDTH}:OPTimization(?)
• [:SENSe]:MCPower:OPTimize:SPAN(?)
When a setup or tiq file that was created with an earlier version of software is recalled, the instrument selects a setting for the global
optimization that will result in the recalled measurement-specific optimizations. If recalled measurements have conflicting optimizations,
then the instrument selects one of the optimizations and issues a GPIB event (event number 108).
Category: SENSe

title: SAMPles (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:ACQuisition:SAMPles

[SENSe]:ACQuisition:MODE is set to SAMPles.

LENGth.

title: DITHer (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:ANALysis:ADVanced:DITHer
Description: Determines whether to enable or disable dithering, or set it automatically.

Dither is a random low-level signal consisting of white noise of one quantizing level peak-to-peak amplitude which may be added to an
analog signal prior to sampling for the purpose of minimizing quantization error.
Category: SENSe

title: STATus (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:ANALysis:ADVanced:DITHer:HWARe:STATus
Description: Returns the dithering hardware status.
Category: SENSe

Default source is Source 1.

The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
