title: BANDwidth|BWIDth (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:SPURious:CARRier:{BANDwidth|BWIDth}
Description: Sets or queries the resolution bandwidth (RBW) to measure the carrier power. This command is valid
when [SENSe]:SPURious:REFerence is set to CARRier. Programming a specified RBW sets
[SENSe]:SPURious:CARRier:BANDwidth [RESolution]:AUTO OFF.
Category: SENSe

title: INTegration (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:SPURious:CARRier:{BANDwidth|BWIDth}:INTegration
Description: Sets or queries the integration bandwidth to calculate the carrier power. This command is valid when
[SENSe]:SPURious:REFerence is set to CARRier.
Category: SENSe

title: RESolution (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:SPURious:CARRier:{BANDwidth|BWIDth}[:RESolution]
Description: Sets or queries the resolution bandwidth (RBW) to measure the carrier power. This command is valid
when [SENSe]:SPURious:REFerence is set to CARRier. Programming a specified RBW sets
[SENSe]:SPURious:CARRier:BANDwidth [RESolution]:AUTO OFF.
Category: SENSe

title: AUTO (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:SPURious:CARRier:{BANDwidth|BWIDth}[:RESolution]:AUTO
Description: Determines whether to set the resolution bandwidth (RBW) automatically or manually when the power reference is set to carrier
([SENSe]:SPURious: REFerence is set to CARRier) in the Spurious measurement.
Category: SENSe

title: DETection (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:SPURious:CARRier:DETection
Description: Sets or queries the carrier detection method. This command is valid when [SENSe]:SPURious:REFerence is set to CARRier.
Category: SENSe

title: FREQuency (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:SPURious:CARRier:FREQuency
Description: Sets or queries the carrier frequency in the Spurious measurement. This command is valid when
[SENSe]:SPURious:REFerence is set to CARRier.
Category: SENSe

title: THReshold (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:SPURious:CARRier:THReshold
Description: Sets or queries the threshold level to detect the carrier in the Spurious measurement. This command is valid when
[SENSe]:SPURious:REFerence is set to CARRier.
Category: SENSe

title: RESults (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:SPURious:CLEar:RESults
Description: Restarts multi-trace functions (Average and Max Hold).
2472
Category: SENSe

title: OVERlap (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:SPURious[:FREQuency]:OVERlap
Description: Queries whether any of the frequency ranges (A to T) overlap, including the carrier when [SENSe]:SPURious:REFerence is set
to CARRier.
Note: If there are any overlaps between the ranges, the measurement will not run correctly.
Category: SENSe

title: LIST (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:SPURious:LIST
Description: Sets or queries how to list the spurious signals in the Spurious measurement.
Category: SENSe

title: MODE (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:SPURious:MODE
Description: Sets or queries the frequency range mode in the Spurious measurement.
Category: SENSe

title: OPTimization (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:SPURious:OPTimization
Description: Sets or queries the method of optimizing the gain and input bandwidth in the Spurious measurement.
Category: SENSe

title: COUNt (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:SPURious:POINts:COUNt
Description: Sets or queries the number of sample points on the spectrum trace per range in the Spurious measurement.
Category: SENSe

title: VIDeo (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:SPURious:RANGe<x>:BANDwidth:VIDeo
Description: Sets or queries the video bandwidth (VBW) in the specified frequency range. Programming a specified VBW sets
[SENSe]:SPURious:BANDwidth:VIDeo STATe OFF.
The parameter <x> = 1 to 20, representing Range A to T, respectively.
Category: SENSe

title: STATe (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:SPURious:RANGe<x>:BANDwidth:VIDeo:STATe
Description: Determines whether to enable or disable the video bandwidth (VBW) in the specified frequency range.
The parameter <x> = 1 to 20, representing Range A to T, respectively.
Category: SENSe

title: DETection (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:SPURious:RANGe<x>:DETection
Description: Sets or queries the spurious detection method in the specified frequency range. The parameter <x> = 1 to 20, representing Range A to T,
respectively.
Category: SENSe

title: EXCursion (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:SPURious:RANGe<x>:EXCursion
Description: Sets or queries the excursion level (how far down the signal must drop between spurious emissions) in the specified frequency range.

The parameter <x> = 1 to 20, representing Range A to T, respectively.
Category: SENSe

title: SHAPe (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:SPURious:RANGe<x>:FILTer[:SHAPe]
Description: Sets or queries the filter shape to search the specified frequency range for spurious signals.
The parameter <x> = 1 to 20, representing Range A to T, respectively.
Category: SENSe

title: BANDwidth (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:SPURious:RANGe<x>:FILTer[:SHAPe]:BANDwidth
Description: Sets or queries the filter bandwidth to search the specified frequency range for spurious signals.
The parameter <x> = 1 to 20, representing Range A to T, respectively.
Category: SENSe

title: AUTO (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:SPURious:RANGe<x>:FILTer[:SHAPe]:BANDwidth:AUTO
Description: Determines whether to set the filter bandwidth automatically or manually for the specified frequency range in the Spurious measurement.
The parameter <x> = 1 to 20, representing Range A to T, respectively.
Category: SENSe

title: STARt (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:SPURious:RANGe<x>:FREQuency:STARt
Description: Sets or queries the start frequency of the specified range in the Spurious measurement.
The parameter <x> = 1 to 20, representing Range A to T, respectively.
Note: The frequency ranges must not be overlapped. Use the [SENSe]:SPURious[:FREQuency]:OVERlap? query
to check whether there is any overlap.
Category: SENSe

title: STOP (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:SPURious:RANGe<x>:FREQuency:STOP
Description: Sets or queries the stop frequency of the specified range in the Spurious measurement.
The parameter <x> = 1 to 20, representing Range A to T, respectively.
Note: The frequency ranges must not be overlapped. Use the [SENSe]:SPURious[:FREQuency]:OVERlap? query
to check whether there is any overlap.
2480
Category: SENSe

title: STARt (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:SPURious:RANGe<x>:LIMit:ABSolute:STARt
Description: Sets or queries the absolute amplitude of the limits at the start (left edge) of the specified range in the Spurious measurement. This
command is valid when [SENSe]:SPURious:RANGe<x>:LIMit:MASK is set to ABS, AND, or OR.
The parameter <x> = 1 to 20, representing Range A to T, respectively.
Category: SENSe

title: STOP (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:SPURious:RANGe<x>:LIMit:ABSolute:STOP
Description: Sets or queries the absolute amplitude of the limits at the stop (right edge) of the specified range in the Spurious measurement. This
command is valid when [SENSe]:SPURious:RANGe<x>:LIMit:MASK is set to ABS, AND, or OR.
The parameter <x> = 1 to 20, representing Range A to T, respectively.
Category: SENSe

title: MASK (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:SPURious:RANGe<x>:LIMit:MASK
Description: Sets or queries the limit mask function mode for the specified range in the Spurious measurement.
The parameter <x> = 1 to 20, representing Range A to T, respectively.
Category: SENSe

title: STARt (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:SPURious:RANGe<x>:LIMit:RELative:STARt
Description: Sets or queries the relative amplitude of the limits at the start (left edge) of the specified range in the Spurious measurement. This
command is valid when [SENSe]:SPURious:RANGe<x>:LIMit:MASK is set to REL, AND, or OR.
The parameter <x> = 1 to 20, representing Range A to T, respectively.
Category: SENSe

title: STOP (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:SPURious:RANGe<x>:LIMit:RELative:STOP
Description: Sets or queries the relative amplitude of the limits at the stop (right edge) of the specified range in the Spurious measurement. This
command is valid when [SENSe]:SPURious:RANGe<x>:LIMit:MASK is set to REL, AND, or OR.
The parameter <x> = 1 to 20, representing Range A to T, respectively.
Category: SENSe

title: STATe (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:SPURious:RANGe<x>:STATe
Description: Determines whether to enable or disable the frequency range in the Spurious measurement.
The parameter <x> = 1 to 20, representing Range A to T, respectively.
Category: SENSe

title: THReshold (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:SPURious:RANGe<x>:THReshold
Description: Sets or queries the threshold level to detect spurious signals in the specified frequency range.
The parameter <x> = 1 to 20, representing Range A to T, respectively.
Category: SENSe

title: REFerence (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:SPURious:REFerence
Description: Sets or queries the power reference in the Spurious measurement.
Category: SENSe

title: POWer (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:SPURious:REFerence:MANual:POWer
Description: Sets or queries the reference power level in the Spurious measurement. This command is valid when
[SENSe]:SPURious:REFerence is set to MANual.
Category: SENSe

title: STATe (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:SQUality[:MEASview<y>]:EVM:ALL[:STATe]
Description: Sets or queries whether or not the display of both EVM and Offset EVM results is enabled. Offset EVM appears only for OQPSK and
SOQPSK modulation types.
The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).
2486
Category: SENSe

title: CHANnel (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:SVPC:ACQuisition:CONTrol:CHANnel
Description: Sets or queries the Channel Settings checkbox state.
Category: SENSe

title: TRIGger (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:SVPC:ACQuisition:CONTrol:TRIGger
Description: Sets or queries the Trigger Settings checkbox state.
Category: SENSe

title: CENTer (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:TOVerview[:MEASview<y>]:FREQuency:CENTer
Description: Sets or queries the center frequency in the time overview.
Category: SENSe

title: MAXTracepoints (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:TOVerview[:MEASview<y>]:MAXTracepoints
Description: Sets or queries the maximum trace points in the time overview.
2492
Category: SENSe

title: DIVision (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:TOVerview[:MEASview<y>]:TIME:PER:DIVision
Description: This command sets or queries the time per division (dB/div) value in the Time Overview display.
Category: SENSe

title: LENGth (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:WLAN:ANALysis:LENGth
Description: Specifies the length of the analysis period to use in the WLAN analysis time settings. Analysis length is specified in either symbols or
seconds, depending on the Units setting. Use [SENSe]:WLAN:UNIT:TIME to set the units.
Programming a specified length sets [SENSe]:WLAN:ANALysis:AUTO to OFF.
Category: SENSe

title: ACTual (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:WLAN:ANALysis:LENGth:ACTual
Description: Returns the value of the actual analysis length for the WLAN analysis time settings.
Category: SENSe

title: AUTO (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:WLAN:ANALysis:LENGth:AUTO
Description: Sets the analysis length value based on the requirements of the selected display. To set the analysis length manually, use the command.
Category: SENSe

title: OFFSet (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:WLAN:ANALysis:OFFSet
Description: Specifies or queries the analysis offset in the symbol interval in WLAN analysis parameters. This value is a percentage of the Guard
Interval. The allowable range is -100% to 0%. -100% positions the FFT to start at the beginning of the Guard Interval, 0% positions it to
start at the end of the Guard Interval. The default value is -50% which usually gives the best measurement results.
Category: SENSe

title: BURSt (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:WLAN:BURSt
Description: Specifies the burst to measure when multiple bursts are present in an acquisition, in the WLAN analysis parameters. The index number of
the first burst is 1, second burst is 2, etc.
Category: SENSe

title: BWIDth (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:WLAN:CHANnel[:BANDwidth|:BWIDth]
Description: Specifies or queries a nominal channel bandwidth to use for the WLAN measurements.
To automatically set the subcarrier spacing for the WLAN measurements, use the command
Category: SENSe

title: TRAining (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:WLAN:EQUalizer:TRAining
Description: Specifies the method to use for estimating channel frequency response and equalization for WLAN measurments (either PREamble or
PDATa). This control can be used to diagnose changes in frequency response over the signal packet.
Category: SENSe

title: LENGth (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:WLAN:FFT:LENGth
Description: Returns the fixed FFT length for the WLAN measurements.
Category: SENSe

title: SRATe (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:WLAN:FFT:SRATe
Description: Returns the FFT sample rate for the WLAN measurements, in samples per second, based on the bandwidth or subcarrier setting.
Category: SENSe

title: ERRor (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:WLAN:FREQuency:ERRor
Description: Sets or queries the frequency error in the WLAN analysis parameters. The entered value is used by the analysis as a fixed frequency
offset. This is useful when
the exact frequency offset of the signal is known. To set the frequency error automatically, use the command
Category: SENSe

title: AUTO (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:WLAN:FREQuency:ERRor:AUTO
Description: Turns on the automatic determination of the frequency error in the WLAN analysis parameters. To enter a value manually when Auto is
turned off, use the command [SENSe]:WLAN:FREQuency:ERRor.
Category: SENSe

title: INTerval (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:WLAN:GUARd:INTerval
Description: Sets or queries the Guard Interval to use in the input signal in the WLAN measurements.
Category: SENSe

title: QAM1024 (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:WLAN:MODulation:DETect:QAM1024
Description: Sets or queries whether or not 1024QAM detection is enabled.
Category: SENSe

title: SOURce (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:WLAN:MODulation:SOURce
Description: Specifies the method of data symbol modulation identification in the WLAN analysis parameters (either Auto Detect, Manual, or Auto from
SIG).
Category: SENSe

title: STATe (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:WLAN:PILot:TRACking:AMPLitude:STATe
Description: Specifies to use the pilot subcarrier to correct amplitude variations over the packet for WLAN measurements. Note: the default setting is (false).
Category: SENSe

title: STATe (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:WLAN:PILot:TRACking:PHASe:STATe
Description: Specifies to use the pilot subcarrier to correct phase variations over the packet, in WLAN measurements. Note: the default setting is (true).
Category: SENSe

title: STATe (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:WLAN:PILot:TRACking:TIMing:STATe
Description: Specifies to use the pilot subcarrier to correct timing variations over the packet, in WLAN measurements. Note: the default setting is (false).
Category: SENSe

title: RADix (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:WLAN:RADix
Description: Specifies or queries the format for displaying the symbol values in the WLAN setting preferences (either binary or HEX).
Category: SENSe

title: SCARriers (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:WLAN:SCARriers
Description: Sets or queries which subcarriers to display in the WLAN view.
Category: SENSe

title: STARt (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:WLAN:SCARriers:RANGe:STARt
Description: Sets or queries the start value of the subcarrier range to be displayed in the WLAN view.
2556
Category: SENSe

title: STOP (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:WLAN:SCARriers:RANGe:STOP
Description: Sets or queries the stop value of the subcarrier range to be displayed in the WLAN view.
Category: SENSe

title: INDex (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:WLAN:SCARriers:SINGle:INDex
Description: Specifies or queries the single subcarrier to display in the WLAN view.
Category: SENSe

title: SPACing (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:WLAN:SCARriers:SPACing
Description: Sets or queries the subcarrier spacing in the WLAN measurements.
Category: SENSe

title: AUTO (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:WLAN:SCARriers:SPACing:AUTO
Description: When turned on, automatically sets the subcarrier spacing in the WLAN measurements.
2558
Category: SENSe

title: TYPE (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:WLAN:SIGNal:STANdard:TYPE
Description: Sets or queries the WLAN signal standard.
Category: SENSe

title: DERotation (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:WLAN:SubCARrier:DERotation
Description: Turning on this state allows some displays to show subcarriers with or without the Gamma subcarrier phase rotation removed. Gamma
phase rotation is applied to 802.11n and 802.11ac subcarriers in defined subranges depending on the Channel Bandwidth selected (only
for Channel Bandwidths of 40 MHz and above).
When derotation is turned off, the rotation is not removed, which provides a direct view of the physical modulation on the channel. With
derotation turned on, the rotation is removed, allowing easier decoding of the underlying data content.
Only the Constellation and Symbol Table results are affected by this control.
Note: This control is only shown for 802.11n and 802.11ac standard selections for channel bandwidths greater than or equal to MHz. It is not present for all others.
Category: SENSe

title: RESults (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:WLAN:SUMMary:CLEar:RESults
Description: Clears the results in the WLAN Summary measurement.
Category: SENSe

title: COUNt (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:WLAN:SUMMary:EVM:RMS:AVERage:COUNt
Description: Specifies the maximum number of bursts to average for the WLAN Summary EVM display. The burst average function must be enabled
using the command [SENSe]:WLAN:SUMMary:EVM:RMS:AVERage:COUNt:ENABle.
Category: SENSe

title: ENABle (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:WLAN:SUMMary:EVM:RMS:AVERage:COUNt:ENABle
Description: Enables the burst average function in the WLAN Summary EVM settings. To specify the maximum number of bursts to average, use the
command [SENSe]:WLAN:SUMMary:EVM:RMS:AVERage:COUNt.
Category: SENSe

title: IQ (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:WLAN:SWAP:IQ
Description: Specifies to swap the I and Q components of a signal in the WLAN analysis parameters. Doing so compensates the input signal for
spectral inversion.
Category: SENSe

title: SYMBols|CHIPs (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:WLAN:SYMBols|CHIPs
Description: Specifies or queries which symbols or chips are displayed in the WLAN Symbol Table.
Use "CHIPs" for 802.11b and "SYMBols" for all other standards. The DAT, PRAM and HEAD enumerations apply to 802.11b only.
2562
Category: SENSe

title: MAX (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:WLAN:SYMBols|CHIPs:MAX
Description: Sets or queries the value for the maximum number of symbols/chips to analyze for the WLAN Symbol Table.
Use "CHIPs" for 802.11b and "SYMBols" for all other standards.
Category: SENSe

title: STATe (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:WLAN:SYMBols|CHIPs:MAX:STATe
Description: Turns on or off the “maximum data symbols to analyze” feature for the WLAN Symbol Table. To specify the maximum number of
symbols/chips to analyze, use the command.
Use "CHIPs" for 802.11b and "SYMBols" for all other standards.
Category: SENSe

title: COUNt (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:WLAN:SYMBols|CHIPs:RANGe:COUNt
Description: Sets or queries the number of symbols or chips to display when displaying a range results in the WLAN Symbol Table.
Use "CHIPs" for 802.11b and "SYMBols" for all other standards.
Category: SENSe

title: STARt (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:WLAN:SYMBols|CHIPs:RANGe:STARt
Description: Sets or queries the symbols/chips start value when displaying a range of results in the WLAN Symbol Table.
Use "CHIPs" for 802.11b and "SYMBols" for all other standards.
Category: SENSe

title: STOP (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:WLAN:SYMBols|CHIPs:RANGe:STOP
Description: Sets or queries the symbols or chips stop value when displaying a range of results in the WLAN Symbol Table.
Use "CHIPs" for 802.11b and "SYMBols" for all other standards.
Category: SENSe

title: INDex (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:WLAN:SYMBols|CHIPs:SINGle:INDex
Description: Sets or queries the symbol or chip to use when displaying results from a single symbol in the WLAN Symbol Table.
Use "CHIPs" for 802.11b and "SYMBols" for all other standards.
Category: SENSe

title: FREQuency (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:WLAN:UNIT:FREQuency
Description: Specifies or queries whether the displayed frequency units are frequency (Hz) or subcarrier in the WLAN settings.
Category: SENSe

title: TIME (SENSe)
body:
Command: [SENSe]:WLAN:UNIT:TIME
Description: Specifies or queries whether the displayed time units are seconds, symbols or chips in the WLAN settings. Chips is only a valid option for
802.11b, and Symbols is valid for all other standards.
Category: SENSe

title: RESolution (SENSe)
body:
Command: SENSe:LTE:ACLR:BANDwidth:RESolution
Description: Sets or queries the resolution bandwidth (RBW) in the LTE ACLR display.
Category: SENSe

title: VIDeo (SENSe)
body:
Command: SENSe:LTE:ACLR:BANDwidth:VIDeo
Description: Sets or queries the video bandwidth (VBW) in the LTE ACLR display.
Category: SENSe

title: STATe (SENSe)
body:
Command: SENSe:LTE:ACLR:BANDwidth:VIDeo:STATe
Description: Sets or queries enabling or disabling the video bandwidth (VBW) in the LTE ACLR display.
Category: SENSe

title: IBANdwidth (SENSe)
body:
Command: SENSe:LTE:ACLR:CHANnel:IBANdwidth
Description: Sets or queries the reference channel integration bandwidth for the LTE ACLR display.
Category: SENSe

title: WIDTh (SENSe)
body:
Command: SENSe:LTE:ACLR:CHANnel:WIDTh
Description: Sets or queries the reference channel width for the LTE ACLR display.
Category: SENSe

title: RESults (SENSe)
body:
Command: SENSe:LTE:ACLR:CLEar:RESults
Description: Clears the results in the LTE ACLR measurement.
2196
Category: SENSe

title: FREQuency (SENSe)
body:
Command: SENSe:LTE:ACLR:FREQuency
Description: Sets or queries the measurement center frequency in the LTE ACLR display.
Category: SENSe

title: MFILter (SENSe)
body:
Command: SENSe:LTE:ACLR:MFILter
Description: Sets or queries the shape of the filter for the LTE ACLR measurement.
Category: SENSe

title: REALtime (SENSe)
body:
Command: SENSe:LTE:ACLR:MODE:REALtime
Description: Sets or queries the real-time LTE ACLR measurement mode. When set to real-time, the reference channel and all requested adjacent
channel regions are acquired together. When real-time if off, the measurement is done in Swept mode (an acquisition for each adjacent
band).
Category: SENSe

title: ACQuire (SENSe)
body:
Command: SENSe:LTE:ACLR:NFLoor:ACQuire
Description: Enables measurement of the noise floor for the LTE ACLR measurement.
Category: SENSe

title: CORRected (SENSe)
body:
Command: SENSe:LTE:ACLR:NFLoor:CORRected
Description: Returns whether or not noise correction was applied on the last acquisition in the LTE ACLR measurement.
Category: SENSe

title: STATe (SENSe)
body:
Command: SENSe:LTE:ACLR:NFLoor:STATe
Description: Determines whether to enable or disable the correction for noise floor for the LTE ACLR measurement.
Category: SENSe

title: RESolution (SENSe)
body:
Command: SENSe:LTE:ACLR:OFFSet<x>:BANDwidth:RESolution
Description: Sets or queries the resolution bandwidth (RBW) value in the specified row in the Offsets & Limits table for the LTE ACLR display.
Category: SENSe

title: COUNt (SENSe)
body:
Command: SENSe:LTE:ACLR:OFFSet<x>:BANDwidth:RESolution:COUNt
Description: Sets or queries the count for the resolution bandwidths in the specified row in the Offsets & Limits table for the LTE ACLR display.
Category: SENSe

title: FILTer (SENSe)
body:
Command: SENSe:LTE:ACLR:OFFSet<X>:BANDwidth:RESolution:FILTer
Description: Sets or queries the filter bandwidth resolution under Offset & Limit table tab for the LTE ACLR display.
Category: SENSe

title: VIDeo (SENSe)
body:
Command: SENSe:LTE:ACLR:OFFSet<x>:BANDwidth:RESolution:VIDeo
Description: Sets or queries the resolution video bandwidth (VBW) value in the specified row in the Offsets & Limits table for the LTE ACLR display.
Category: SENSe

title: STATe (SENSe)
body:
Command: SENSe:LTE:ACLR:OFFSet<x>:BANDwidth:RESolution:VIDeo:STATe
Description: Sets or queries whether to enable or disable the video resolution bandwidth (VBW) in the specified row in the Offsets & Limits table for the
LTE ACLR display.
Category: SENSe

title: DETection (SENSe)
body:
Command: SENSe:LTE:ACLR:OFFSet<x>:DETection
Description: Sets or queries the detection settings for all offsets for the LTE ACLR display.
Category: SENSe

title: BANDwidth (SENSe)
body:
Command: SENSe:LTE:ACLR:OFFSet<x>:FREQuency:BANDwidth
Description: Sets or queries the frequency bandwidth value for the specified row of the Offsets & Limits table for the LTE ACLR display.
Category: SENSe

title: OFFSet (SENSe)
body:
Command: SENSe:LTE:ACLR:OFFSet<x>:FREQuency:OFFSet
Description: Sets or queries the frequency offset value of the specified row in the Offsets & Limits table for the LTE ACLR display.
Category: SENSe

title: ABSolute (SENSe)
body:
Command: SENSe:LTE:ACLR:OFFSet<x>:LIMit:ABSolute
Description: Sets or queries the absolute limit value of the row in the Offset & Limit table tab for the LTE ACLR display.
Category: SENSe

title: MASK (SENSe)
body:
Command: SENSe:LTE:ACLR:OFFSet<x>:LIMit:MASK
Description: Sets or queries the limit mask setting for the specified row in the Offsets & Limits table for the LTE ACLR display.
Category: SENSe

title: RELative (SENSe)
body:
Command: SENSe:LTE:ACLR:OFFSet<x>:LIMit:RELative
Description: Sets or queries the relative limit value of channel under Offset & Limit table tab for the LTE ACLR display.
2206
Category: SENSe

title: SIDE (SENSe)
body:
Command: SENSe:LTE:ACLR:OFFSet<x>:LIMit:SIDE
Description: Sets or queries the side of the offset limit for the specified row in the Offsets & Limits table for the LTE ACLR display.
Category: SENSe

title: STATe (SENSe)
body:
Command: SENSe:LTE:ACLR:OFFSet<x>:STATe
Description: Sets or queries the specified row of the Offsets & Limits table for the LTE ACLR display.
Category: SENSe

title: COUNt (SENSe)
body:
Command: SENSe:LTE:ACLR:POINts:COUNt
Description: Sets or queries the number of points used for the trace in the LTE ACLR measurement.
Category: SENSe

title: AUTO (SENSe)
body:
Command: SENSe:LTE:ACLR:REFerence:MAGNitude:AUTO
Description: Sets or queries the reference magnitude to automatic or manual for the LTE ACLR measurement.
Category: SENSe

title: DETection (SENSe)
body:
Command: SENSe:LTE:ACLR:REFerence:MAGNitude:DETection
Description: Sets or queries the reference magnitude detection for the LTE ACLR measurement.
Category: SENSe

title: MAGNitude (SENSe)
body:
Command: SENSe:LTE:ACLR:REFerence:MAGNitude
Description: Sets or queries the reference power magnitude for the LTE ACLR measurement.
Category: SENSe

title: AUTO (SENSe)
body:
Command: SENSe:LTE:ACLR:STEP:AUTO
Description: Determines whether to automatically update the center frequency increment/decrement step size in the LTE ACLR display. The step value
controls the magnitude of each step when using the front panel controls or arrow key on the keyboard.
Category: SENSe

title: MAGNitude (SENSe)
body:
Command: SENSe:LTE:ACLR:STEP:MAGNitude
Description: Sets or queries the step magnitude for the center frequency in the LTE ACLR display.
Category: SENSe

title: ALL (SYSTem)
body:
Command: SYSTem:ERRor:ALL
Description: Queries the error/event queue for all the unread items and removes them from the queue. The response is a comma separated list of
number, string pairs in FIFO order. For details of the error messages, refer to (See Table 3-16.)
Category: SYSTem

title: ALL (SYSTem)
body:
Command: SYSTem:ERRor:CODE:ALL
Description: Queries the error/event queue for all the unread items and removes them from the queue. The response returns a comma separated list of
only the error/event code numbers in FIFO order. For details of the error messages, refer to (See Table 3-16.)
Category: SYSTem

title: NEXT (SYSTem)
body:
Command: SYSTem:ERRor:CODE[:NEXT]
Description: Queries the error/event queue for the next item and removes it from the queue. The response returns only the error/event code number
omitting the string. Except for the shortened response, the query operates identically to SYSTem:ERRor[:NEXT]?. For details of the error
messages, refer to (See Table 3-16.)
Category: SYSTem

title: COUNt (SYSTem)
body:
Command: SYSTem:ERRor:COUNt
Description: Queries the error/event queue for the number of unread items. As errors and events may occur at any time, more items may be present in
the queue at the time it is actually read.
Category: SYSTem

title: NEXT (SYSTem)
body:
Command: SYSTem:ERRor[:NEXT]
Description: Queries the error/event queue for the next item and removes it from the queue. The response returns the full queue item consisting of an
integer and a string. For details of the error messages, refer to the Measurement status conditions table in the PDF of the Programmer
manual for your product.
Category: SYSTem

Description: Restores the analyzer to the defaults for the WLAN Standards preset type. To restore the analyzer to a preset file, use the command
Category: SYSTem

title: ACTion (SYSTem)
body:

Description: Sets or queries the preset action for the Standards preset type.
Category: SYSTem

title: USER (SYSTem)
body:

Description: Restores the analyzer to the defaults for the user preset type.

title: ACTion (SYSTem)
body:

Description: Sets or queries the preset action for the user preset type.

title: SELected (SYSTem)
body:

Description: Sets or queries the presets for the User preset type.

title: STANdard (SYSTem)
body:
Command: SYSTem:PRESet:WLAN:STANdard
Description: Sets or queries the standard and bandwidth presets for the WLAN standards preset type. The first enumeration is the 802.11 standard; the
second enumeration is the bandwidth.
Category: SYSTem

title: TEMPerature (SYSTem)
body:
Command: SYSTem:TEMPerature
Description: Queries the current device temperature.
Category: SYSTem

title: TIME (SYSTem)
body:
Command: SYSTem:TIME
Description: Sets or queries the time (hours, minutes, and seconds). This command is equivalent to the time setting through the Windows Control
Panel.
Category: SYSTem

title: VERSion (SYSTem)
body:
Command: SYSTem:VERSion
Description: Returns the SCPI version number for which the analyzer complies.
2662
Category: SYSTem

title: STANdard (SYSTem)
body:
Command: SYSTem:PRESet:LTE:STANdard
Description: Sets or queries the following: Preset, Test Setup, frame structure channel bandwidth, Base Station category (for LTE ACLR and SEM test
setups), Adjacent
channel type (for LTE ACLR test setup), Retain current Center Frequency setting, and Retain current Reference Level.
Category: SYSTem

title: ENABLE (SYSTem)
body:
Command: SYSTem:SVPC:HSInterface:ENABLE
Description: Sets or queries the status of the HSI interface as enabled or disabled.
Category: SYSTem

title: LOCAlhost (SYSTem)
body:
Command: SYSTem:SVPC:HSInterface:USE:LOCAlhost
Description: Sets or queries whether the HSI interface is connected via the local host.
Category: SYSTem

title: ADDRess (SYSTem)
body:

Description: Sets or queries the IP address of the instrument connected via HSI interface.
Category: SYSTem

title: NUMBer (SYSTem)
body:
Command: SYSTem:SVPC:HSInterface:PORT:NUMBer
Description: Sets or queries the port number of the instrument connected via HSI interface.
Category: SYSTem

title: AM|FM|PM (TRACe)
DETection (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe:{AM|FM|PM}:DETection
Description: Sets or queries the display detector, the method to be used for decimating traces to fit the available horizontal space on screen. The
number of horizontal pixels on screen is generally smaller than that of waveform data points. When actually displayed, the waveform data
is therefore thinned out, according to the number of pixels, for being compressed.
Category: TRACe

title: FREeze (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe:{AM|FM|PM}:FREeze
Description: Determines whether or not to freeze the trace display in the AM/FM/PM view.
Category: TRACe

title: FUNCtion (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe:{AM|FM|PM}:FUNCtion
Description: Sets or queries the trace function in the AM/FM/PM view.
Category: TRACe

title: CONSte (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:BLUEtooth:CONSte
Description: Sets or queries the specified trace to display or hide in the Bluetooth Constellation display.
The parameter <x> is trace 1 or 2.
2724
Category: TRACe

title: FREeze (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:BLUEtooth:CONSte:FREeze
Description: Sets or queries the specified trace to freeze or not to freeze in the Bluetooth Constellation display.
The parameter <x> is trace 1 or 2.
Category: TRACe

title: MODE (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:BLUEtooth:CONSte:MODE
Description: Sets or queries the Bluetooth Constellation trace display mode. The parameter <x> is trace 1 or 2.
Category: TRACe

title: SELect (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:BLUEtooth:CONSte:SELect
Description: Sets or queries the active trace in the Bluetooth Constellation diagram. The parameter <x> is trace 1 or 2.

title: Q (TRACe)
body:

Category: TRACe

title: I (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:BLUEtooth:EDIagram:SELect:I
Description: Selects the I trace in the Bluetooth eye diagram. The query version of this command returns whether the I trace is selected or not.
Category: TRACe

title: Q (TRACe)
body:

title: MODE (TRACe)
body:

title: MODE (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:P25:CONStellation:MODE

title: SELect (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:P25:CONStellation:SELect

2780

title: Q (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:P25:EDIagram:ENABle:Q

title: I (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:P25:EDIagram:SELect:I
Description: This command selects the I trace in the P25 Eye Diagram display. The query version of this command returns whether the I trace is
selected or not.
Category: TRACe

title: Q (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:P25:EDIagram:SELect:Q
Description: This command selects the Q trace in the P25 Eye Diagram display. The query version of this command returns whether the Q trace is
selected or not.
Category: TRACe

title: AVERageCOUNt (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:NOISe:FIGure:AVERageCOUNt
Description: Sets or queries the average counts for the specified trace in the Noise Figure display.
Category: TRACe

title: ENABle (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:NOISe:FIGure:COUNt:ENABle
Description: Sets or queries whether or not the count feature for the specified trace in the Noise Figure display is enabled or disabled.
Category: TRACe

title: DETection (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:NOISe:FIGure:DETection
Description: Sets or queries the trace detection method in the Noise Figure display.
Category: TRACe

title: FREeze (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:NOISe:FIGure:FREeze
Description: Specifies whether or not to freeze (halt updates of) the specified trace in the Noise Figure display.
Category: TRACe

title: FUNCtion (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:NOISe:FIgure:FUNCtion
Description: Sets or queries the trace processing method (function) in the Noise Figure display.
Category: TRACe

title: SELect (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:NOISe:FIGure:SELect
Description: Sets the trace to be shown in the Noise Figure display. The query form of this command returns whether or not the specified trace is set to
show (ON) or not (OFF).

title: SHOW (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:NOISe:FIGure:SHOW
Description: Sets or queries the state (shown or not shown) of the specified trace in the Noise Figure display.
Category: TRACe

title: COUNt (TRACe)
body:

body:

Category: TRACe

title: DETection (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:NOISe:GAIN:DETection
Description: Sets or queries the trace detection method in the Gain display.
Category: TRACe

title: FREeze (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:NOISe:GAIN:FREeze

Category: TRACe

title: FUNCtion (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:NOISe:GAIN:FUNCtion
Description: Sets or queries the trace processing method (function) in the Gain display.
Category: TRACe

title: SELect (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:NOISe:GAIN:SELect
Description: Sets the trace to be shown in the Gain display. The query form of this command returns whether or not the specified trace is set to show
(ON) or not (OFF).

body:
Command: TRACe<x>:NOISe:GAIN:SHOW

Category: TRACe

body:

body:

body:

Description: Sets or queries the trace detection method in the Noise Temperature display.

title: FUNCtion (TRACe)
body:

Description: Sets or queries the trace processing method (function) in the Noise Temperature display.
Category: TRACe

title: SELect (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:NOISe:TEMPerature:SELect
Description: Sets the trace to be shown in the Noise Temperature display. The query form of this command returns whether or not the specified trace is
set to show (On) or not (Off).
Category: TRACe

title: SHOW (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:NOISe:TEMPerature:SHOW

body:
Command: TRACe<x>:NOISe:YFACtor:AVERage:COUNt
Description: Sets or queries the average counts for the specified trace in the Y Factor display.
Category: TRACe

body:
Command: TRACe<x>:NOISe:YFACtor:COUNt:ENABle
Description: Sets or queries whether or not the count feature for the specified trace in the Y Factor display is enabled or disabled.
Category: TRACe

title: DETection (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:NOISe:YFACtor:DETection
Description: Sets or queries the trace detection method in the Y Factor display.

body:
Command: TRACe<x>:NOISE:YFACtor:FREeze
Description: Specifies whether or not to freeze (halt updates of) the specified trace in the Y Factor display.

title: FUNCtion (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:NOISe:YFACtor:FUNCtion
Description: Sets or queries the trace processing method (function) in the Y Factor display.

body:
Command: TRACe<x>:NOISe:YFACtor:SELect
Description: Sets the trace to be shown in the Y Factor display. The query form of this command returns whether or not the specified trace is set to
show (ON) or not (OFF).

body:

Description: Sets or queries the state (shown or not shown) of the specified trace in the Y Factor display.

title: MEASviewy (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:AVTime[:MEASview<y>]
Description: Determines whether or not to show the specified trace in the Amplitude versus Time view.
The parameter <x> = 1 to 4; All traces are valid.
2716
Category: TRACe

title: COUNt (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe:{FSETtling|PSETtling}:AVERage:COUNt
Description: Sets or queries the number of traces to combine. This command is effective when you select AVERage with the s command.
Category: TRACe

title: COUNt (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:AVTime[:MEASview<y>]:AVERage:COUNt
Description: Sets or queries the number of traces to combine. This command is effective when you select AVERage with the
TRACe<x>:AVTime[:MEASview<y>]:FUNCtion command.
The parameter <x> = 1 to 3; Trace 4 (math trace) is invalid.
Category: TRACe

title: RESet (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:AVTime[:MEASview<y>]:AVERage:RESet
Description: Restarts acquisition and display of waveforms for the specified trace. For an Average, Max Hold, or Min Hold trace, it restarts the
sequence, discarding accumulated data and resetting the counter.
The parameter <x> = 1 to 3; Trace 4 (math trace) is invalid.
Category: TRACe

title: COUNt (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:AVTime[:MEASview<y>]:COUNt
Description: Sets or queries how many acquisitions run in the single acquisition mode for the Max or Min Hold trace in the Amplitude versus Time
measurement. This command is effective when TRACe<x>:AVTime[:MEASview<y>]:FUNCtion is set to MAXHold or
MINHold and INITiate:CONTinuous is set to OFF.
The parameter <x> = 1 to 3; Trace 4 (math trace) is invalid.
Category: TRACe

title: ENABle (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:AVTime[:MEASview<y>]:COUNt:ENABle
Description: Determines whether to enable or disable the count for the Max or Min Hold trace in the Amplitude versus Time view. This command is
effective when TRACe<x>:AVTime[:MEASview<y>]:FUNCtion is set to MAXHold or MINHold.
The parameter <x> = 1 to 3; Trace 4 (math trace) is invalid.
Category: TRACe

title: RESet (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:AVTime[:MEASview<y>]:COUNt:RESet
Description: Clears the Max or Min Hold data and counter, and restarts the process for the specified trace in the Amplitude versus Time view. This
command is effective when TRACe<x>:AVTime[:MEASview<y>]:FUNCtion is set to MAXHold or MINHold.
The parameter <x> = 1 to 3; Trace 4 (math trace) is invalid.
Category: TRACe

title: DETection (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:AVTime[:MEASview<y>]:DETection
Description: Sets or queries the display detector (method to be used for decimating traces to fit the available horizontal space on screen). The number
of horizontal pixels on screen is generally smaller than that of waveform data points. When actually displayed, the waveform data is
therefore thinned out, according to the number of pixels, for being compressed.
Category: TRACe

title: FREeze (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:AVTime[:MEASview<y>]:FREeze
Description: Determines whether or not to freeze the display of the specified trace in the Amplitude versus Time view.
The parameter <x> = 1 to 4; All traces are valid.
Category: TRACe

title: FUNCtion (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:AVTime[:MEASview<y>]:FUNCtion
Description: Sets or queries the function for the specified trace in the Amplitude versus Time view.
The parameter <x> = 1 to 3; Trace 4 (math trace) is invalid.
Category: TRACe

title: LEFToperand (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:AVTime[:MEASview<y>]:LEFToperand
Description: Sets or queries the left operand for the math trace (Trace 4) in the Amplitude versus Time view.
The parameter <x> = 4; Only Trace 4 (math trace) is valid.
Category: TRACe

title: RIGHtoperand (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:AVTime[:MEASview<y>]:RIGHtoperand
Description: Sets or queries the right operand for the math trace (Trace 4) in the Amplitude versus Time view.
The parameter <x> = 4; Only Trace 4 (math trace) is valid.
Category: TRACe

title: SELect (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:AVTime[:MEASview<y>]:SELect
Description: Sets or queries the trace whose measurement results are being displayed in the readout on the top and bottom of the view.
The parameter <x> = 1 to 4; All traces are valid.
Category: TRACe

title: FREeze (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:CCDF:FREeze
Description: Determines whether or not to freeze the display of the specified trace (Trace 1 or 2) in the CCDF view.
The parameter <x> = 1 or 2; Trace 3 (Gaussian curve) is invalid.
Category: TRACe

title: SELect (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:CCDF:SELect
Description: Sets or queries the trace whose measurement results are being displayed in the readout on the top of the view. The selected trace is
indicated by the measurement pointer (pink triangle) on the waveform.
The parameter <x> = 1 to 3; All traces are valid.
Category: TRACe

title: SHOW (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:CCDF:SHOW
Description: Determines whether to show or hide the specified trace in the CCDF view. The parameter <x> = 1 to 3; All traces are valid.
2730
Category: TRACe

title: X (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:CCDF:X
Description: Sets or queries the horizontal position of the measurement pointer (pink triangle) to measure the CCDF. Use the TRACe<x>:CCDF:Y?
query to read the value.
The parameter <x> = 1 to 3.
Note: Use the TRACe<x>:CCDF:SHOW command to show the specified trace and the TRACe<x>:CCDF:SELect
command to select the trace before running the TRACe<x>:CCDF:X command.
Category: TRACe

title: Y (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:CCDF:Y
Description: Queries the vertical position (CCDF value) of the measurement pointer (displayed as a pink triangle). Use the TRACe<x>:CCDF:X
command to set the horizontal position of the pointer.
The parameter <x> = 1 to 3.
Note: Use the TRACe<x>:CCDF:SHOW command to show the specified trace and the TRACe<x>:CCDF:SELect
command to select the trace before running the TRACe<x>:CCDF:Y? query.
Category: TRACe

title: MEASviewy (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:CONSte[:MEASview<y>]
Description: Specifies whether or not to show the specified trace (Trace 1 or 2) in the Constellation display.
The parameter <x> = 1 or 2.
The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).
Category: TRACe

title: FREeze (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:CONSte[:MEASview<y>]:FREeze
Description: Specifies whether or not to freeze (halt updates) to the display of the specified trace (Trace 1 or 2) in the Constellation display.
The parameter <x> = 1 or 2.
The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).
Category: TRACe

title: MODE (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:CONSte[:MEASview<y>]:MODE
Description: Sets or queries how to display the constellation trace.
The parameter <x> = 1 or 2.
The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).
Category: TRACe

title: OFFSet (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:CONSte[:MEASview<y>]:Q:OFFSet
Description: Sets the Q offset in the Constellation display to Use shared Pref, Remove Q offset, or Include Q offset.
The parameter <x> = 1 or 2.
The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).
Category: TRACe

title: SELect (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:CONSte[:MEASview<y>]:SELect
Description: Sets or queries the trace to which the Show, Freeze, Content, and Q Offset settings apply.
The parameter <x> = 1 or 2.
The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).
Category: TRACe

title: I (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe:DIQVtime[:MEASview<y>]:ENABle:I
Description: Enables or disables the I trace in the Demod I&Q versus Time measurement.
The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).
Category: TRACe

title: Q (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe:DIQVtime[:MEASview<y>]:ENABle:Q
Description: Enables or disables the Q trace in the Demod I&Q versus Time measurement.
The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).
Category: TRACe

title: MODE (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe:DIQVtime[:MEASview<y>]:MODE
Description: Sets or queries whether to display the Demod I&Q versus Time trace as vectors or symbols (points).
The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).
Category: TRACe

title: I (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe:DIQVtime[:MEASview<y>]:SELect:I
Description: Selects the I trace in the Demod I&Q versus Time. The query version of this command returns whether the I trace is selected or not.
The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).
2666
Category: TRACe

title: Q (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe:DIQVtime[:MEASview<y>]:SELect:Q
Description: Selects the Q trace in the Demod I&Q versus Time. The query version of this command returns whether the Q trace is selected or not.
The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).
Category: TRACe

title: DPX (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:DPX
Description: Determines whether or not to show the specified trace in the DPX, Spectrum view.
The parameter <x> = 1 to 7; All traces are valid. Trace 6 is for the DPXogram trace and Trace 7 is for the Ogram line.
Category: TRACe

title: COUNt (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:DPX:AVERage:COUNt
Description: Sets or queries the number of traces to combine for averaging in the DPX, Spectrum view.
The parameter <x> = 3; Only Trace 3 (average trace) is valid.
Category: TRACe

title: CURVe (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:DPX:COLor:CURVe
Description: Sets or queries how colors are mapped to the signal density in the DPX, Spectrum bitmap display. The mapping can be linear (Curve =
1), or it can be set to concentrate the resolution on the lower level of the range (Curve > 1) or the mapping can be set to show the best
resolution on the upper range of density or hit count (Curve = 0.1 – 0.99). This command is only valid for the Bitmap display.
The parameter <x> = 5; only Trace5 is valid.
Category: TRACe

title: INTensity (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:DPX:COLor:INTensity
Description: Sets or queries the color intensity in the DPX, Spectrum view. The value is common to all traces.
The parameter <x> = 1 to 5; All traces are valid.
Category: TRACe

title: AUTO (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:DPX:COLor:SCALe:AUTO
Description: Automatically adjusts the Max and Min color settings to display the broadest range of colors in the DPX, Spectrum bitmap display. This
command is only valid for the Bitmap display.
The parameter <x> = 5; only Trace5 is valid.
Category: TRACe

title: DETection (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:DPX:DETection
Description: Sets or queries the detection type (POSitive, NEGative, AVERage) for traces 1, 2 and 3 in the DPX, Spectrum view.
Trace 4 (math trace) and Trace 5 (bitmap trace) are invalid choices. Trace 6 is only valid for DPXogram.
Category: TRACe

title: LINE (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:DPX:DGRAM:SELect:LINE
Description: Sets or queries a line from the DPXogram display to send to the DPX, Spectrum display.
Trace 6 is the DPXogram trace. No other traces are allowed.
Category: TRACe

title: PERSistent (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:DPX:DOT:PERSistent
Description: Determines whether to enable or disable the dot persistence for the bitmap trace (Trace 5) in the DPX, Spectrum view.
The parameter <x> = 5; Only Trace 5 (bitmap trace) is valid.
Category: TRACe

title: TYPE (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:DPX:DOT:PERSistent:TYPE
Description: Sets or queries the persistence type for the bitmap trace (Trace 5) in the DPX, Spectrum view.
The parameter <x> = 5; Only Trace 5 (bitmap trace) is valid.
Category: TRACe

title: VARiable (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:DPX:DOT:PERSistent:VARiable
Description: Sets or queries how long data points are displayed. This command is effective when TRACe<x>:DPX:DOT:PERSistent:TYPE
is set to VARiable. This affects the display only.
The parameter <x> = 5; Only Trace 5 (bitmap trace) is valid.
2740
Category: TRACe

title: FREeze (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:DPX:FREeze
Description: Determines whether or not to freeze the display of the specified trace in the DPX, Spectrum view.
The parameter <x> = 1 to 6; All traces are valid. Trace 6 is for the DPXogram trace and Trace 7 is for the Ogram line.
Category: TRACe

title: FUNCtion (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:DPX:FUNCtion
Description: Sets or queries the trace function for the +Peak, -Peak, or Average trace (Trace 1, 2, or 3, respectively) in the DPX, Spectrum view.

The parameter <x> = 1 to 3; Trace 4 (math trace) and Trace 5 (bitmap trace) are invalid.
Category: TRACe

title: LEFToperand (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:DPX:LEFToperand
Description: Sets or queries the left operand for the math trace (Trace 4) in the DPX, Spectrum view.
The parameter <x> = 4; Only Trace 4 (math trace) is valid.
Category: TRACe

title: RIGHtoperand (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:DPX:RIGHtoperand
Description: Sets or queries the right operand for the math trace (Trace 4) in the DPX, Spectrum view.
The parameter <x> = 4; Only Trace 4 (math trace) is valid.
Category: TRACe

title: SELect (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:DPX:SELect
Description: Sets or queries the trace to display the readout at the upper left of the DPX, Spectrum view.
The parameter <x> = 1 to 7; All traces are valid. Trace 6 is for the DPXogram trace and Trace 7 is for the Ogram line.
Category: TRACe

title: I (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe:EDIagram[:MEASview<y>]:ENABle:I
Description: Determines whether to show or hide the I trace in the Eye diagram.
The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).
Category: TRACe

title: Q (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe:EDIagram[:MEASview<y>]:ENABle:Q

The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).

title: I (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe:EDIagram[:MEASview<y>]:SELect:I
Description: Selects the I trace in the Eye diagram. When used as query, it returns whether the I trace is selected or not.
The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).
Category: TRACe

title: Q (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe:EDIagram[:MEASview<y>]:SELect:Q

The parameter <y> = 1 to number of instances of a measurement created or number of channels available in an instrument (Example: 1 to
8 channels for MSO58).

8 channels for MSO58).

body:

body:
Command: TRACe:IQVTime[:MEASview<y>]:COUNt:RESet

title: I (TRACe)
body:

Category: TRACe

title: FUNCtion (TRACe)
body:

body:

title: Q (TRACe)

body:

2692

Value.

body:

body:

Category: TRACe

body:
Command: TRACe<x>:OFDM:MERRor

The parameter <x> = 1 or 2, where 1 is Average and 2 is Value.

title: FREeze (TRACe)
body:

Description: Determines whether or not to freeze the display of the specified trace in the OFDM Magnitude error view.
The parameter <x> = 1 or 2, where 1 is Average and 2 is Value.

body:

body:
Command: TRACe<x>:OFDM:PERRor:FREeze
Description: Determines whether or not to freeze the display of the specified trace in the OFDM Phase error view.
The parameter <x> = 1 or 2, where 1 is Average and 2 is Value.

title: SELect (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:OFDM:PERRor:SELect
Description: Sets or queries the specified trace to display in the OFDM Phase error view. The parameter <x> = 1 or 2, where 1 is Average and 2 is
Value.

Description: Determines whether or not to freeze the display of the specified trace in the OFDM Power view.

title: SELect (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:OFDM:POWer:SELect
Description: Sets or queries the specified trace to display in the OFDM Power view. The parameter <x> = 1 or 2, where 1 is Average and 2 is Value.

title: FREeze (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe:OFDM:FLATness:FREeze
Description: Sets or queries whether to freeze the average trace on the OFDM Spectral Flatness display. When the freeze state is turned on, the trace
stops updating.

Description: Displays or hides the trace on the OFDM Spectral Flatness display.

Description: Sets or queries whether to display the Frequency deviation versus Time trace as vectors or symbols (points).

8 channels for MSO58).

body:
Command: TRACe:PHVTime[:MEASview<y>]
Description: Sets or queries whether or not to show the trace
Category: TRACe

title: COUNt (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe:PHVTime[:MEASview<y>]:AVERage:COUNt
Description: Sets or queries the number of traces to combine for averaging
Category: TRACe

title: COUNt (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe:PHVTime[:MEASview<y>]:COUNt
Description: Sets or queries the count for the Max or Min Hold trace
Category: TRACe

body:
Command: TRACe:PHVTime[:MEASview<y>]:COUNt:ENABle
Description: Sets or queries whether or not to enable the count for Max/Min Hold

body:
Command: TRACe:PHVTime[:MEASview<y>]:COUNt:RESet

title: DETection (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:PHVTime[:MEASview<y>]:DETection
Description: Sets or queries the display detector (method to be used for decimating traces to fit the available horizontal space on screen). The number
of horizontal pixels on screen is generally smaller than that of waveform data points. When actually displayed, the waveform data is
therefore thinned out, according to the number of pixels, for being compressed.
Category: TRACe

title: FREeze (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:{FSETtling|PSETtling}:FREeze
Description: Determines whether to freeze the specified trace in the Settling Time measurement display.
The parameter <x> = 1 or 2.
Category: TRACe

title: FREeze (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe:PHVTime[:MEASview<y>]:FREeze
Description: Determines whether to freeze the trace display in the Phase versus Time measurement
Category: TRACe

title: FUNCtion (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe:PHVTime[:MEASview<y>]:FUNCtion
Description: Sets or queries the trace function
Category: TRACe

title: SELect (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:{FSETtling|PSETtling}:SELect
Description: Selects the trace in the settling time measurement. The query returns the currently selected trace.
The parameter <x> = 1 or 2.
Category: TRACe

title: SHOW (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:{FSETtling|PSETtling}:SHOW
Description: Determines whether to show or hide the specified trace in the settling time view. The parameter <x> = 1 or 2.
Category: TRACe

title: ENABle (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe:{FSETtling|PSETtling}:AVERage:ENABle
Description: Sets or queries the number of data points for smoothing the trace
Category: TRACe

title: SELect (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:PNOise:SELect
Description: Selects the trace in the phase noise measurement. The query returns the currently selected trace.
The parameter <x> = 1 and 2.
Category: TRACe

title: SHOW (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:PNOise:SHOW
Description: Shows or hides the specified trace in the phase noise view. The parameter <x> = 1 and 2.
Category: TRACe

title: COUNt (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:PNOise:SMOothing:COUNt
Description: Sets or queries the number of data points to take the moving average for smoothing the trace. This command is effective when
TRACe<x>:PNOise: SMOothing:ENABle is set to ON.
The parameter <x> = 1 and 2.
Category: TRACe

title: ENABle (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:PNOise:SMOothing:ENABle
Description: Determines whether to enable or disable smoothing the specified trace in the phase noise view.
The parameter <x> = 1 and 2.
Category: TRACe

title: RESet (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:PNOise:SMOothing:RESet
Description: Restarts the smoothing process, discarding accumulated data and resetting the counter. This command is effective when
TRACe<x>:PNOise:SMOothing: ENABle is set to ON.
2786
The parameter <x> = 1 and 2.
Category: TRACe

title: CURRent (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe:{FSETtling|PSETtling}:AVERage:COUNt:CURRent
Description: Queries the current running average count value. The returned value is valid (non-zero) only while the instrument is acquiring and
averaging a signal. Use the TRACe:{FSETtling|PSETtling}:AVERage:COUNt command to determine the setting for
Average.
Category: TRACe

title: RESet (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe:{FSETtling|PSETtling}:RESet
Description: If trace averaging is enabled, this command resets the current averaged trace to 0.
Category: TRACe

title: COUNt (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe:{FSETtling|PSETtling}:SMOothing:COUNt
Description: Sets or queries the number of data points to take the moving average for smoothing the traces. This command applies to both Trace 1 and
Trace 2.
Category: TRACe

title: ENABle (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe:{FSETtling|PSETtling}:SMOothing:ENABle
Description: Determines whether to enable or disable smoothing the trace(s).
Category: TRACe

title: COUNt (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe:SEM:COUNt
Description: Sets or queries how many acquisitions run in the single acquisition mode for multi-trace functions (Max Hold and Average) in the Spectral
Emissions Mask measurement. This command is effective when TRACe:SEM:FUNCtion is set to MAXHold or AVERage and
INITiate:CONTinuous is set to OFF.
Category: TRACe

title: RESet (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe:SEM:COUNt:RESet
Description: Clears the multi-function (Max Hold or Average) data and counter and restarts the process in the Spectral Emissions Mask view. This
command is effective when TRACe:SEM:FUNCtion is set to MAXHold or AVERage.
Category: TRACe

title: FUNCtion (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe:SEM:FUNCtion
Description: Sets or queries the trace function in the Spectral Emissions Mask view.
Category: TRACe

title: DETection (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe:SGRam[:MEAview<y>]:DETection
Description: Sets or queries the display detector (method to be used for decimating traces to fit the available horizontal space on screen). The number
of horizontal pixels on screen is generally smaller than that of waveform data points. When actually displayed, the waveform data is
therefore thinned out, according to the number of pixels, for being compressed.
Category: TRACe

title: FREeze (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe:SGRam[:MEAview<y>]:FREeze
Description: Sets or queries whether or not to freeze the spectrogram display
Category: TRACe

title: FUNCtion (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe:SGRam[:MEAview<y>]:FUNCtion
Description: Note: This function is no longer available in software versions 2.3.0159 and later. Sending
this command selects the correct. SENSe:SGRam[:MEASview<y>]:TIME[:SCALe]:MODe and
TRACe:SGRam[:MEASview<y>]:DETection to achieve the same result as with previous software versions. The
query will return the equivalent trace function based on the MODe and DETection settings.
Sets or queries the trace function for the specified trace in the spectrogram.
Category: TRACe

title: TIME (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe:SGRam[:MEAview<y>]:FUNCtion:TIME
Description: Note: This command has a new function in software versions 2.3.159 and
above. Sets the Spectrum Monitor mode values for Time per update. Sending
this command sets SENSe:SGRam[:MEASview<y>]:TIME:SCALe:PER:UPDate:MINutes and
SENSe:SGRam[:MEASview<y>]:WATerfall:DIRection based on the value sent in. The query form returns
the number of minutes based on the new settings values.
Category: TRACe

title: LINE (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe:SGRam[:MEAview<y>]:SELect:LINE
Description: Sets or queries the line number to send to the spectrum display.
Category: TRACe

title: MEAviewy (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:SPECtrum[:MEAview<y>]
Description: Sets or queries the Reference level of measurement source for Q data type.
Category: TRACe

title: COUNt (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:SPECtrum[:MEAview<y>]:AVERage:COUNt
Description: Sets or queries the number of traces to combine. This command is effective when
TRACe<x>:SPECtrum[:MEASview<y>]:FUNCtion is AVERage, MAXHold or MINHold.
The parameter <x> = 1 to 3; Trace 4 (math trace) and Trace 5 (spectrogram) are invalid.
Category: TRACe

title: RESet (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:SPECtrum[:MEAview<y>]:AVERage:RESet
Description: Clears average data and counter, and restarts the average process for the specified trace in the Spectrum view. This command is effective
when TRACe<x>:SPECtrum[:MEASview<y>]:FUNCtion is set to AVERage, MAXHold or MINHold.
The parameter <x> = 1 to 3; Trace 4 (math trace) and Trace 5 (spectrogram) are invalid.
Category: TRACe

title: COUNt (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:SPECtrum[:MEAview<y>]:COUNt
Description: Sets or queries how many acquisitions run in the single acquisition mode for the Max or Min Hold trace in the Spectrum measurement.
This command is effective when TRACe<x>:SPECtrum[:MEASview<y>]:FUNCtion is set to MAXHold or MINHold and
INITiate:CONTinuous is set to OFF.
The parameter <x> = 1 to 3; Trace 4 (math trace) and Trace 5 (spectrogram) are invalid.
Category: TRACe

title: ENABle (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:SPECtrum[:MEAview<y>]:COUNt:ENABle
Description: Determines whether to enable or disable the count for the Max or Min Hold trace in the Spectrum view. This command is effective when
TRACe<x>:SPECtrum[:MEASview<y>]:FUNCtion is set to MAXHold or MINHold.
The parameter <x> = 1 to 3; Trace 4 (math trace) and Trace 5 (spectrogram) are invalid.
Category: TRACe

title: RESet (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:SPECtrum[:MEAview<y>]:COUNt:RESet
Description: Clears the Max or Min Hold data and counter, and restarts the process for the specified trace in the Spectrum view. This command is
effective when TRACe<x>:SPECtrum[:MEASview<y>]:FUNCtion is set to MAXHold or MINHold.
The parameter <x> = 1 to 3; Trace 4 (math trace) and Trace 5 (spectrogram) are invalid.
Category: TRACe

title: DETection (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:SPECtrum[:MEAview<y>]:DETection
Description: Sets or queries the display detector (method to be used for decimating traces to fit the available horizontal space on screen). The number
of horizontal pixels on screen is generally smaller than that of waveform data points. When actually displayed, the waveform data is
therefore thinned out, according to the number of pixels, for being compressed.
The parameter <x> = 1 to 3; Trace 4 (math trace) and Trace 5 (spectrogram) are invalid.
Category: TRACe

title: FREeze (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:SPECtrum[:MEAview<y>]:FREeze
Description: Sets or queries whether or not to freeze the display of the trace
Category: TRACe

title: FUNCtion (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:SPECtrum[:MEAview<y>]:FUNCtion
Description: Sets or queries the function for the specified trace in the Spectrum view.
The parameter <x> = 1 to 3; Trace 4 (math trace) and Trace 5 (spectrogram) are invalid.
Category: TRACe

title: LEFToperand (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:SPECtrum[:MEAview<y>]:LEFToperand
Description: Sets or queries the left operand for the math trace (Trace 4) in the Spectrum view. The parameter <x> = 4; Only Trace 4 (math trace) is
valid.
Category: TRACe

title: RIGHtoperand (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:SPECtrum[:MEAview<y>]:RIGHtoperand
Description: Sets or queries the right operand for the math trace (Trace 4) in the Spectrum view. The parameter <x> = 4; Only Trace 4 (math trace) is
valid.
Category: TRACe

title: SELect (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:SPECtrum[:MEAview<y>]:SELect
Description: Sets or queries the trace to display the readout at the upper left of the Spectrum view.
The parameter <x> = 1 to 5; All traces are valid.
Note: TRACe5 (spectrogram) is valid when the spectrum and spectrogram measurements are running.
Category: TRACe

title: COUNt (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe:SPURious:COUNt
Description: Sets or queries how many acquisitions run in the single acquisition mode for multi-trace functions (Max Hold and Average) in the
Spurious measurement. This command is effective when TRACe:SPURious:FUNCtion is set to MAXHold or AVERage and
INITiate:CONTinuous is set to OFF.
Category: TRACe

title: ENABle (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe:SPURious:COUNt:ENABle
Description: Determines whether to enable or disable the count for multi-trace functions (Max Hold and Average) in the Spurious view. This command is
effective when TRACe:SPURious:FUNCtion is set to MAXHold or AVERage.
Category: TRACe

title: RESet (TRACe)
body:

when TRACe:SPURious:FUNCtion is set to MAXHold or AVERage.

title: FREeze (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe:SPURious:FREeze

Category: TRACe

title: FUNCtion (TRACe)
body:

Description: Sets or queries the trace function in the Spurious view.
Category: TRACe

title: MEASviewy (TRACe)
body:

body:

title: COUNt (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe1:TOVerview[:MEASview<y>]:COUNt
Description: Enables or queries the count set for the specified trace. Only Trace1 is valid.
Category: TRACe

title: ENABle (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe1:TOVerview[:MEASview<y>]:COUNt:ENABle
Description: Enables or queries the Average count for the specified trace. Only Trace1 is valid.
Category: TRACe

title: RESet (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe1:TOVerview[:MEASview<y>]:COUNt:RESet
Description: Resets the waveform count for the specified trace. Only Trace1 is valid.
Category: TRACe

title: DETection (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe1:TOVerview[:MEASview<y>]:DETection
Description: Enables or queries the type of detection for the specified trace. Only Trace1 is valid.

title: FREeze (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe1:TOVerview[:MEASview<y>]:FREeze

title: FUNCTion (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe1:TOVerview[:MEASview<y>]:FUNCTion
Description: Enables or queries the selected Function for the specified trace. Only Trace1 is valid.
Category: TRACe

title: WLANgroup (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe:WLANgroup
Description: Wireless LAN measurements
Category: TRACe

title: FREeze (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe:WLAN:FLATness:FREeze
Description: Sets or queries whether to freeze the average trace on the WLAN Spectral Flatness display. When the freeze state is turned on, the
selected trace stops updating.
2710
Note: WLAN Spectral Flatness is only available for OFDM (non-802.11b) signals.
Category: TRACe

title: SHOW (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe:WLAN:FLATness:SHOW
Description: Displays or hides the trace on the WLAN Spectral Flatness display.
Note: WLAN Spectral Flatness is only available for OFDM (non-802.11b) signals.
Category: TRACe

title: CONSte (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:WLAN:CONSte
Description: Turns the selected trace on or off in the constellation plot display, or queries the enabled status. < x> is accepted by the command but
ignored as there is only a single WLAN constellation trace.
Category: TRACe

title: FREeze (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:WLAN:CONSte:FREeze
Description: When the freeze state is turned on, the selected trace stops updating in the constellation plot. <x> is accepted by the command but
ignored as there is only a single WLAN constellation trace.
Category: TRACe

title: SELect (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:WLAN:CRESponse:SELect
Description: Sets or queries which WLAN Channel Response trace is the active trace.TRACe1 is Magnitude, TRACe2 is Phase.
Category: TRACe

title: FREeze (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:WLAN:EVM:FREeze
Description: When the freeze state is turned on, the selected trace stops updating. TRACe1 is Averages. TRACe2 is Points.
Category: TRACe

title: SHOW (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:WLAN:EVM[:SHOW]
Description: Displays or hides the selected trace. TRACe1 is Averages.TRACe2 is Points.
Note: Use this command only with multicarrier standards and not singlecarrier (802.11b).
Category: TRACe

title: FREeze (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:WLAN:MERRor:FREeze
Description: When the freeze state is turned on, the selected trace stops updating. TRACe1 is Averages. TRACe2 is Points.
2804
Category: TRACe

title: SHOW (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:WLAN:MERRor[:SHOW]
Description: Displays or hides the selected trace. TRACe1 is Averages. TRACe2 is Points.
Note: Use this command only with multicarrier standards and not singlecarrier (802.11b).
Category: TRACe

title: FREeze (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:WLAN:PERRor:FREeze
Description: When the freeze state is turned on, the selected trace stops updating. TRACe1 is Averages. TRACe2 is Points.
Category: TRACe

title: SHOW (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:WLAN:PERRor[:SHOW]
Description: Displays or hides the selected trace. TRACe1 is Averages. TRACe2 is Points.
Note: Use this command only with multicarrier standards and not singlecarrier (802.11b).
Category: TRACe

title: COUNt (TRACe)
body:

ACLR measurement. This command is effective when TRACe<x>: LTE:ACLR:FUNCtion is set to MAXHold or AVERage
and INITiate:CONTinuous is set to OFF.
Category: TRACe

title: RESet (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:LTE:ACLR:COUNt:RESet
Description: Clears the multifunction (Max Hold or Average) data and counter and restarts the process in the LTE ACLR display. This command is
effective when TRACe<x>:LTE:ACLR:FUNCtion is set to MAXHold or AVERage.
Category: TRACe

title: FUNCtion (TRACe)
body:
Command: TRACe<x>:LTE:ACLR:FUNCtion
Description: Sets or queries the trace function in the LTE ACLR display.
Category: TRACe

body:
Command: TRACe<x>:LTE:CONSte:SYNChronization:SEQUence:STATe

specified trace in the Trace tab of the settings control panel of the LTE Constellation display.

title: MODE (TRIGger)
body:

acquisitions worth of span.
Category: TRIGger

body:
Command: TRIGger:DPX:SHOW:FRAMes
Description: Determines when to show frames in the swept acquisition mode. Show frames continuously when ON or show only triggered frames when
OFF.
Category: TRIGger

title: ALL (TRIGger)
body:

Description: Sets or queries the selections of the DPX Prefs control panel tab page.
2808
Category: TRIGger

title: SLOPe (TRIGger)
body:

Description: Sets or queries the signal slope of the External input to determine a trigger event.
Category: TRIGger

title: LEVel (TRIGger)
body:
Command: TRIGger:EVENt:INPut:LEVel
Description: Sets or queries the power level that the signal must pass through to cause a trigger event when the trigger type is set to Power.
Category: TRIGger

title: SLOPe (TRIGger)
body:
Command: TRIGger:EVENt:INPut:SLOPe
Description: Sets or queries the signal slope of the RF Input to determine a trigger event.
Category: TRIGger

title: TYPE (TRIGger)
body:
Command: TRIGger:EVENt:INPut:TYPE
Description: Sets or queries the type of trigger. (Power is the only available setting.)
Category: TRIGger

title: SOURce (TRIGger)
body:
Command: TRIGger:EVENt:SOURce
Description: Sets or queries the source of the signal to be monitored for a trigger event.
Category: TRIGger

title: NEW (TRIGger)
body:
Command: TRIGger:MASK:NEW
Description: Loads a new frequency mask.
Category: TRIGger

title: AUTO (TRIGger)
body:
Command: TRIGger:MASK:NEW:AUTO
Description: Draws a new frequency mask automatically based on a reference trace.
Category: TRIGger

title: OPEN (TRIGger)
body:
Command: TRIGger:MASK:OPEN
Description: Opens a trigger mask with a specified file. To save a trigger mask, use the TRIGger:MASK:SAVE command.
Category: TRIGger

title: SAVE (TRIGger)
body:
Command: TRIGger:MASK:SAVE
Description: Saves the current trigger mask to a specified file. To open the trigger mask, use the TRIGger:MASK:OPEN command.
Category: TRIGger

title: MAXimum (TRIGger)
body:
Command: TRIGger:SAVE:COUNt:MAXimum
Description: Sets or queries the number of times a Save on Trigger action stores a file. After this limit is reached, no more files are saved. The
instrument will continue to run, but no additional files are saved when a trigger event occurs.
Category: TRIGger

title: STATe (TRIGger)
body:
Command: TRIGger:SAVE:COUNt[:STATe]
Description: Sets or queries the Maximum total number of files feature. When enabled and the set limit is reached, no more files are saved. The
instrument will continue to run, but no additional files are saved when a trigger event occurs.
Category: TRIGger

title: FORMat (TRIGger)
body:
Command: TRIGger:SAVE:DATA:FORMat
Description: Sets or queries the file format used when saving the acquisition data on a trigger event.
Category: TRIGger

title: STATe (TRIGger)
body:
Command: TRIGger:SAVE:DATA[:STATe]
Description: Sets or queries to save data when a trigger event occurs.
Category: TRIGger

title: FORMat (TRIGger)
body:
Command: TRIGger:SAVE:PICTure:FORMat
Description: Sets or queries the file format used when saving a picture of the display when a trigger event occurs.
Category: TRIGger

title: STATe (TRIGger)
body:
Command: TRIGger:SAVE:PICTure[:STATe]
Description: Sets or queries the save picture on trigger setting. This enables saving a picture of the display when a trigger event occurs.
2816
Category: TRIGger

title: HOLDoff (TRIGger)
body:
Command: TRIGger[:SEQuence]:ADVanced:HOLDoff

period.

body:

Description: Enables or queries the status of the Advanced Trigger Holdoff function.

title: MODE (TRIGger)
body:

Description: Sets or enables the analyzer to wait for a trigger for each acquisition in a swept spectrum or other measurement requiring multiple
acquisitions worth of span.

body:
Command: TRIGger[:SEQuence]:ADVanced:SWEPt:SEGMent:ENABle

requiring multiple acquisitions worth of span.

body:

body:

body:

acquisition.

body:

body:

body:

body:

Description: Sets or queries the positive or negative logic for the gated trigger input on the rear panel.

body:
Command: TRIGger[:SEQuence]:EVENt:INPut:DDENsity:AMPLitude
Description: Sets or queries the center vertical level for the density trigger region of the RF input DPX bitmap display.
Category: TRIGger

body:

defined for the DPX bitmap display.

body:

Density must be higher or lower (specified by the Density selection) than this value to define a trigger event.

body:

Description: Sets or queries whether a density value higher or lower than the THReshold value defines a trigger event for the density trigger region of
the DPX bitmap display.

title: LEVel (TRIGger)
body:
Command: TRIGger[:SEQuence]:EVENt:INPut:FEDGe:LEVel
Description: Sets or queries the trigger level at the frequency edge input.

body:

title: SLOPe (TRIGger)
body:
Command: TRIGger[:SEQuence]:EVENt:INPut:FEDGe:SLOPe
Description: Sets or queries the trigger slope of the frequency edge input.
Category: TRIGger

title: LEVel (TRIGger)
body:
Command: TRIGger[:SEQuence]:EVENt:INPut:FEDGe:THReshold:LEVel
Description: Set or queries the threshold level in the DPX measurement.
2828

body:
Command: TRIGger[:SEQuence]:EVENt:INPut:FEDGe:THReshold:STATe

title: LEVel (TRIGger)

body:

body:

TRIGger[:SEQuence]:EVENt:INPut:TDBWidth: STATe is ON.

body:

Description: Queries the actual time-domain bandwidth for the RF input power trigger.

title: STATe (TRIGger)
body:
Command: TRIGger[:SEQuence]:EVENt:INPut:TDBWidth:STATe
Description: Determines whether to set the time-domain bandwidth automatically or manually for the RF input power trigger.
Category: TRIGger

title: TYPE (TRIGger)
body:
Command: TRIGger[:SEQuence]:EVENt:INPut:TYPE
Description: Sets or queries the type of trigger. (Power is the only available setting.)

body:

Description: Sets or queries the source of the signal to be monitored for a trigger event.
Category: TRIGger

when
TRIGger[:SEQuence]:TIME:QUALified:TIME<x> is set to On (the trigger mode is Triggered).

body:

Description: Causes a trigger immediately, skipping the event detection and delay. This command is valid when
TRIGger[:SEQuence]:TIME:QUALified:TIME<x> is set to On (the trigger mode is Triggered).

title: STATus (TRIGger)
body:
Command: TRIGger[:SEQuence]:STATus
Description: Sets or queries the trigger mode (Free Run or Triggered).

title: STATus (TRIGger)
body:

Description: Sets or queries the trigger mode setting.
Category: TRIGger

title: DELay (TRIGger)
body:

Description: Sets or queries the trigger delay time which occurs after recognizing an event but before actually declaring the trigger.
Category: TRIGger

body:

disables the Auto setting.

body:

Description: Sets or queries the Time1 and Time2 parameters for time qualified triggering. Time1 is the shorter or first time, and Time2 is the second or
later time for the qualifying period. Each TIME<x> must be set with a separate command.
Category: TRIGger

Category: TRIGger

title: SETTings (TRIGger)
body:

body:

body:

body:

title: TYPE (TRIGger)
body:
